title: 1Password Service Account Token
description: Discovered a 1Password Service Account Token, which grants unauthorized programmatic access to vaults and risks a full-scale exposure of all stored company secrets.
helpText: A 1Password Service Account Token provides automated, programmatic access to specific vaults. If leaked, an attacker can bypass biometric and two-factor authentication to export all stored credentials, secret notes, and API keys. This allows for a massive 'lateral movement' attack, where the thief uses these stolen secrets to breach your other connected services and infrastructure.

title: 1Password Secret Key
description: Discovered a 1Password Secret Key, which provides the critical encryption component needed to decrypt and hijack a user's entire password vault.
helpText: A 1Password Secret Key is a unique, 34-character string required to encrypt and decrypt your local data. If leaked alongside account credentials, an attacker can reconstruct your account on a new device, granting them full access to every vault you own. Unlike a password, this key cannot be 'guessed,' but once compromised, it provides the fundamental layer for a total account takeover.

title: Abbysale API Key
description: Identified a potential Abbysale API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Abbysale services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised Abbysale API Key could allow an attacker to use the account's subscription to generate content, potentially incurring costs or using up quotas. It may also grant access to the user's templates and previously generated content, leading to a leak of proprietary (but not typically PII) data.

title: AbuseIPDB API Key
description: Identified a potential AbuseIPDB API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to AbuseIPDB services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An AbuseIPDB API Key allows users to report malicious IPs and check IP reputations. A leaked key could be used to submit false reports or perform lookups, but it does not grant access to sensitive data or systems. The primary impact is potential disruption to the user's reporting reputation or hitting API rate limits.

title: Accuweather API Key
description: Identified a potential Accuweather API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to weather data and services.
helpText: An Accuweather API Key provides access to weather data. While it does not expose sensitive user data or critical systems, a leaked key tied to a paid plan could be abused by an attacker to make a large number of requests. This could lead to financial costs from exhausting the API call quota or incurring overage charges, and result in a denial of service for the weather-related features of the legitimate application.

title: AdafruitIO API Key
description: Identified a potential AdafruitIO API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to AdafruitIO services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An Adafruit IO API Key provides full programmatic access to a user's account. An attacker with this key could read all historical and real-time data from connected IoT devices, which may include sensitive information like location data, camera feeds, or home environmental data. More importantly, the key grants write access, enabling an attacker to send commands to control physical devices, such as unlocking smart locks, disabling security systems, or manipulating machinery. This potential for direct physical impact and severe privacy invasion makes the secret critical.

title: Adzuna API Key
description: Identified a potential Adzuna API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Adzuna job search services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised Adzuna API Key could be used by an attacker to make excessive requests against the service. If the key is linked to a paid plan, this could result in unexpected financial costs for the owner. It could also lead to denial of service by exhausting the API request quota. The key does not provide access to sensitive user data or critical systems, making the primary impact financial.

title: Adzuna Client ID
description: Detected an Adzuna Client ID, posing a risk of compromised Adzuna integrations and data breaches.
helpText: The Adzuna API uses an Application ID (Client ID) and a secret Application Key for authentication. The Client ID is a public identifier used to identify the application making the request. On its own, it does not grant any access to the API or sensitive data. An attacker would also need the corresponding secret Application Key to perform authenticated actions, making the impact of a leaked Client ID minimal.

title: Agora API Key
description: Identified a potential Agora API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Agora services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An Agora API Key (Customer ID and Secret) grants access to Agora's RESTful APIs. An attacker could abuse this key to disrupt services by kicking or banning users from live channels. More critically, they could manage cloud recordings, potentially accessing sensitive audio or video data from past sessions. The key could also be used to incur significant financial costs by initiating billable services. The combination of service disruption, potential PII exfiltration, and financial abuse warrants a high severity rating.

title: Aha! API Token
description: Detected an Aha! API token, risking unauthorized access to Aha! services and sensitive project management data.
helpText: An Aha! API token provides full programmatic access to an Aha! account, equivalent to the permissions of the user who generated it. An attacker could use this token to read, modify, or delete highly sensitive business information, including product roadmaps, strategic plans, feature backlogs, and competitive analysis. This could lead to corporate espionage, sabotage of product development, and leakage of intellectual property, representing a critical threat to the business.

title: Aha! Sensitive URL
description: Detected a potential Aha! sensitive URL, which could expose instance information or lead to unauthorized access.
helpText: Aha! sensitive URLs provide unauthenticated, read-only access to a specific presentation, such as a product roadmap, feature list, or strategic plan. While this access is limited and does not allow for data modification or access to the entire account, the information contained within these presentations is often highly confidential. An attacker with this URL could exfiltrate sensitive business strategy, future product plans, and intellectual property, potentially giving a competitor a significant advantage. The impact of this sensitive data exposure is high.

title: Airbrake API Key
description: Identified a potential Airbrake API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Airbrake project deployments and sensitive data.
helpText: An Airbrake Project Key is used to send error data from an application to the Airbrake service. An attacker with this key can inject a high volume of fake or malicious error reports. This can obscure real production errors, trigger alert fatigue, and disrupt incident response, potentially allowing critical issues or security breaches to go unnoticed. While the key does not grant read access to existing error data, the ability to blind a team to real-time operational and security events represents a significant risk to application stability and security.

title: Airbrake Access ID
description: Detected a potential Airbrake Project ID, which could lead to unauthorized access to project deployments and data.
helpText: A leaked Airbrake Project ID, when paired with its Project Key, allows an attacker to send arbitrary error reports to the associated project. This can be used to flood the system with fake errors, obscuring real issues and making debugging difficult (a form of denial of service against the monitoring tool). It may also exhaust error quotas on a paid plan, incurring minor costs. However, these credentials do not grant access to read existing (and potentially sensitive) error data, nor do they provide access to the application's infrastructure or user data.

title: AirVisual Access Token
description: Identified a potential AirVisual Access Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to AirVisual services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An exposed AirVisual Access Token allows an attacker to make requests on behalf of the legitimate user. This can lead to the exhaustion of API call quotas, causing a denial of service for the application relying on this data. If the key is associated with a paid plan, unauthorized use could also result in direct financial costs for the key's owner.

title: Aiven API Key
description: Detected an Aiven API Key, potentially compromising access to Aiven services and sensitive data.
helpText: An Aiven API Key grants programmatic control over projects and services within an Aiven account. An attacker could use this key to create, modify, or delete critical data infrastructure such as production databases and message queues, leading to data loss and service outages. They could also retrieve service connection details, including credentials, to exfiltrate sensitive data or incur significant financial costs by provisioning new resources. The key provides direct administrative access to core data systems, making its exposure critical.

title: Alchemy API Key
description: Detected an Alchemy API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Alchemy services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An Alchemy API Key is used to interact with blockchain networks. If exposed, an attacker could cause a Denial of Service for the application by exhausting API rate limits, leading to significant service disruption. They could also incur substantial financial costs for the key's owner by making a high volume of requests. While the key does not directly expose wallet private keys or funds, it is critical for a dApp's core functionality and can be abused for major operational and financial harm.

title: Alegra Access ID
description: Detected a potential Alegra Access ID, which could lead to unauthorized access to Alegra services and sensitive data.
helpText: An Alegra Access ID, when paired with its corresponding API token, provides full programmatic access to a company's entire accounting system. An attacker could read, create, modify, and delete highly sensitive financial data, including invoices, client details, bank account information, and sales reports. This level of access could lead to direct financial fraud, theft of sensitive business data, and severe operational disruption, warranting a critical severity.

title: Alegra API Key
description: Detected an Alegra API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Alegra services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An Alegra API Key provides full programmatic access to a company's accounting system. An attacker with this key could read, create, modify, and delete invoices, customer and supplier contacts (PII), inventory, and bank account details. This level of access could be used to commit financial fraud, exfiltrate sensitive business and customer data, and severely disrupt core business operations, making it a critical-risk secret.

title: Aletheia API Key
description: Identified a potential Aletheia API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Aletheia services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An Aletheia API Key allows users to scrape public data from social media platforms. A leaked key could be abused by an attacker to make requests on behalf of the legitimate user, leading to financial loss by consuming the paid API quota or incurring overage charges. The key does not grant access to sensitive private data, making the impact primarily financial.

title: AlienVault API Key
description: Identified an AlienVault API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to AlienVault services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An AlienVault OTX API Key provides programmatic access to the threat intelligence platform. An attacker with this key could read an organization's private threat intelligence, including IOCs and threat 'pulses' they are tracking. This would expose the organization's security posture and ongoing investigations. The attacker could also manipulate or delete this data, blinding the security team to active threats or misleading them with false information, which could directly facilitate a security breach. This level of access to sensitive security data warrants a high severity rating.

title: Allsports API Key
description: Identified a potential Allsports API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Allsports services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An exposed Allsports API Key allows an attacker to make requests on behalf of the subscribed user. This could lead to the exhaustion of the plan's request quota, causing a denial of service for the legitimate application and potentially incurring financial costs from overage charges. The key does not grant access to sensitive data or critical systems.

title: Amadeus Client ID
description: Detected a potential Amadeus Client ID, posing a risk of unauthorized access to Amadeus services and compromised integrations.
helpText: An Amadeus Client ID is a public identifier used in the OAuth 2.0 authentication flow. It must be paired with a confidential Client Secret to obtain an access token. On its own, the Client ID cannot be used by an attacker to authenticate or perform any actions. It grants no access to data or functionality, making its exposure a low-risk event.

title: Amadeus Client Secret
description: Discovered a potential Amadeus Client Secret, which, if exposed, could allow unauthorized access to Amadeus APIs and sensitive data.
helpText: An Amadeus Client Secret, when combined with a Client ID, is used to obtain access tokens for the Amadeus API. A compromised secret could allow an attacker to perform sensitive actions such as booking flights or hotels, which could lead to significant direct financial loss. Furthermore, depending on the API permissions, an attacker could potentially access highly sensitive Personally Identifiable Information (PII) from passenger booking records (PNRs), leading to a major data breach. The potential for both substantial financial fraud and sensitive data exfiltration makes this secret's exposure critical.

title: Amplitude API Key
description: Detected an Amplitude API Key, potentially compromising access to Amplitude services and sensitive data.
helpText: An Amplitude API Key is used to send event data to a specific Amplitude project. If compromised, an attacker could send large volumes of fake or malformed events, corrupting the integrity of the analytics data. This can disrupt business intelligence, skew metrics, and lead to flawed product decisions. While the key does not grant access to read existing sensitive data, the potential to compromise the integrity of business-critical analytics warrants a Medium severity.

title: Anthropic API Key
description: Identified an Anthropic API Key, which may compromise AI assistant integrations and expose sensitive data to unauthorized access.
helpText: An Anthropic API Key grants programmatic access to their powerful AI models like Claude. An attacker with this key could make API calls on behalf of the legitimate user, leading to significant and potentially uncapped financial costs. Furthermore, if the application uses the API to process sensitive data (like PII, source code, or internal documents), this information could be exposed. This combination of significant financial risk and potential for sensitive data exfiltration warrants a high severity rating.

title: ApiFlash Access Key
description: Identified an ApiFlash Access Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to ApiFlash services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An ApiFlash Access Key allows an attacker to make screenshot requests on behalf of the user's account. This can lead to financial loss by exhausting the paid plan's quota or incurring overage charges. More critically, an attacker could use the key to take screenshots of internal, staging, or non-public web applications if they are accessible by ApiFlash's servers. This could lead to the exfiltration of sensitive information, application UIs, or proprietary data, representing a significant information disclosure risk.

title: ApiFlash Sensitive URL
description: Uncovered a potential ApiFlash Sensitive URL, which could lead to unauthorized access to web content or data exposure via the ApiFlash API.
helpText: A compromised Sensitive URL allows an attacker to make screenshot requests billed to the victim's account. This can cause direct financial loss by exhausting paid quotas and incurring overage charges. More critically, an attacker could use the key to take screenshots of internal or authenticated web pages that the application has access to, potentially exfiltrating sensitive data from admin panels, user profiles, or other private systems. This potential for sensitive data exfiltration elevates the severity beyond simple financial abuse.

title: Apify API Token
description: Identified a potential Apify API Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to Apify services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An Apify API Token provides full programmatic access to an Apify account. An attacker could exfiltrate potentially sensitive data scraped and stored in datasets, disrupt services by modifying or deleting actors and schedules, and incur significant financial costs by running jobs on the victim's account. While the scope is limited to the Apify platform, the potential for a severe data breach and direct financial loss makes this a high-severity finding.

title: APILayer API Key
description: Identified an APILayer API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to APILayer services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised APILayer API Key could allow an attacker to make API calls on behalf of the user. This could lead to financial loss by exceeding paid plan quotas or cause a denial-of-service for the application's features that rely on the API by exhausting request limits. The key itself does not provide direct access to sensitive user data or critical systems.

title: APITemplate API Key
description: Detected an APITemplate API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to APITemplate services and data exposure.
helpText: A compromised APITemplate API Key allows an attacker to generate images and PDFs using the victim's account, potentially exhausting quotas and incurring financial costs. It also grants access to manage the user's templates, which could lead to the theft or modification of proprietary designs or sensitive information stored within those templates.

title: Apptivo API Key
description: Identified a potential Apptivo API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Apptivo services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An Apptivo API Key provides programmatic access to a company's core business data, including sensitive customer information (PII), financial records like invoices and estimates, and project details. An attacker with this key could exfiltrate sensitive data, manipulate financial records, disrupt business operations by deleting or altering data, and potentially commit fraud. This level of access to a central business management system constitutes a critical risk.

title: Artsy Client ID
description: Detected a potential Artsy Client ID, posing a risk of compromised Artsy integrations and data breaches.
helpText: An Artsy Client ID is a public identifier used to identify an application to the Artsy API. According to Artsy's own documentation, the Client ID is considered public information. It does not provide any authenticated access on its own and cannot be used to read sensitive data or perform privileged actions without its corresponding Client Secret. Therefore, its exposure represents minimal to no risk.

title: Artsy Client Secret
description: Discovered a potential Artsy Client Secret, which, if exposed, could allow unauthorized Artsy service access and data manipulation.
helpText: An Artsy Client Secret is used with a Client ID to obtain a token for server-to-server API requests. This token primarily provides access to public data, such as information about artworks and artists. A compromised secret does not expose sensitive user data or provide access to modify or purchase items. The primary risk is that an attacker could use the credential to hit API rate limits, potentially causing a temporary denial of service for the application's integration with Artsy.

title: Asana Personal Access Token
description: Uncovered a possible Asana Personal Access Token, risking unauthorized access to Asana projects and sensitive task information.
helpText: An Asana Personal Access Token (PAT) grants programmatic access to the Asana API with the same permissions as the user who created it. An attacker could use this token to read, modify, or delete sensitive project data, tasks, conversations, and attachments. This could lead to the exfiltration of intellectual property, strategic plans, and other confidential business information, as well as significant disruption to team workflows.

title: Asana Access Token
description: Identified an Asana Access Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to Asana accounts and sensitive user data.
helpText: An Asana Access Token allows programmatic access to a user's Asana account. A compromised token could allow an attacker to read, modify, and delete sensitive project data, tasks, conversations, and attachments. This could lead to the exfiltration of confidential business plans, intellectual property, and internal communications, as well as significant disruption to business operations. While the scope is limited to the Asana platform, the potential for sensitive data theft and operational sabotage is high.

title: AssemblyAI API Key
description: Detected an AssemblyAI API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to AssemblyAI services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised AssemblyAI API Key grants full programmatic access to an account's data. An attacker can exfiltrate potentially highly sensitive audio and video transcriptions, which may contain PII, trade secrets, or confidential conversations. The key could also be used to delete existing data, causing permanent data loss, or to incur significant financial costs by processing unauthorized files. This direct access to read and write sensitive data makes the severity HIGH.

title: Atera API Key
description: Identified a potential Atera API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Atera services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An Atera API Key provides extensive programmatic access to manage an entire IT environment. An attacker with this key could retrieve sensitive information about all managed devices and customers, and most critically, execute arbitrary scripts on any managed agent. This allows for remote code execution, enabling an attacker to take over systems, deploy ransomware, exfiltrate data, or cause widespread disruption across all managed infrastructure. The potential for complete system compromise makes this a critical-severity secret.

title: AutoPilot API Key
description: Identified a potential AutoPilot API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to AutoPilot services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An Autopilot API Key grants extensive control over the marketing automation platform. An attacker could use it to exfiltrate the entire contact list, which often contains sensitive Personally Identifiable Information (PII) like names, emails, and phone numbers. The key could also be used to send unauthorized emails or messages to the contact list, leading to phishing attacks, reputational damage, and potential blacklisting of the company's domain.

title: Avaza Personal Access Token
description: Uncovered a possible Avaza Personal Access Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to Avaza services and sensitive data.
helpText: An Avaza Personal Access Token provides programmatic access to a user's account. According to Avaza's API documentation, this allows an attacker to perform actions such as reading/writing customer data (PII), managing projects, and creating or modifying financial documents like invoices and expenses. The ability to access and manipulate sensitive customer and financial data, and disrupt core business operations, constitutes a critical risk.

title: AviationStack Access Key
description: Identified a potential AviationStack Access Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to AviationStack services and sensitive flight data.
helpText: An exposed AviationStack Access Key allows an attacker to query the aviation data API using the victim's account. This does not expose sensitive user data, but if the key is associated with a paid subscription, an attacker could exhaust the API request quota. This could lead to a denial of service for the legitimate application and potentially incur financial costs for the account owner.

title: AWS Access Token
description: Identified a potential AWS Access Token, risking unauthorized cloud resource access and data breaches on AWS platforms.
helpText: An AWS Access Token, paired with its secret key, provides programmatic access to AWS resources. The level of access is determined by the IAM permissions associated with the key. An attacker with a compromised key could potentially read sensitive data from S3, manage or destroy infrastructure like EC2 instances and databases, or incur significant financial costs by provisioning new resources. Due to the potential for complete infrastructure compromise and data exfiltration, the severity is CRITICAL.

title: Amazon Bedrock Long Term API Key
description: Identified a potential Amazon Bedrock Long Term API Key, risking unauthorized access to Amazon Bedrock.
helpText: An Amazon Bedrock LongTerm API Key grants programmatic access to high-performance AI models. If leaked, an attacker can bypass safety guardrails, access sensitive training data, and trigger 'LLMjacking'—using your account to run massive, unauthorized AI workloads that can cost thousands of dollars per hour.

title: Amazon Bedrock Short Term API Key
description: Identified a potential Amazon Bedrock Short Term API Key, risking unauthorized access to Amazon Bedrock.
helpText: An Amazon Bedrock Short Term API Key grants temporary programmatic access to foundation models. While the security risk is lower due to its automatic expiration, a leak still allows an attacker to bypass safety guardrails and incur high AI compute charges for the duration of the key's life.

title: AWS Secret Key
description: Detected a potential AWS Secret Key, risking unauthorized cloud resource access and data breaches on AWS platforms.
helpText: An AWS Secret Key is paired with an Access Key ID to provide programmatic access to AWS resources. The level of access is determined by the IAM policies attached to the key's identity. In a worst-case scenario, this key could belong to an administrator, giving an attacker complete control over the AWS account. This includes the ability to read/write sensitive data (S3, RDS), modify or destroy infrastructure (EC2), and incur massive financial costs. The potential for total compromise of cloud infrastructure and data warrants a critical severity.

title: Axonaut API Key
description: Detected an Axonaut API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Axonaut services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An Axonaut API Key provides programmatic access to a company's entire business management system. An attacker with this key could read, create, update, and delete highly sensitive data, including customer information (PII), invoices, accounting records, and employee data. This could lead to a major data breach, direct financial fraud, and complete disruption of business operations, making its exposure a critical risk.

title: Azure Client ID
description: Detected a pattern that resembles an Azure Client ID, posing a risk of compromised Azure integrations and data breaches.
helpText: An Azure Client ID (also known as an Application ID) is a public identifier for an application registered in Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD). It is not a secret and, by itself, does not grant any access to Azure resources. An attacker would need a corresponding secret (like a client secret or certificate) to authenticate as the application. Therefore, its exposure represents minimal risk.

title: Azure Client Secret
description: Detected an Azure AD Client Secret, posing a risk of compromised Azure integrations and data breaches.
helpText: An Azure Client Secret is a credential used by an application or service principal to authenticate programmatically to Azure services. If compromised, an attacker can use it to obtain an access token and assume the identity and permissions of that service principal. Depending on the assigned permissions, this could grant the attacker extensive control over Azure resources, including the ability to read/write/delete sensitive production data, manage or destroy infrastructure (like VMs and databases), and provision new resources, leading to significant financial costs. The potential for full administrative access to cloud infrastructure makes this secret critical.

title: Azure Tenant ID
description: Detected a potential Azure Tenant ID, which identifies an Azure Active Directory instance and could be used in unauthorized access attempts if combined with other credentials.
helpText: An Azure Tenant ID is a globally unique identifier (GUID) for an Azure Active Directory instance. It is not a secret or a credential, but rather a public identifier used to specify which organization's directory is being targeted for authentication. It does not grant any access to resources or data on its own. An attacker would still need separate, valid credentials to perform any malicious action.

title: Bannerbear API Token
description: Detected a Bannerbear API Token, risking unauthorized access to image and video generation services.
helpText: A Bannerbear API Token grants full read, write, and delete access to a user's projects, including templates, images, and videos. An attacker could exfiltrate potentially sensitive data contained in the generated media (e.g., PII in personalized images), delete all assets causing significant data loss and service disruption, or incur financial costs by exhausting API quotas. This level of access to potentially sensitive user-generated content and the ability to perform destructive actions warrants a HIGH severity.

title: Baremetrics API Token
description: Detected a Baremetrics API Token, potentially risking unauthorized access to Baremetrics services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Baremetrics API Token provides full programmatic access to a company's most sensitive financial metrics and customer data. An attacker could exfiltrate customer lists (including PII), detailed revenue data (MRR, LTV), and other confidential business intelligence. With write permissions, an attacker could also manipulate or delete customers, plans, and subscriptions, leading to catastrophic data corruption and disruption of critical business analytics.

title: Beamer API Key
description: Identified a Beamer API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Beamer services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Beamer API Key allows for programmatic management of posts in a company's changelog and notification center. An attacker with this key could create, modify, or delete public announcements. This could be used to spread misinformation, deface the product's announcements, or launch phishing attacks against the application's user base by publishing posts with malicious links. The potential for direct, malicious communication with end-users presents a significant reputational and security risk.

title: BestTime API Key
description: Identified a BestTime API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to BestTime services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A leaked BestTime API Key can be used to make requests against a user's subscription plan. An attacker could exhaust the monthly API request quota, causing a denial of service for the legitimate application's features that rely on this data. This abuse would result in a minor financial loss for the victim by wasting their paid subscription.

title: BetterStack API Token
description: Detected a BetterStack API Token, potentially risking unauthorized access to monitoring services and sensitive uptime data.
helpText: A BetterStack API Token can grant extensive control over an organization's observability and incident management infrastructure. An attacker with this token could read potentially sensitive information from logs and incident data, delete all monitors to blind the company from outages, trigger false incidents to cause disruption and alert fatigue, or deface public status pages. The ability to severely disrupt critical operational monitoring and exfiltrate sensitive operational data makes this a high-severity secret.

title: BitBar API Key
description: Identified a BitBar API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to BitBar cloud services and sensitive data.
helpText: A BitBar API Key grants programmatic access to a user's testing projects. An attacker could use this key to download proprietary application files (.apk, .ipa) uploaded for testing, exposing intellectual property. They could also access test results, logs, and screenshots, which may contain sensitive data. Additionally, the key could be abused to run unauthorized tests, consuming the account's paid testing minutes and incurring significant financial costs.

title: BitLy Access Token
description: Detected a Bitly Access Token, risking unauthorized access to Bitly services and user data.
helpText: A Bitly Access Token provides full programmatic control over a user's account. An attacker with this token could maliciously edit the destination of existing short links, redirecting users from legitimate content to phishing sites or malware-hosting pages. This could lead to severe reputational damage and compromise of the company's user base. Additionally, the attacker could access sensitive link analytics and create new malicious links under the guise of the compromised account.

title: BitMEX API Key
description: Identified a BitMEX API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to cryptocurrency trading accounts and financial loss.
helpText: A BitMEX API Key allows for programmatic trading on a user's account. Depending on the permissions granted, an attacker could execute unauthorized trades, liquidate positions, or, in the worst-case scenario with withdrawal permissions, steal all cryptocurrency funds from the account. The potential for direct and immediate financial theft makes this a critical secret.

title: BitMEX Secret Key
description: Discovered a potential BitMEX Secret Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to cryptocurrency trading accounts and financial loss.
helpText: A BitMEX Secret Key, paired with its corresponding API Key, provides programmatic access to a user's account. Depending on the permissions granted, an attacker could execute trades, manipulate positions, or, in the worst-case scenario with withdrawal permissions enabled, steal all cryptocurrency funds from the account. The potential for direct and irreversible financial theft makes this secret critical.

title: BlazeMeter Access Token
description: Detected a BlazeMeter Access Token, risking unauthorized access to BlazeMeter services and sensitive data.
helpText: A BlazeMeter API key grants programmatic access to manage tests, workspaces, and users. An attacker could use this key to start costly performance tests, leading to financial loss and service disruption. More importantly, the key provides access to test configurations, scripts, and reports, which can contain sensitive data like test credentials, application endpoints, or performance metrics that reveal vulnerabilities. This constitutes a significant data exfiltration risk and disruption to the development lifecycle.

title: BlitApp API Key
description: Identified a potential BlitApp API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to BlitApp services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised BlitApp API Key would allow an attacker to make requests on behalf of the user, leading to financial loss by consuming the paid quota. An attacker could also potentially access previously captured screenshots by listing and retrieving them via the API. This could lead to a data leak if the service was used on internal or authenticated web pages containing sensitive information.

title: Blocknative API Key
description: Detected a Blocknative API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Blocknative services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised Blocknative API Key could allow an attacker to consume the account's API quota, potentially leading to financial costs through overages on a paid plan. It could also cause a denial-of-service for any application relying on the key for its functionality (e.g., transaction simulation or gas fee estimation). The key does not grant direct access to on-chain funds or sensitive user data, but the potential for service disruption and financial loss makes it a medium severity risk.

title: Blogger API Key
description: Identified a potential Blogger API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Blogger services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Blogger API Key grants access to public data via the Blogger API. It does not provide access to private user data or the ability to modify content, as those actions require OAuth 2.0 authentication. However, an attacker could use the leaked key to make requests against the associated Google Cloud project, potentially exhausting API quotas. This could cause a denial-of-service for the legitimate application and may incur financial costs for the project owner if the usage exceeds the API's free tier.

title: BoostNote Access Token
description: Discovered a BoostNote Access Token, potentially compromising access to BoostNote services and sensitive data.
helpText: A BoostNote Access Token grants programmatic access to a user's or team's knowledge base. An attacker with this token could read, modify, or delete sensitive internal documentation, code snippets, and other proprietary information. This could lead to intellectual property theft, exposure of internal system details, or disruption of team operations, making the potential for sensitive data exfiltration significant.

title: BorgBase Access Token
description: Uncovered a BorgBase Access Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to BorgBase services and data.
helpText: A BorgBase Access Token grants full programmatic access to a user's backup repositories. An attacker with this key can list, create, and, most importantly, permanently delete all backup repositories. While the backups themselves are client-side encrypted and their content cannot be read, the ability to destroy all backup data represents a catastrophic data loss event, making this a critical-level secret.

title: Braintree Client ID
description: Detected a Braintree Client ID, which could lead to unauthorized access to payment services and sensitive financial data.
helpText: A Braintree Client ID is a public identifier for an application using Braintree's OAuth capabilities. It is used to identify the application but does not grant any permissions on its own. An attacker would also need the corresponding Client Secret to authenticate the application. By itself, the Client ID poses a minimal security risk and is often considered public information.

title: Braintree Secret Key
description: Uncovered a possible Braintree Secret Key, potentially compromising payment processing and sensitive financial data.
helpText: A Braintree Secret Key provides full programmatic access to a merchant's payment gateway. An attacker with this key can perform critical actions such as processing fraudulent charges, issuing refunds to their own accounts, and accessing sensitive customer and transaction data. The potential for direct financial theft and exposure of sensitive financial information makes this a critical vulnerability.

title: Brandfetch API Key
description: Identified a Brandfetch API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Brandfetch services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised Brandfetch API Key allows an attacker to make requests against the Brandfetch API on behalf of the key's owner. Since Brandfetch operates on a quota system with paid tiers, an attacker could exhaust the monthly quota, causing a denial of service for the legitimate application, or incur financial costs by making excessive requests on a paid plan. The key does not grant access to sensitive user data, but the potential for financial abuse and service disruption warrants a MEDIUM severity.

title: Browshot API Key
description: Identified a potential Browshot API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Browshot services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised Browshot API Key allows an attacker to make screenshot requests on behalf of the user, potentially incurring financial costs or exhausting the account's quota. Additionally, an attacker could access previously taken screenshots, which may contain sensitive information if the legitimate user was capturing images of internal dashboards, private reports, or authenticated sessions. This potential for financial loss and data leakage makes the severity medium.

title: BscScan API Key
description: Identified a BscScan API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to BscScan services and sensitive blockchain data.
helpText: A BscScan API Key provides programmatic access to public blockchain data. It does not grant access to private keys, user funds, or any sensitive private information. An attacker could use a leaked key to consume the API request quota associated with it, potentially causing a minor denial-of-service for the legitimate application relying on it. The impact is minimal as the key only provides a higher rate limit for accessing publicly available information.

title: Bugsnag API Token
description: Detected a Bugsnag API Token, potentially compromising error tracking and application monitoring services.
helpText: A Bugsnag API Token (often a Project API Key) is used to send error reports from an application to a specific Bugsnag project. A compromised key allows an attacker to send a large volume of fake error reports. This can pollute the monitoring data, making it difficult to identify real issues, trigger excessive notifications, and exhaust the account's event quota, potentially incurring financial costs or causing a denial of service for the error monitoring. The key does not grant access to read existing error data.

title: Buildkite Access Token
description: Detected a Buildkite Access Token, potentially compromising continuous integration and deployment pipelines.
helpText: A Buildkite Access Token provides API access to the CI/CD platform. A compromised token could allow an attacker to read source code, exfiltrate sensitive secrets and credentials used in build jobs, modify pipeline configurations to inject malicious code, or trigger deployments of compromised software. This provides a direct path to compromising the software supply chain and accessing production systems, making its exposure a critical risk.

title: ButterCMS API Key
description: Uncovered a possible ButterCMS API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to content management services and data breaches.
helpText: A ButterCMS API Key could be a Write API Token. If compromised, an attacker could create, read, update, or delete all content within the CMS. This could lead to website defacement, service disruption, or the injection of malicious scripts to attack end-users. While a less sensitive read-only token also exists, the potential for a write token to compromise the integrity of the application's content warrants a high severity rating.

title: Caflou API Token
description: Detected a Caflou API Token, potentially risking unauthorized access to Caflou services and sensitive account data.
helpText: A Caflou API Token provides full programmatic access (read, write, update, delete) to a company's core business data. An attacker with this token could exfiltrate or destroy highly sensitive information, including customer data (CRM), project details, and financial records like invoices and cashflow statements. The ability to manipulate financial records and delete crucial business data poses a direct and severe threat to a company's operations and financial stability, justifying a critical severity.

title: Calendarific API Key
description: Identified a potential Calendarific API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Calendarific services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Calendarific API Key allows users to fetch holiday data. A compromised key could be used by an attacker to make excessive requests, exhausting the API quota. This would cause a denial of service for the legitimate application and could lead to minor financial costs if the key is tied to a paid plan. It does not provide access to sensitive data.

title: Calendly Access Token
description: Discovered a Calendly Access Token, potentially compromising scheduling services and sensitive user data.
helpText: A Calendly Access Token provides programmatic access to a user's account via the API. An attacker with this token could access sensitive information, including the user's personal details (name, email) and a complete list of their scheduled events with attendee information. They could also disrupt the user's schedule by canceling existing meetings or modifying event types. This level of access to sensitive personal and business data constitutes a significant security risk.

title: Campayn API Key
description: Detected a Campayn API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Campayn services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Campayn API Key provides programmatic access to manage contact lists and send email campaigns. An attacker with this key could exfiltrate sensitive user data (PII like names and email addresses) from contact lists, disrupt marketing operations by deleting data, or send phishing or spam emails from the compromised account. This could lead to a significant data breach, reputational damage, and financial loss.

title: Canny API Key
description: Detected a Canny API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Canny services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Canny API Key provides full administrative access to a company's feedback board. An attacker could use this key to read all user feedback, which may contain sensitive information like unpatched security vulnerabilities, strategic product plans, or user PII. They could also manipulate the data by creating, deleting, or altering posts and comments, which could disrupt the product development lifecycle and damage the company's reputation.

title: CapsuleCRM API Token
description: Detected a CapsuleCRM API Token, potentially compromising access to CRM data and sensitive customer information.
helpText: A CapsuleCRM API Token grants full programmatic access to a company's customer and sales data. An attacker with this token could read, create, modify, and delete highly sensitive information, including customer PII (names, emails, phone numbers), sales opportunities, and communication history. This could lead to a catastrophic data breach, severe business disruption, and significant reputational damage, making its exposure a critical risk.

title: Carbon Interface API Key
description: Identified a Carbon Interface API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Carbon Interface services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised Carbon Interface API Key allows an attacker to make carbon emission estimate requests on behalf of the user. This can exhaust the API quota associated with the account, potentially leading to financial costs from overage charges on paid plans or a denial-of-service for the legitimate application. Since the API does not handle sensitive personal data, the impact is primarily financial and operational, classifying it as Medium.

title: Censys Access ID
description: Detected a Censys Access ID, which, when combined with a secret key, could lead to unauthorized access to Censys services and data.
helpText: A Censys API ID, along with its secret, grants access to a powerful reconnaissance platform. An attacker could use these credentials to query for detailed information about an organization's internet-facing assets, discover vulnerabilities, and identify misconfigurations. This provides a comprehensive blueprint for launching a targeted attack. While it doesn't grant direct control over systems, it facilitates the intelligence-gathering phase of a potential breach, representing a significant security risk.

title: Censys API Key
description: Identified a Censys API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Censys services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised Censys API Key allows an attacker to perform searches and access internet-wide scan data using the victim's account. This can lead to a denial of service for the legitimate user by exhausting API query quotas, which may also incur financial costs on a paid plan. Furthermore, an attacker could potentially view the account's query history, revealing sensitive information about the organization's security interests and reconnaissance targets.

title: CentralStationCRM API Key
description: Identified a CentralStationCRM API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to CentralStationCRM services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: The API key provides full programmatic access to a user's CentralStationCRM account. An attacker with this key can read, create, update, and delete all CRM data, including sensitive customer information (PII), contact details, deal history, and private notes. This could lead to a major data breach, business disruption through data manipulation, or permanent data loss, making it a critical risk.

title: Cex.IO User ID
description: Identified a Cex.IO User ID, which could lead to unauthorized access to Cex.IO accounts and sensitive data.
helpText: A CEX.IO User ID and secret grant programmatic access to a user's cryptocurrency exchange account. An attacker with these credentials could execute trades, view account balances and history, and potentially withdraw funds to their own wallet, leading to direct and irreversible financial loss. The potential for theft of financial assets makes this a critical finding.

title: Cex.IO API Key
description: Identified a Cex.IO API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to cryptocurrency trading accounts and financial loss.
helpText: An API key for a cryptocurrency exchange like CEX.IO provides programmatic access to a user's account. Depending on the permissions granted, an attacker with this key and its corresponding secret could execute trades, view account balances and history, or, in the worst-case scenario, withdraw all funds. The potential for direct, irreversible financial theft makes this a critical finding.

title: ChartMogul API Key
description: Detected a ChartMogul API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to ChartMogul services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A ChartMogul API Key provides programmatic access to a company's core financial and subscription metrics. An attacker with this key could read, and potentially write, highly sensitive data including customer lists, revenue figures, MRR, and churn. This would constitute a major breach of confidential business and customer data. If the key has write permissions, an attacker could also corrupt financial records, severely impacting business intelligence, reporting, and strategic decision-making. The access to a company's complete financial analytics data makes this a critical exposure.

title: Chec.io API Key
description: Identified a potential Chec.io API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Chec.io services and sensitive e-commerce data.
helpText: A Chec.io API Key provides full administrative access to an e-commerce backend. An attacker with this key could read sensitive customer data (PII) from orders, modify products and pricing, and potentially issue fraudulent refunds. The direct access to sensitive customer information and control over core e-commerce operations makes this a critical vulnerability.

title: Checkout Account ID
description: Detected a Checkout Account ID, which could lead to unauthorized access to customer data and financial information.
helpText: An Account ID for a payment processor like Checkout.com, when paired with a Client Secret grants an attacker the ability to perform highly sensitive operations. This includes initiating fraudulent charges, processing refunds to their own accounts, and accessing sensitive customer transaction history.

title: Checkout Secret Key
description: Uncovered a possible Checkout secret key, potentially compromising payment processing and financial transactions.
helpText: A Checkout.com secret Key is used to authenticate server-side API requests. An attacker with this key can perform highly sensitive operations, such as capturing payments, voiding authorizations, and, most critically, issuing refunds. This provides a direct path to stealing funds from the merchant's account. It also grants access to sensitive transaction and customer data, making its exposure a critical security risk.

title: Checkvist Access ID
description: Detected a Checkvist Access ID in an email address format, which could be used as a username in conjunction with a key, risking unauthorized access to Checkvist accounts.
helpText: A Checkvist Access ID in email format when paired with a password grants full read, write, and delete access to a user's checklists and tasks. An attacker could exfiltrate all of the user's data, which may include sensitive personal information, project plans, or confidential business notes. Furthermore, an attacker could maliciously modify or permanently delete all of the user's data, leading to significant data loss and disruption.

title: Checkvist API Key
description: Uncovered a possible Checkvist API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Checkvist services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Checkvist API key provides full read and write access to a user's account data. An attacker with this key could exfiltrate all lists and tasks, which may contain sensitive personal information (PII), business plans, or intellectual property. They could also maliciously delete or modify all user data, leading to permanent data loss and service disruption. The potential for significant sensitive data exfiltration makes this a high-severity finding.

title: Cicero API Key
description: Detected a potential Cicero API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Cicero services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A leaked Cicero API Key could allow an attacker to make unauthorized requests on behalf of the user. Since the Cicero API operates on a tiered pricing model, this could lead to financial costs by exhausting the monthly quota or incurring overage charges on a paid plan. This would also cause a denial of service for the legitimate application relying on the API. While the data itself is public, the potential for financial impact and service disruption warrants a medium severity.

title: Clarifai API Key
description: Detected a Clarifai API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Clarifai AI services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Clarifai API Key provides full access to a specific application within the platform. An attacker with this key could exfiltrate, modify, or delete all data (images, videos, text) uploaded to that application, which may include sensitive PII. They could also disrupt services by altering AI models and workflows, and incur significant financial costs by exhausting API quotas. The potential for a sensitive data breach and service disruption warrants a high severity rating.

title: Clearbit API Key
description: Identified a potential Clearbit API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Clearbit services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Clearbit API Key allows for data enrichment lookups, which can return sensitive Personally Identifiable Information (PII) and business contact details. An attacker with this key could exfiltrate large amounts of this enriched data at the victim's expense. The exposure of PII, combined with the potential for significant financial abuse by exhausting API quotas on a paid plan, classifies this as a high-severity secret.

title: ClickHelp Access ID
description: Detected a ClickHelp email address, which serves as an access ID and could be used in conjunction with a secret key to gain unauthorized access to ClickHelp services.
helpText: A ClickHelp Access ID, when paired with its corresponding Secret, grants full programmatic access to a company's documentation portal via the API. An attacker could use these credentials to read, modify, or delete all technical documentation, knowledge bases, and user guides. This could lead to the theft of sensitive intellectual property, service disruption for customers relying on the documentation, and reputational damage.

title: ClickHelp API Key
description: Identified a potential ClickHelp API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to ClickHelp services and sensitive project data.
helpText: A ClickHelp API key inherits the permissions of the user it was generated for. A compromised key could grant an attacker extensive control over a documentation portal, allowing them to read, create, modify, or delete sensitive documentation content (intellectual property). An attacker could also manage users, potentially exfiltrating user lists (PII) or disrupting access for legitimate team members. The potential for intellectual property theft and service disruption warrants a high severity.

title: ClickHelp Sensitive URL
description: Detected a ClickHelp server pattern, potentially exposing access to ClickHelp services and sensitive data.
helpText: A sensitive ClickHelp URL, such as a time-limited reviewer link, can grant unauthenticated access to view and comment on specific documentation topics. If this URL is exposed, an attacker could access potentially confidential information about pre-release features, internal processes, or other proprietary data. While it does not grant administrative access to the portal or expose user PII, the potential for intellectual property leakage justifies a medium severity.

title: ClickUp Personal Access Token
description: Detected a ClickUp Personal Access Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to ClickUp services and sensitive user data.
helpText: A ClickUp Personal Access Token grants full programmatic access equivalent to the user's own permissions. An attacker could use this token to read, modify, and delete sensitive data including tasks, documents, and internal communications. This could lead to the exfiltration of intellectual property, project plans, and other confidential business information, as well as cause significant disruption to operations by deleting or altering critical project data.

title: Clockify API Key
description: Identified a Clockify API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Clockify services and sensitive time tracking data.
helpText: A Clockify API key grants programmatic access equivalent to the user it belongs to. An attacker could exfiltrate sensitive business data, including project details, client lists, and employee work patterns. The key could also be used to create, modify, or delete time entries, severely disrupting billing, payroll, and project management. This level of access to read and write sensitive operational data justifies a high severity.

title: CloudElements Access ID
description: Detected a CloudElements Organization ID, which is used in conjunction with a user key for authorization to CloudElements API services.
helpText: A CloudElements credential grants access to its API integration platform. Since this platform is used to connect and orchestrate multiple backend cloud services (like Salesforce, NetSuite, or other CRMs/ERPs), a compromised key acts as a master key. An attacker could potentially access, exfiltrate, or manipulate sensitive data across all connected services, making the impact severe and widespread.

title: CloudElements API Key
description: Identified a CloudElements API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to CloudElements services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Cloud Elements API Key authenticates a user or organization to the integration platform. A compromised key could allow an attacker to access and control all the third-party applications connected through Cloud Elements. This could grant them indirect access to highly sensitive systems like CRMs (e.g., Salesforce), cloud storage (e.g., Dropbox), and financial platforms, enabling them to read, modify, or delete critical business and customer data across multiple services.

title: Cloudflare API Key
description: Detected a Cloudflare API Key, potentially compromising cloud application deployments and operational security.
helpText: A Cloudflare API Key can grant extensive, and in some cases, complete control over an account's domains. Depending on its permissions, an attacker could modify DNS records to hijack traffic (e.g., redirecting a website to a malicious server), disable critical security protections like the Web Application Firewall (WAF) and DDoS mitigation, or purge/modify cached content. The potential for a full domain takeover and bypassing security controls makes this a critical risk.

title: Cloudflare API Token
description: Detected a Cloudflare API Token, potentially compromising cloud application deployments and operational security.
helpText: A Cloudflare API Token provides programmatic access to manage Cloudflare settings. Depending on its permissions, an attacker could manipulate DNS records to hijack traffic, disable security features like the WAF and DDoS protection, or deploy malicious code using Cloudflare Workers to intercept sensitive user data. This level of control over a website's traffic, security posture, and application logic represents a full compromise of the application's integrity and security, making it a critical risk.

title: Cloudmersive API Key
description: Detected a Cloudmersive API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Cloudmersive services and data exposure.
helpText: A compromised Cloudmersive API Key allows an attacker to make requests to any of its APIs on behalf of the legitimate user. This could be abused to incur significant financial costs by exhausting paid API quotas or cause a denial of service for the application relying on Cloudmersive. While it doesn't grant direct access to stored sensitive data, it facilitates abuse of a paid service, making its impact moderate.

title: Cloverly API Key
description: Identified a potential Cloverly API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Cloverly services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised Cloverly API Key allows an attacker to make authenticated requests on behalf of the user. This includes purchasing carbon offsets, which would be charged to the account owner's payment method on file. This constitutes a direct financial risk, as an attacker could make fraudulent purchases, leading to significant financial loss. While it doesn't expose sensitive PII, the ability to initiate financial transactions makes the severity high.

title: Coda API Key
description: Detected a Coda API Key, potentially compromising access to Coda services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Coda API Key grants programmatic access to a user's Coda documents with permissions equivalent to that user. Attackers can read, write, and delete content within any accessible doc. Since Coda is frequently used as a central knowledge base, it may contain highly sensitive information such as strategic plans, internal processes, PII, or even other secrets and credentials. A leak could lead to massive data exfiltration, business disruption, and a staging point for further attacks, justifying a critical severity.

title: Codemagic API Token
description: Detected a Codemagic API Token, potentially compromising cloud application deployments and operational security.
helpText: A Codemagic API Token provides programmatic access to the CI/CD pipeline. An attacker with this token could access source code, steal sensitive secrets stored as environment variables (such as code signing keys, deployment credentials, or other API keys), download build artifacts, and trigger builds to inject malicious code into the final application. This level of access to the software supply chain represents a critical security risk.

title: Codequiry API Key
description: Identified a potential Codequiry API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Codequiry services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Codequiry API Key allows for programmatic submission of source code for analysis. An attacker with this key could access previously submitted code and analysis reports, leading to the exfiltration of potentially sensitive or proprietary source code. Additionally, they could abuse the key to exhaust the account's API quota or incur financial costs on a paid plan. The primary risk is the theft of intellectual property, which is considered highly sensitive data.

title: CoinAPI API Key
description: Identified a CoinAPI API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to CoinAPI services and sensitive financial data.
helpText: A compromised CoinAPI API Key allows an attacker to make requests on behalf of the legitimate user. This could lead to financial loss by exhausting the request quota of a paid subscription plan, effectively causing a denial of service for the application that relies on this data feed. The key does not grant access to any cryptocurrency funds, private user data, or critical systems, but the potential for service disruption and financial cost warrants a medium severity.

title: Coinlib API Key
description: Identified a potential Coinlib API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Coinlib services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A leaked Coinlib API Key allows an attacker to make requests for cryptocurrency market data on behalf of the user. If the key is for a paid plan, this could lead to the exhaustion of API quotas, resulting in financial loss (wasted subscription fees) and a denial of service for the legitimate application relying on this data feed. The key does not grant access to user funds, portfolios, or other sensitive personal information.

title: CommerceJS API Key
description: Detected a CommerceJS API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to e-commerce services and sensitive data.
helpText: CommerceJS uses both public and secret API keys. While a public key has limited scope, a secret key provides full administrative access to the e-commerce store's backend. A compromised secret key would allow an attacker to read all customer and order data (including PII), modify products and pricing, and potentially process fraudulent orders or refunds. Due to the potential for a full store takeover and access to sensitive customer data, this secret is rated as critical.

title: CompanyHub API Key
description: Uncovered a possible CompanyHub API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to CompanyHub services and sensitive data.
helpText: A CompanyHub API Key provides programmatic access to a company's CRM data. An attacker with this key could potentially read, create, update, or delete sensitive customer information (PII), sales leads, and deal records. This could lead to a major data breach, sabotage of sales operations, and significant business disruption, making its exposure a high severity risk.

title: CompanyHub Client ID
description: Detected a pattern that resembles a CompanyHub Client ID, posing a risk of compromised CompanyHub integrations and data breaches.
helpText: A CompanyHub Client ID is a public identifier used in an OAuth flow to identify an application. It is not a secret and does not grant any access on its own. It must be paired with a corresponding Client Secret to authenticate. Therefore, the exposure of only the Client ID poses a minimal security risk.

title: Confluent API Key
description: Identified a Confluent API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Confluent Cloud services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Confluent API Key and secret provide programmatic access to Kafka clusters and other resources within Confluent Cloud. Depending on its permissions, an attacker could read from, write to, or manage topics containing sensitive real-time data, such as financial transactions, PII, or application logs. This could lead to massive data exfiltration, data tampering, or complete service disruption by deleting critical topics or clusters. The potential for direct control over an organization's central data backbone makes this a critical finding.

title: Confluent Secret Key
description: Discovered a potential Confluent Secret Key, risking unauthorized operations and data access within Confluent services.
helpText: A Confluent Secret Key, paired with its API Key, is used to authenticate to Kafka clusters and other Confluent Cloud resources. A compromised key could allow an attacker to read from or write to data streams (Kafka topics). This could lead to the exfiltration of massive amounts of sensitive real-time data (e.g., financial transactions, PII, logs) or the injection of malicious data, causing widespread system disruption and data corruption. Given its role as a central data hub, unauthorized access is considered critical.

title: Contentful Personal Access Token
description: Uncovered a possible Contentful Personal Access Token, which may compromise access to content management systems and sensitive data.
helpText: A Contentful Personal Access Token (CFPAT) provides full programmatic access to the Content Management API (CMA), inheriting the permissions of the user who created it. An attacker with this token could read, modify, and delete all content, including sensitive drafts and published production data. This could lead to website defacement, injection of malicious code into content served to users, exfiltration of confidential information, and complete destruction of the content repository. The potential for a complete takeover of an application's content infrastructure makes this a critical-level secret.

title: ConversionTools API Token
description: Identified a ConversionTools API token, which could lead to unauthorized access to ConversionTools services and data manipulation.
helpText: An exposed ConversionTools API Token allows an attacker to use the file conversion service under the victim's account. This could lead to financial loss by exhausting paid quotas or incurring pay-as-you-go charges. It could also cause a denial of service for the legitimate user. Furthermore, if the API allows access to conversion history, the attacker might be able to see metadata or even the contents of previously converted files, posing a data leak risk.

title: ConvertAPI API Key
description: Identified a ConvertAPI API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to ConvertAPI services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised ConvertAPI Key would allow an attacker to use the associated account's subscription to perform file conversions. This could lead to financial loss by exhausting paid conversion credits or incurring pay-as-you-go charges. It could also result in a denial of service for the legitimate application relying on this API once the quota is reached. The key does not grant access to a repository of previously converted files, but the potential for direct financial impact and service disruption warrants a medium severity.

title: ConvertKit API Key
description: Uncovered a possible ConvertKit API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to email marketing services and subscriber data.
helpText: A ConvertKit API key provides extensive programmatic control over an account's core assets. An attacker with this key can exfiltrate the entire subscriber list (which is sensitive PII), send malicious phishing emails to all subscribers from a trusted source, and delete or manipulate the user list. For any business using ConvertKit, the subscriber list is a critical asset, and its compromise can lead to catastrophic reputational damage, direct financial loss, and regulatory penalties related to a data breach.

title: CountryLayer Access Key
description: Identified a CountryLayer Access Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to country data services and sensitive information exposure.
helpText: A CountryLayer Access Key allows users to fetch country and geo-location data. While the data itself is not sensitive, a compromised key could be used by an attacker to exhaust the API request quota of the user's subscription plan. This could lead to a denial of service for the application relying on this data and potentially incur financial costs for the account owner if it's a paid plan.

title: Courier API Key
description: Identified a Courier API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Courier services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Courier API Key grants permissions to send notifications (email, SMS, etc.) on behalf of the application, manage user notification profiles, and access user data such as email addresses and phone numbers. An attacker could exploit this to send phishing messages to users, incur significant costs via SMS, or potentially perform account takeovers by manipulating user profiles and triggering password reset notifications. This direct access to sensitive user data and critical communication channels warrants a high severity.

title: Coveralls API Token
description: Detected a Coveralls API Token, potentially compromising code coverage reports and project insights.
helpText: A Coveralls API Token (repo_token) allows anyone to submit code coverage reports to a specific repository. An attacker with this token could upload falsified coverage data, misleading developers about the quality and test coverage of their code. This could cause untested or buggy code to be merged, degrading the integrity of the software development process. It does not grant access to source code, but the ability to tamper with a key quality metric warrants a medium severity.

title: Crowdin API Token
description: Uncovered a possible Crowdin API Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to Crowdin services and sensitive translation data.
helpText: A Crowdin API Token can provide extensive access to localization projects. Depending on its permissions, an attacker could read, modify, or delete source strings and translations. This could lead to the exfiltration of sensitive or unreleased product information, defacement of the application by injecting incorrect or malicious content into translations, or disruption of the development and release pipeline by deleting critical localization files. The ability to alter content served to users presents a significant reputational and security risk.

title: CryptoCompare API Key
description: Identified a potential CryptoCompare API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to CryptoCompare services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A CryptoCompare API Key grants access to cryptocurrency market data. If a key from a paid plan is compromised, an attacker could exhaust the account's API call quota. This would cause a denial of service for the legitimate application using the key and could lead to financial loss through plan overages. The key does not grant access to sensitive user data or trading capabilities, limiting its severity.

title: CurrencyCloud Access ID
description: Detected a CurrencyCloud Access ID (email format), which could be used in conjunction with an API key for unauthorized access to CurrencyCloud services.
helpText: A CurrencyCloud Access ID, used in conjunction with an API key, grants programmatic access to the CurrencyCloud payments platform. An attacker with these credentials could initiate fraudulent payments, transfer funds, manage beneficiaries, and exfiltrate sensitive financial transaction data.

title: CurrencyCloud API Key
description: Identified a CurrencyCloud API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to financial services and sensitive transaction data.
helpText: A CurrencyCloud API Key grants programmatic access to the platform's financial services. An attacker could use this key to initiate unauthorized payments, transfer funds to malicious accounts, exfiltrate sensitive transaction history and beneficiary bank details, and manage account balances. The potential for direct financial theft and a major data breach makes this a critical exposure.

title: CurrencyFreaks API Key
description: Identified a CurrencyFreaks API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to currency data and financial information.
helpText: A compromised CurrencyFreaks API Key allows an attacker to make requests for currency exchange data on behalf of the legitimate user. If the user has a paid subscription, this could lead to the exhaustion of their API request quota, causing a denial of service for the application relying on it and a minor financial loss related to the subscription cost. The key does not grant access to sensitive data or critical systems.

title: CurrentsAPI API Key
description: Identified a potential CurrentsAPI API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to CurrentsAPI services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A leaked CurrentsAPI Key allows an attacker to make requests on behalf of the legitimate user. Since CurrentsAPI operates on a quota-based subscription model, an attacker could exhaust the user's monthly request limit. This would cause a denial of service for the news feature and potentially incur financial costs if the plan includes overage charges. While it does not expose sensitive data, the potential for service disruption and financial impact justifies a medium severity.

title: Dandelion API Key
description: Identified a potential Dandelion API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Dandelion services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised Dandelion API Key allows an attacker to make requests to their text analysis services on behalf of the user. This could lead to financial costs by exhausting the quota of a paid plan or cause a denial of service for the application relying on the API. It does not grant access to stored sensitive data, but abuses the service subscription.

title: Dareboost API Token
description: Detected a Dareboost API Token, risking unauthorized access to Dareboost services and data.
helpText: A leaked Dareboost API Token allows an attacker to launch website performance analyses, which could exhaust the account's quota or incur financial costs. More importantly, the token can be used to retrieve detailed performance reports and monitoring data.
