These reports contain sensitive technical information about a website's configuration, technologies used, and potential vulnerabilities, which could be leveraged for reconnaissance in a targeted attack.

title: Data.gov API Key
description: Identified a potential Data.gov API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Data.gov services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Data.gov API Key provides higher rate limits for accessing public, open-source government datasets. The key is free to obtain and does not grant access to any private data or system modification capabilities. An attacker could only use the key to consume the legitimate user's API request quota, which has minimal impact as the data is already public.

title: Deepgram API Key
description: Identified a potential Deepgram API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Deepgram services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Deepgram API Key authenticates requests to its speech-to-text service. An attacker with this key could transcribe audio on the victim's behalf, leading to significant financial costs. More importantly, if the application transcribes sensitive audio (e.g., customer support calls, medical consultations, confidential meetings), the key could be used to process and exfiltrate sensitive data, PII, or protected health information (PHI). The potential for a serious data breach makes the severity HIGH.

title: Delighted API Key
description: Detected a Delighted API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Delighted services and sensitive customer feedback data.
helpText: A Delighted API Key grants full programmatic access to the account's data. An attacker can use this key to exfiltrate sensitive customer data, including personally identifiable information (PII) like names, email addresses, and phone numbers, as well as all survey responses. The ability to read this sensitive information constitutes a significant data breach, making the severity HIGH.

title: Demio API Key
description: Identified a Demio API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Demio services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised Demio API Key allows an attacker to perform sensitive actions such as listing events, fetching registrant lists (which include PII like names and emails), and retrieving chat logs. This presents a significant data exfiltration risk. An attacker could also disrupt webinars by registering fake attendees or impersonating legitimate ones, leading to reputational damage and a breach of user privacy.

title: Demio Secret Key
description: Discovered a potential Demio Secret Key, risking unauthorized access to Demio services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Demio API Secret Key, along with the API Key, provides full programmatic access to a user's account. An attacker could exfiltrate sensitive registrant information, such as names and email addresses (PII), from events. They could also disrupt business operations by deleting or modifying scheduled webinars, or register fake users, causing reputational damage and potential data breaches. This level of access to sensitive data and critical business functions justifies a high severity rating.

title: Deno Deploy Access Token
description: Uncovered a possible Deno Deploy Access Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to Deno Deploy services and resources.
helpText: A Deno Deploy Access Token grants full programmatic control over an account's projects via the Deno Deploy API. An attacker with this token can deploy arbitrary code, effectively taking over the application. This could be used to steal sensitive data processed by the application, exfiltrate environment variables, deface the service, or attack its users. The ability to modify running production code makes this a critical vulnerability.

title: Detectify API Key
description: Detected a Detectify API Key, potentially compromising access to Detectify services and sensitive security data.
helpText: A Detectify API Key allows programmatic access to the security platform. An attacker with this key could retrieve detailed vulnerability reports for the configured web applications. This sensitive information provides a direct roadmap for exploiting weaknesses, potentially leading to system compromise, data breaches, or service disruption. The key also allows managing scan profiles, which could be used to disrupt security monitoring.

title: DetectLanguage API Key
description: Identified a potential DetectLanguage API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to language detection services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A leaked DetectLanguage API Key could be used by an attacker to make requests on behalf of the user. This could result in unexpected financial costs if the key is associated with a paid plan, or cause a denial-of-service for the legitimate application by exhausting the API request quota. While the service itself does not store the text being analyzed, the potential for financial abuse and service disruption warrants a medium severity.

title: Diffbot API Key
description: Identified a potential Diffbot API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Diffbot services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Diffbot API Key authenticates requests to its data extraction services. An attacker with this key could perform extensive web crawling and data extraction on behalf of the legitimate user, leading to significant financial costs by exhausting paid API quotas. More importantly, an attacker could access the results of previous or ongoing crawls, which may contain sensitive or proprietary information extracted from public or private websites. This potential for both financial abuse and sensitive data exfiltration warrants a HIGH severity rating.

title: Diggernaut API Token
description: Identified a Diggernaut API Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to Diggernaut services and data.
helpText: A Diggernaut API Token provides programmatic control over a user's web scraping projects. An attacker with this token could exfiltrate all the data collected by the user's scrapers, which may be sensitive, proprietary, or contain PII. Additionally, they could run scrapers, consuming the user's paid quota and incurring financial costs, or disrupt legitimate data collection operations. The potential for sensitive data exfiltration makes this a high-severity finding.

title: DigitalOcean V1 Access Token
description: Identified a DigitalOcean Access Token, risking unauthorized cloud resource access and data compromise.
helpText: A DigitalOcean Personal Access Token provides full programmatic control over an account's cloud resources via the API. An attacker with this token could destroy infrastructure (Droplets, databases), steal sensitive data from servers or object storage, and provision new resources, leading to significant financial costs. This direct control over critical infrastructure and data warrants a CRITICAL severity.

title: DigitalOcean V2 Access Token
description: Identified a DigitalOcean Access Token, risking unauthorized cloud resource access and data compromise.
helpText: A DigitalOcean Personal Access Token provides programmatic access to a DigitalOcean account. Depending on its scope (read/write), an attacker can manage and destroy all cloud resources, including Droplets (servers), databases, and storage volumes. This can lead to complete service outages, theft of sensitive production data, and significant financial costs from provisioning new resources. The ability to directly control and destroy critical infrastructure makes this a critical risk.

title: Disqus API Key
description: Detected a Disqus API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Disqus services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Disqus API Key, specifically the Secret Key, is used to authenticate an application to perform administrative and moderation actions on its associated forum. An attacker with this key could delete comments, ban users, or manipulate the forum's content, causing service disruption. More critically, the key can be used to access user information related to the forum, potentially including sensitive PII like the email addresses of commenters. The ability to exfiltrate user data and disrupt a key feature of a website makes this a high-severity finding.

title: Ditto API Token
description: Uncovered a Ditto API Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to Ditto services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Ditto API Token provides read-only access to all text content within a company's workspace. An attacker with this token could exfiltrate all UI/UX copy, including text for unreleased features, internal projects, and future marketing campaigns. This represents a significant intellectual property leak, giving competitors insight into a company's product roadmap and strategy.

title: Docparser API Key
description: Identified a potential Docparser API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Docparser services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Docparser API Key provides full programmatic access to an account. An attacker with this key could upload new documents, potentially incurring costs, but more critically, they could fetch and exfiltrate sensitive data from all previously parsed documents, which may include invoices, contracts, or personal information (PII). They could also delete documents, leading to data loss and business process disruption. This level of access to potentially sensitive business data warrants a high severity rating.

title: Documo Access Token
description: Detected a potential Documo Access Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to Documo services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Documo Access Token grants full programmatic access to a user's account. An attacker could use this token to send, receive, and delete documents, which often contain highly sensitive data such as PII, PHI, or financial information. This could lead to a major data breach, service disruption through data deletion, and potentially fraudulent activity by sending faxes or documents on behalf of the user. The ability to access and manage these sensitive documents makes the impact of a leak critical.

title: DocuSign Client Secret
description: Discovered a potential DocuSign Client Secret, which, if exposed, could allow unauthorized DocuSign service access and data manipulation.
helpText: A DocuSign Client Secret is used to authenticate a server-side application in an OAuth 2.0 flow. If compromised, an attacker could potentially impersonate the application. By combining this with other vulnerabilities (like stealing an authorization code), an attacker could gain access tokens on behalf of users. This would grant them the ability to read, download, modify, and send legally binding documents and contracts, which often contain highly sensitive personal and financial information. The potential for data exfiltration, fraud, and significant legal and financial impact is severe.

title: Doppler API Token
description: Discovered a Doppler API token, posing a risk to environment and secrets management security.
helpText: A Doppler API Token provides programmatic access to the Doppler secrets management platform. An attacker with this token could read, and potentially modify, all secrets stored within the associated Doppler project. This includes highly sensitive credentials like database connection strings, cloud provider API keys, and other third-party service tokens. Compromise of this token can lead to a full-scale breach of all connected systems and services, making it a 'key to the kingdom'.

title: DronaHQ API Key
description: Identified a potential DronaHQ API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to DronaHQ services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A DronaHQ API key provides programmatic access to the applications and automations built on the platform. Since these applications often connect to critical data sources like production databases and internal APIs, an attacker could use this key to read, modify, or delete sensitive data (e.g., PII, business data), or disrupt business operations by manipulating internal tools. The potential for significant data exfiltration and service disruption makes this a high-severity finding.

title: DroneCI Access Token
description: Detected a DroneCI Access Token, potentially compromising continuous integration and deployment workflows.
helpText: A Drone CI Access Token grants API access with the permissions of the user who created it. An attacker with this token could trigger malicious builds, inject code into the deployment pipeline, and access other secrets (like cloud credentials or database passwords) stored within Drone for the repositories the user has access to. This provides a direct path to compromise production infrastructure, steal sensitive data, and modify application source code, making its exposure a critical risk.

title: Dynalist API Token
description: Detected a Dynalist API Token, potentially compromising access to Dynalist services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Dynalist API Token provides full read and write access to all of a user's documents and files. An attacker with this token could exfiltrate, modify, or delete a user's entire knowledge base, which could contain highly sensitive personal information (PII), work-related secrets, or other confidential data. This complete access to a user's private data makes the impact significant.

title: Edamam API Key
description: Detected an Edamam API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Edamam services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A leaked Edamam API Key allows an attacker to make requests to Edamam's APIs on behalf of the legitimate user. While this does not expose sensitive user PII, it could be abused to exhaust the API quota of a paid plan, leading to financial costs or a denial of service for the application's features that rely on Edamam.

title: Edamam Client ID
description: Detected a pattern that resembles an Edamam Client ID, posing a risk of compromised Edamam integrations and data breaches.
helpText: An Edamam Client ID (or App ID) is a public identifier used to identify an application when making API calls. It must be paired with a secret App Key to authenticate successfully. Exposing the Client ID alone does not grant an attacker any access to data or functionality, as they would still need the secret key. Therefore, the impact of its exposure is minimal.

title: ElasticEmail API Key
description: Detected an ElasticEmail API Key, potentially compromising email marketing services and sensitive communication data.
helpText: A compromised ElasticEmail API Key grants an attacker extensive control over an account's email sending capabilities. They could send phishing or spam emails from the victim's domain, severely damaging their reputation and potentially getting them blacklisted. The key also allows access to manage contact lists, enabling the exfiltration of sensitive user data like names and email addresses, leading to a significant data breach.

title: ElevenLabs API Key
description: Identified an ElevenLabs API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to ElevenLabs services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An ElevenLabs API Key grants full programmatic access to an account. An attacker could abuse this key to generate audio using the account's subscription, leading to financial costs. More severely, they can access the history of all previously generated audio, potentially exfiltrating sensitive information. The most significant threat is the ability to use custom and cloned voices to generate speech, enabling sophisticated impersonation, social engineering, and fraud (vishing) attacks.

title: Enigma API Key
description: Identified a potential Enigma API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Enigma services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An Enigma API Key grants access to the datasets the user is subscribed to, which can include sensitive business intelligence, financial information, or government data. An attacker could exfiltrate this valuable data, disrupt service by exhausting API quotas, and potentially incur significant financial costs for the victim. The primary risk is the theft of sensitive, paid-for data, which justifies a HIGH severity rating.

title: Envoy API Key
description: Detected an Envoy API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Envoy services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An Envoy API Key grants extensive access to a company's workplace data and operations. An attacker with this key could exfiltrate sensitive Personally Identifiable Information (PII) of all employees and visitors, including their schedules and visit history. Most critically, an attacker can create new visitor invites, which could be used to gain unauthorized physical access to a company's facilities. This direct bridge from a digital compromise to a physical security breach represents a critical threat.

title: Etherscan API Key
description: Identified a potential Etherscan API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Etherscan services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An Etherscan API Key provides programmatic access to public blockchain data. It does not grant any transactional capabilities, access to private keys, or control over funds. A compromised key could be used by an attacker to exhaust the API call quota associated with the user's plan. This might disrupt the legitimate user's application and, if on a paid plan, could lead to minor overage charges, but it does not expose any sensitive data or critical systems.

title: Ethplorer API Key
description: Identified a potential Ethplorer API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Ethplorer services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An Ethplorer API Key provides access to retrieve public data from the Ethereum blockchain. While it does not grant access to sensitive data or control over assets, a leaked key associated with a paid plan could be abused by an attacker. This would exhaust the API request quota, potentially incurring financial costs for the legitimate owner and causing a denial of service for applications relying on it.

title: Eventbrite API Token
description: Detected an Eventbrite API Token, potentially compromising event management and user data.
helpText: An Eventbrite API Token grants programmatic access to an organizer's account. An attacker could use this token to access sensitive Personally Identifiable Information (PII) of event attendees, including names and email addresses. They could also disrupt business operations by modifying, publishing, or canceling events, and access financial information related to ticket sales and payouts. This constitutes a significant data breach and operational risk.

title: Everhour API Key
description: Identified an Everhour API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Everhour services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An Everhour API Key grants extensive programmatic access to a company's core operational and financial data. An attacker could read, create, modify, or delete sensitive information including employee time logs, client lists, project details, team billing rates, and invoices. This could lead to the exfiltration of sensitive business data, disruption of payroll and billing, and potential financial fraud through invoice manipulation. The broad read/write access to this sensitive data warrants a high severity rating.

title: ExchangeRate-API API Key
description: Identified an ExchangeRate-API API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to currency exchange rate services and sensitive financial data.
helpText: A leaked ExchangeRate-API API key allows an attacker to make requests on behalf of the legitimate user. This can lead to two main impacts: 1) Exhaustion of the API request quota, causing a denial of service for the application feature that relies on it. 2) If the key is for a paid plan with overage charges, an attacker could generate excess requests, leading to direct financial costs for the key's owner. While it does not expose sensitive data, the potential for service disruption and financial loss makes it a medium severity risk.

title: ExchangeRates API Access Key
description: Identified a potential ExchangeRates API Access Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to currency exchange rate data and services.
helpText: An ExchangeRates API Access Key allows users to fetch currency exchange rate data. If exposed, an attacker could abuse the key, potentially exhausting the API request quota on a paid plan. This would cause a denial of service for the legitimate application relying on the data and could lead to minor financial costs for the account owner. The key does not grant access to sensitive user data or critical systems.

title: ExtractorAPI API Key
description: Identified an ExtractorAPI API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to ExtractorAPI services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised ExtractorAPI Key allows an attacker to make web scraping requests using the victim's account. This can lead to financial loss by exhausting the paid API request quota or incurring charges on a pay-as-you-go plan. It can also cause a denial-of-service for the legitimate application that depends on this API. The impact is primarily financial and a disruption of a third-party service, which corresponds to a medium severity.

title: FastForex API Key
description: Identified a FastForex API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to currency exchange services and financial data.
helpText: A leaked FastForex API Key allows an attacker to make requests to the currency exchange rate API on behalf of the account holder. While the API does not expose sensitive user data, its abuse could exhaust the account's request quota on a paid plan. This can lead to unexpected financial costs from overage charges or cause a denial-of-service for applications that rely on this API for currency conversion.

title: Fastly API Token
description: Uncovered a Fastly API Token, which may compromise CDN and edge cloud services, leading to content delivery and security issues.
helpText: A Fastly API Token grants programmatic control over an application's CDN configuration. An attacker with this token could modify caching rules, re-route traffic to a malicious server to steal data or serve malware, disable the Web Application Firewall (WAF), or purge the cache to cause a denial-of-service attack. This level of control over an application's public-facing infrastructure constitutes a critical risk.

title: Figma Access Token
description: Detected a Figma Personal Access Token, risking unauthorized access to Figma designs and sensitive data.
helpText: A Figma Personal Access Token grants programmatic access to the Figma API on behalf of a user. An attacker with this token could read all of the user's design files, which often contain sensitive intellectual property, unreleased product designs, and internal company data. This represents a significant data exfiltration risk, potentially leading to the loss of competitive advantage. While it may not grant direct control over production systems, the value of the stolen assets justifies a high severity rating.

title: File.io Access Token
description: Uncovered a possible File.io Access Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to FileIO services and data exposure.
helpText: A File.io Access Token is an API key, typically associated with a paid plan. An attacker with this key could upload files using the victim's account, potentially exhausting quotas or incurring financial costs on a usage-based plan. While the key does not grant access to previously uploaded files (due to the service's ephemeral nature), it can be abused for hosting malicious content, leading to reputational damage and minor financial loss.

title: Finage API Key
description: Identified a Finage API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Finage services and sensitive financial data.
helpText: A Finage API Key is used to access financial data feeds, which are typically tied to a paid subscription with usage quotas. If an attacker obtains the key, they could exhaust the API call limit, causing a denial of service for the legitimate application. This could also lead to financial costs if the plan includes overage charges. While the key does not expose sensitive PII or grant access to critical systems, the potential for service disruption and financial impact makes it a MEDIUM severity risk.

title: Financial Modeling Prep API Key
description: Identified a Financial Modeling Prep API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to financial data and analytics.
helpText: A leaked Financial Modeling Prep API Key allows an attacker to query financial data on behalf of the account owner. Since the service operates on a tiered pricing model with API call limits, an attacker could abuse the key to exhaust the monthly/minute quota of a paid plan, leading to denial of service for the legitimate application and potential financial costs. The key does not grant access to sensitive user data or critical systems, making the impact primarily financial and service-specific.

title: Flat API Key
description: Identified a Flat API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Flat services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Flat API Key provides full programmatic access to a user's account, including all their music scores. An attacker with this key could read, modify, or permanently delete a user's creative work. For composers or musicians, this represents sensitive intellectual property, and its theft or destruction would have a significant impact.

title: Fleetbase API Key
description: Detected a Fleetbase API Key, potentially compromising access to Fleetbase services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Fleetbase API Key provides full programmatic access to a company's logistics operations. An attacker with this key could read, create, update, and delete sensitive data, including customer PII (names, addresses), driver information, and real-time vehicle locations. They could also disrupt operations by modifying or deleting orders, diverting shipments, or erasing critical business records. The potential for a massive data breach and severe operational sabotage makes this a critical-level secret.

title: Flickr API Key
description: Identified a Flickr API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Flickr services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Flickr API Key, along with its secret, identifies an application to Flickr's services. While the key itself does not grant access to private user photos, an attacker could use it to impersonate the legitimate application. This could lead to several negative outcomes: 1) The key could be abused, causing it to be rate-limited or revoked by Flickr, resulting in a denial of service for the application's integration. 2) The attacker could use the key to build a phishing page that tricks users into granting the compromised application access to their accounts, potentially leading to the theft of private photos and data.

title: Float Access Token
description: Discovered a potential Float Access Token, risking unauthorized access to Float services and sensitive project data.
helpText: A Float Access Token provides programmatic access to the Float API. An attacker with this token could perform critical financial operations, such as creating new virtual corporate cards, initiating bill payments to external accounts, and accessing all historical transaction data. This represents a direct and immediate risk of significant financial theft and sensitive data exfiltration, making it a critical-level secret.

title: Flutterwave Secret Key
description: Identified a Flutterwave Secret Key, risking unauthorized financial transactions and data breaches.
helpText: A Flutterwave Secret Key is used for server-side API calls to perform sensitive operations. An attacker with this key can initiate transfers and payouts, effectively stealing funds from the merchant's account. They can also access all transaction history, issue fraudulent refunds, and manage customer data, leading to direct financial loss and a severe data breach. This is a live payment gateway key with direct access to funds.

title: FourSquare API Token
description: Discovered a potential Foursquare API Key, which, if exposed, could allow unauthorized Foursquare service access and data manipulation.
helpText: A Foursquare API Key Secret is used for authenticating against Foursquare APIs.

title: FourSquare Client Secret
description: Discovered a potential Foursquare Client Secret, which, if exposed, could allow unauthorized Foursquare service access and data manipulation.
helpText: A Foursquare Client Secret is used with a Client ID to authenticate an application in the OAuth 2.0 flow. If compromised, an attacker can impersonate the application and obtain access tokens on behalf of users. These tokens can then be used to access sensitive user data, including personal profile information, friends lists, and historical location data (check-ins), leading to a significant breach of user privacy.

title: Frame.io API Token
description: Found a Frame.io API Token, potentially compromising video collaboration and project management.
helpText: A Frame.io API Token grants programmatic access with the same permissions as the user who created it. An attacker could use this token to access, download, modify, or delete highly sensitive and valuable video assets, which often represent confidential intellectual property like unreleased films, TV shows, or commercials. If the token belongs to an admin, an attacker could also manage team members, leading to a complete account takeover. The potential for major data exfiltration and operational disruption makes this a critical finding.

title: Front Access Token
description: Detected a Front Access Token, risking unauthorized access to Front services and sensitive communication data.
helpText: A Front Access Token grants programmatic access to the Front API, which can read, create, and manage all customer conversations. An attacker with this token could exfiltrate massive amounts of sensitive data, including PII from customer support tickets and sales communications. More importantly, they could send messages on behalf of the company, enabling sophisticated phishing attacks against customers, spreading misinformation, or causing severe reputational damage. The ability to both read all sensitive communications and impersonate the company makes this a critical-level threat.

title: Fulcrum API Token
description: Uncovered a possible Fulcrum API Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to Fulcrum services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Fulcrum API Token provides programmatic access to an account, inheriting the permissions of the associated user. An attacker with this token could potentially read, create, modify, or delete all collected field data, which can include sensitive PII, proprietary business information, or critical infrastructure details. Given that this data is often the core operational data for an organization, the potential for complete data exfiltration, manipulation, and service disruption constitutes a critical risk.

title: FXMarket API Key
description: Identified a potential FXMarket API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to FXMarket services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised FXMarket API Key allows an attacker to make requests for currency data on behalf of the account holder. While this does not expose sensitive user data, it can be abused to exhaust the API request quota associated with a paid subscription plan. This could lead to a denial of service for the legitimate application relying on this data and potentially incur financial costs for the key's owner if the plan has overage charges.

title: GCP Service Account Key
description: Detected a GCP Service Account Key, which could lead to compromised Google Cloud services.
helpText: A GCP Service Account Key is a private credential used to authenticate a non-human user (an application or service) to Google Cloud APIs. Depending on the IAM roles granted to the service account, an attacker with this key could gain extensive permissions, potentially including reading and writing sensitive data in Cloud Storage, managing or destroying databases and virtual machines, or deploying their own resources, leading to significant financial costs and a complete compromise of the associated cloud environment.

title: Geoapify API Key
description: Identified a potential Geoapify API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Geoapify services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A leaked Geoapify API Key can be abused by an attacker to make requests on behalf of the legitimate user. This can lead to financial costs by exhausting paid quotas and incurring overage charges. It can also cause a denial-of-service for the application's mapping features if the rate limits are reached. The key does not typically grant access to sensitive user data, but the potential for financial loss and service disruption warrants a MEDIUM severity.

title: Geocode API Key
description: Identified a potential Geocode API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Geocode services and sensitive location data exposure.
helpText: A leaked Geocode API Key can be abused by an attacker to make requests against the owner's account. This can lead to financial costs by exhausting paid quotas and can cause a denial of service for the legitimate application that relies on the geocoding functionality. However, it does not grant access to sensitive user data or critical infrastructure.

title: Geocodify API Key
description: Identified a potential Geocodify API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Geocodify services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised Geocodify API Key allows an attacker to make requests against the owner's account. This can lead to direct financial costs by exhausting the paid quota or cause a denial-of-service for the legitimate application if the API rate limits are reached. The key does not grant access to sensitive user data or critical systems, but the potential for financial abuse and service disruption warrants a MEDIUM severity.

title: Geocodio API Key
description: Identified a potential Geocodio API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Geocodio services and sensitive location data exposure.
helpText: A compromised Geocodio API Key allows an attacker to perform geocoding lookups against the owner's account. This can lead to financial loss by exhausting paid quotas or incurring pay-as-you-go charges. It could also cause a denial of service for the legitimate application if daily limits are reached. The key does not grant access to stored sensitive data, making the primary impact financial and operational.

title: GetGeoAPI API Key
description: Identified a GetGeoAPI API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to GetGeoAPI services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A leaked GetGeoAPI Key allows an attacker to make requests to the geolocation service. This does not expose sensitive user data but can lead to financial impact by exhausting the paid API request quota, potentially incurring overage charges. It can also cause a denial-of-service for the legitimate application that relies on this API for its functionality.

title: GetResponse API Key
description: Identified a GetResponse API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to GetResponse services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A GetResponse API Key provides full programmatic access to an account's data and functionality. An attacker with this key could exfiltrate entire contact lists containing Personally Identifiable Information (PII) like names and email addresses. They could also send unauthorized emails (e.g., spam, phishing) to the subscriber base, delete contacts and campaigns, and disrupt the user's marketing operations, leading to significant reputational damage and potential data breaches.

title: GitHub Personal Access Token
description: Identified a GitHub Fine-Grained Personal Access Token, which could lead to unauthorized repository access and code manipulation.
helpText: A GitHub Personal Access Token allows authentication to the GitHub API. Depending on its permissions (scopes), an attacker could gain read and write access to private source code repositories. This would allow them to steal intellectual property, inject malicious code, tamper with CI/CD pipelines, and potentially access other secrets stored within the repository, leading to a severe supply chain attack. Due to the potential for direct modification of source code and access to sensitive data, this is considered critical.

title: GitHub Fine-Grained Personal Access Token
description: Identified a GitHub Fine-Grained Personal Access Token (PAT), which could lead to unauthorized repository access and code manipulation.
helpText: A Fine-Grained Personal Access Token (PAT) grants limited access to specific repositories and permissions. If leaked, an attacker can modify code, delete issues, or steal secrets only within the scoped repositories. While safer than a 'Classic' token, it can still be used for supply chain attacks and unauthorized data exfiltration within its allowed boundaries.

title: GitHub OAuth Access Token
description: Identified a GitHub OAuth Access Token, posing a risk of compromised GitHub account integrations and data leaks.
helpText: A GitHub OAuth Access Token allows an application to act as a specific user. If leaked, an attacker can impersonate you, accessing all repositories and organizations you have permission to view. This is a high-risk credential that bypasses Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) and remains valid until manually revoked.

title: GitHub App Token
description: Identified a GitHub App Token, which may compromise GitHub application integrations and source code security.
helpText: A GitHub App Token (Installation Token) allows an integration to act independently of a user. If compromised, an attacker can access organization resources, trigger workflows, and bypass branch protections. Because these are short-lived, the 'window of high risk' is small, but a leak during that window can lead to full repository compromise.

title: GitHub Refresh Token
description: Identified a GitHub Refresh Token, which could allow prolonged unauthorized access to GitHub services.
helpText: A GitHub Refresh Token is used to generate new access tokens without user intervention. If leaked, an attacker can maintain persistent, long-term access to your GitHub account by continuously generating new access tokens, effectively 'staying logged in' as you even after you change your password.

title: GitHub Client Secret
description: Discovered a potential GitHub Client Secret, which, if exposed, could allow unauthorized GitHub service access and data manipulation.
helpText: A GitHub Client Secret is used to authenticate an application to GitHub. An attacker who obtains this secret can impersonate the legitimate application. By tricking a user into authorizing the malicious application (e.g., through phishing), the attacker can obtain an access token for that user's account. Depending on the scopes configured for the OAuth app, this could grant the attacker extensive permissions, such as reading and writing to private source code repositories, which could lead to code theft, insertion of malware, or infrastructure compromise. The potential for direct access and modification of sensitive source code makes this a critical risk.

title: GitHub Client ID
description: Detected a pattern that resembles a GitHub Client ID, posing a risk of compromised GitHub integrations and data breaches.
helpText: A GitHub Client ID is a public identifier for an application. It is not a secret and is often exposed in client-side code or URLs. Unlike the Client Secret, it cannot be used on its own to authenticate the application or gain access to user data. An attacker could use it to initiate an OAuth flow to phish users, but without the corresponding Client Secret, they cannot complete the authorization process to obtain an access token. Therefore, its exposure has minimal direct security impact.

title: GitLab Personal Access Token
description: Identified a GitLab Personal Access Token, risking unauthorized access to GitLab repositories and codebase exposure.
helpText: A GitLab Personal Access Token allows programmatic access to GitLab repositories and APIs. Depending on its granted scopes, an attacker could read, modify, or delete source code, leading to intellectual property theft and the injection of malicious code (supply chain attack). The token could also be used to access sensitive information stored in CI/CD variables, potentially leading to a compromise of production environments. This direct and severe threat to source code integrity and infrastructure makes it critical.

title: Glassnode API Key
description: Identified a Glassnode API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Glassnode's financial market data and analytics.
helpText: A compromised Glassnode API Key allows an attacker to query the platform's extensive blockchain data. While the key does not grant access to user PII or allow financial transactions, it can be abused to exhaust the API quota of the legitimate user's paid subscription. This leads to a denial of service for the owner's applications and a potential financial loss related to the subscription cost.

title: GoCardless API Token
description: Detected a GoCardless API Token, potentially risking unauthorized direct debit payment operations and financial data exposure.
helpText: A GoCardless API Token provides full programmatic access to a merchant's payment operations. An attacker with this token could initiate fraudulent payments from customers' bank accounts, access sensitive customer PII and payment history, issue refunds, and manage payment mandates. The ability to directly manipulate live financial transactions and access sensitive customer data makes this a critical risk.

title: GoodDay API Token
description: Detected a GoodDay API Token, potentially compromising access to GoodDay services and sensitive data.
helpText: A GoodDay API Token provides the same level of access as the user's account. A leaked token could allow an attacker to read, create, modify, or delete sensitive information such as tasks, projects, and internal discussions. This could lead to the exfiltration of intellectual property, business plans, and disruption of company operations.

title: Grafana Access Token
description: Discovered a Grafana Access Token, posing a risk of compromised monitoring services and data integrity.
helpText: A Grafana Access Token provides programmatic access to the Grafana API. Depending on its assigned role (Viewer, Editor, or Admin), an attacker could gain significant control. A token with Admin privileges would allow an attacker to read data from all connected sources (which could include sensitive production databases), modify or delete dashboards, disrupt critical monitoring and alerting, and manage users and data source configurations. Due to the potential for full administrative takeover and access to sensitive underlying data, the severity is critical.

title: Grafana Sensitive URL
description: Detected a Grafana Sensitive URL, which could expose the endpoint for sensitive Grafana Cloud API operations when combined with a service account token.
helpText: A Grafana Sensitive URL provides programmatic API access to a Grafana instance. Depending on the assigned role, an attacker could read sensitive data from dashboards, modify or delete dashboards and alerts, or, with admin privileges, manage data sources and view their credentials. This could lead to a complete takeover of the monitoring infrastructure and potentially allow the attacker to pivot to connected production databases and other services, making it a critical vulnerability.

title: GraphHopper API Key
description: Identified a GraphHopper API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to mapping and routing services and sensitive location data.
helpText: A leaked GraphHopper API Key allows an attacker to make requests to its routing and geocoding services, authenticated as the victim. This can lead to financial costs by exhausting the paid plan's quota and incurring overage charges. It can also cause a denial-of-service for the legitimate application relying on this key, as API rate limits would be quickly reached. The key does not grant direct access to sensitive user data or core infrastructure, but the potential for financial impact and service disruption warrants a MEDIUM severity.

title: GTMetrix API Key
description: Identified a potential GTMetrix API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to GTMetrix services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised GTmetrix API Key allows an attacker to initiate performance tests, which consumes the account's API credits, leading to potential financial loss and denial of service for the legitimate user. An attacker can also access historical test reports, which may reveal information about non-public websites or staging environments if they were previously tested.

title: Guardianapi API Key
description: Identified a Guardian API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Guardian API services and content.
helpText: A Guardian API Key provides read-only access to public news content. A leaked key does not expose sensitive data or provide write access. The primary risk is an attacker using the key to exhaust the API rate limit, which could cause a temporary denial of service for the application's news feed feature. This is a minor disruption with no risk of data loss or system compromise.

title: Gumroad Access Token
description: Uncovered a possible Gumroad Access Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to Gumroad services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Gumroad Access Token grants full programmatic control over a creator's account. An attacker can use it to list all sales, exposing sensitive customer PII (names, emails) and detailed financial information. They can also create, modify, or delete products, which could lead to direct financial loss and complete business disruption. The ability to exfiltrate sensitive customer data and destroy a creator's entire product catalog makes this a critical-severity secret.

title: Gyazo Access Token
description: Discovered a Gyazo Access Token, potentially compromising image and GIF sharing services and user data.
helpText: A Gyazo Access Token grants an attacker full programmatic access to a user's account. This allows them to list, view, and download all of the user's past screenshots and captures, which may contain highly sensitive personal or business information (PII, credentials, private conversations). An attacker can also delete the user's entire image history or upload new content under their identity. This potential for major data exfiltration and destructive action classifies it as HIGH.

title: Happyscribe API Token
description: Identified a Happyscribe API Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to Happyscribe services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Happyscribe API Token provides programmatic access to a user's account. An attacker could use this token to access, download, and exfiltrate all past and present transcriptions and source audio/video files, which could contain highly sensitive or confidential information (e.g., private meetings, interviews, legal depositions). Additionally, the attacker could delete existing data or incur significant financial costs by submitting new transcription jobs. The potential for a major sensitive data breach justifies a high severity rating.

title: HelloSign API Key
description: Identified a potential HelloSign API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to HelloSign services and sensitive document management.
helpText: A HelloSign API Key provides programmatic access to an account's e-signature workflows. An attacker could use this key to download all existing documents, which often contain highly sensitive information like contracts, personal data (PII), and financial details. They could also send fraudulent signature requests on behalf of the user, potentially leading to legally binding agreements being made without authorization. This represents a significant data breach and fraud risk.

title: HelpCrunch API Token
description: Detected a HelpCrunch API Token, risking unauthorized access to customer support services and sensitive data.
helpText: A HelpCrunch API Token provides full programmatic access to manage customers and messages. An attacker with this token could read, update, or delete sensitive customer data (PII), including names, emails, and phone numbers. They could also send messages to customers while impersonating the company, which could be used for phishing or spreading misinformation. This represents a significant data breach and service disruption risk.

title: Helpscout API Key
description: Identified a Helpscout API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Helpscout documentation services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Help Scout API Key provides programmatic access to customer service data. An attacker with this key could read, modify, or delete sensitive customer conversations, which often contain Personally Identifiable Information (PII) and other private details. This could lead to a significant data breach, reputational damage, and disruption of customer support operations. The ability to exfiltrate a large volume of sensitive user data justifies a high severity rating.

title: HERE API Key
description: Identified a potential HERE API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to HERE services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A HERE API Key authenticates requests for mapping and location-based services. An attacker who obtains this key can make unauthorized API calls, potentially incurring significant financial costs for the account owner by exceeding usage quotas. This could also lead to a denial of service for the legitimate application when the API rate limits are exhausted. The key itself does not grant access to sensitive user data, but the financial and operational impact is significant.

title: Heroku API Key
description: Discovered a potential Heroku API Key, potentially compromising cloud application deployments and operational security.
helpText: A Heroku API Key grants full control over your account, bypassing Two-Factor Authentication (2FA). If leaked, an attacker can steal your database content, modify your source code, or scale up expensive resources—leaving you with the bill and a compromised application.

title: Heroku V2 API Key
description: Discovered a potential Heroku V2 API Key, potentially compromising cloud application deployments and operational security.
helpText: A Heroku V2 API Key grants full control over your account, bypassing Two-Factor Authentication (2FA). If leaked, an attacker can steal your database content, modify your source code, or scale up expensive resources—leaving you with the bill and a compromised application.

title: Honeycomb API Key
description: Detected a Honeycomb API Key, potentially risking unauthorized access to monitoring and observability services.
helpText: A Honeycomb API Key allows sending and querying telemetry data (logs, traces, metrics) from an application. If exposed, an attacker could exfiltrate potentially sensitive production data, including user information (PII), application errors, and internal system communications. This constitutes a serious data breach. An attacker could also disrupt monitoring by sending junk data or deleting datasets, hindering incident response and debugging efforts.

title: Host.io API Key
description: Identified a Host.io API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Host.io services and domain information.
helpText: The prefixes associated with this detection (`LTAI`, `sql`, `ftp://`) point to several highly sensitive credential types. A leaked `LTAI` key could grant an attacker extensive control over an Alibaba Cloud account. A leaked `sql` or `ftp://` connection string could provide direct access to a production database or application file server, allowing an attacker to steal or corrupt data, deface websites, or take over systems. Due to the potential for complete system compromise, the severity is critical.

title: Hugging Face Access Token
description: Discovered a Hugging Face Access token, which could lead to unauthorized access to AI models and sensitive data.
helpText: A Hugging Face access token authenticates a user against the Hugging Face Hub API. Depending on its permissions (read or write), a leaked token poses a significant threat. An attacker with a write-access token could access, modify, or delete private models, datasets, and Spaces, leading to a major intellectual property breach. They could also push malicious models to the user's public repositories, initiating a supply chain attack against the broader community. The potential for intellectual property theft, code modification, and widespread distribution of malicious models makes this a critical finding.

title: Hunter API Key
description: Identified a Hunter API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Hunter services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Hunter API Key allows for searching and verifying email addresses, which consumes the account's quota and can incur financial costs. More critically, the API key grants full read, write, and delete access to the account's saved leads, which may contain sensitive Personally Identifiable Information (PII) like names, email addresses, and phone numbers. An attacker could exfiltrate or delete entire contact lists, leading to a significant data breach.

title: Hybiscus API Key
description: Identified a Hybiscus API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Hybiscus services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A leaked Hybiscus API Key could allow an attacker to generate PDF documents at the expense of the legitimate user. This could lead to financial costs by exhausting the API quota on a paid plan and cause a denial of service for the application's reporting functionality. The key does not directly expose existing sensitive data but allows for service abuse and potential financial impact.

title: Hypertrack Secret Key
description: Identified a Hypertrack Secret Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Hypertrack services and sensitive data.
helpText: The Hypertrack Secret Key, used for server-to-server API authentication, has been exposed. A compromised key grants an attacker full programmatic access to the Hypertrack account. This includes the ability to read real-time and historical location data for all tracked devices, which constitutes a severe breach of sensitive personal or operational data. An attacker could also create, modify, or delete trips and devices, causing major disruption to business operations that rely on the location tracking service. The potential for a complete compromise of highly sensitive location data warrants a critical severity.

title: Hypertrack API Key
description: Identified a Hypertrack API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Hypertrack services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Hypertrack API Key provides access to sensitive real-time and historical location data for all devices associated with the account. An attacker could use this key to exfiltrate highly sensitive Personally Identifiable Information (PII), track individuals without their consent, and disrupt business operations by manipulating tracking data. This constitutes a significant privacy breach and data leak.

title: Iconfinder Access Token
description: Identified an Iconfinder Access Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to Iconfinder services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An Iconfinder access token provides authenticated access to a user's account via the API. An attacker could use this token to retrieve the user's personal information (including their email address), view their download and license history, and download premium assets that the user has purchased or has access to via a subscription. The combination of exposing Personally Identifiable Information (PII) and allowing the exfiltration of paid digital assets constitutes a significant data breach, making the severity HIGH.

title: Imagekit API Key
description: Identified a potential Imagekit API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Imagekit services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An Imagekit private API Key grants full programmatic access to an account's media library. An attacker could use this key to upload malicious content, delete all existing images and videos causing a denial of service, or exfiltrate potentially sensitive visual data. The ability to destroy or access all media assets constitutes a significant disruption to application services and a potential data breach.

title: Imagga API Key
description: Identified a potential Imagga API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Imagga services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An Imagga API Key allows programmatic access to their image analysis services. A compromised key could be used by an attacker to exhaust the account's API request quota, leading to financial costs for the owner and a denial of service for any application relying on it. While the key does not grant direct access to sensitive infrastructure, the potential for financial abuse and service disruption warrants a medium severity.

title: Infura API Key
description: Identified a potential Infura API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Infura services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An Infura API Key provides gateway access to the Ethereum network. An attacker cannot use this key alone to sign transactions or steal funds, as that requires a separate private key. However, an attacker can use the key to make a large volume of requests, which could cause a denial-of-service for the legitimate application by exhausting its request quota. If the key is linked to a paid plan, this could also incur significant financial costs for the owner. This potential for service disruption and financial impact classifies it as a MEDIUM severity risk.

title: Insightly API Key
description: Identified a potential Insightly API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Insightly services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An Insightly API Key provides full programmatic read and write access to the associated CRM account's data. An attacker with this key could exfiltrate a company's entire customer database, including sensitive PII, sales opportunities, and project details. They could also modify or delete this critical business data, leading to severe operational disruption and reputational damage. The potential for a mass data breach of sensitive customer and business information makes this a high-severity secret.

title: Instabot API Key
description: Identified a potential Instabot API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Instabot services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An Instabot API Key grants programmatic access to the data collected by the chatbot. An attacker with this key could exfiltrate sensitive user information and conversation logs, which may contain Personally Identifiable Information (PII) like names, email addresses, and phone numbers shared by visitors. This access to potentially sensitive user data constitutes a significant privacy breach, making the severity HIGH.

title: Instamojo Client ID
description: Detected an Instamojo Client ID, posing a risk of compromised Instamojo integrations and data breaches.
helpText: An Instamojo Client ID is a public identifier used in the OAuth 2.0 authentication flow. By itself, it is not a secret. An attacker would also need the corresponding private Client Secret to generate an access token. Without the Client Secret, the Client ID cannot be used to access sensitive payment information, user data, or perform any actions on the account. Therefore, its exposure represents a low risk.

title: Instamojo Client Secret
description: Discovered a potential Instamojo Client Secret, which could lead to unauthorized access to payment processing services and sensitive financial data.
helpText: An Instamojo Client Secret, along with a Client ID, is used to generate access tokens for the Instamojo API. A compromised secret allows an attacker to perform highly sensitive actions, such as viewing transaction details, issuing refunds, and initiating payouts to a bank account. This provides a direct path for an attacker to steal all funds from the merchant's account, making it a critical vulnerability.

title: Intrinio API Key
description: Identified an Intrinio API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to financial data and services.
helpText: An exposed Intrinio API Key allows an attacker to query the financial data endpoints associated with the user's paid subscription. This could lead to financial loss by exhausting the account's data credits or API call quota, potentially incurring overage charges. It could also cause a denial-of-service for the legitimate application if rate limits are hit. The key does not expose PII or provide control over infrastructure, so the primary impact is financial and service availability.

title: Ip2Location API Key
description: Identified a potential Ip2Location API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to IP geolocation services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An IP2Location API Key allows for IP address lookups. A compromised key could be abused by an attacker to exhaust the query quota associated with a paid plan, leading to financial loss for the account owner and a denial of service for the application feature that relies on this geolocation data. While it does not expose sensitive user data, the potential for financial impact and service disruption warrants a medium severity.

title: Ipapi Access Key
description: Identified a potential Ipapi Access Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Ipapi services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An Ipapi Access Key is used to make requests to their IP geolocation API. The service operates on a tiered subscription model, including paid plans with request quotas. A compromised key could be abused by an attacker to exhaust the monthly request limit, causing a denial of service for the legitimate application and potentially incurring financial costs for the account owner. The key does not grant access to sensitive PII or critical systems.

title: IPGeolocation API Key
description: Identified a potential IPGeolocation API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to IPGeolocation services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An IPGeolocation API Key allows users to make requests to look up information about IP addresses. A compromised key could be abused by an attacker to exhaust the API request quota associated with the user's subscription, leading to a denial of service for the legitimate application. If the account is on a paid plan, this could also incur unexpected financial costs. The key does not grant access to sensitive user data, but the potential for service disruption and financial impact warrants a medium severity.

title: IPinfoDB API Key
description: Identified a potential IPinfoDB API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to IPinfoDB services and sensitive IP data.
helpText: An IPinfoDB API Key is used to query an IP geolocation database. A compromised key could be abused by an attacker to exhaust the user's API request quota. This can lead to a denial of service for the legitimate application and potential financial costs if the user is on a paid plan with a high query limit. The key does not grant access to sensitive account data.

title: IpStack Access Key
description: Identified a potential IpStack Access Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to IP geolocation services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An IpStack Access Key allows for IP address to geolocation lookups. If the key is linked to a paid plan, an attacker could abuse it to make a large volume of requests, potentially incurring financial costs through overages or exhausting the monthly quota. This would also result in a denial of service for the application's geolocation features. The key does not expose sensitive user data or grant access to critical systems.

title: Jotform API Key
description: Identified a Jotform API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to form data and account management.
helpText: A Jotform API Key can grant programmatic access to an account's forms and, most importantly, all of the submission data. Depending on the key's permissions (Read, Write, or Full), an attacker could exfiltrate sensitive user data (PII) from form submissions, or tamper with/delete forms and submission data. This could lead to a significant data breach and loss of critical information.

title: JumpCloud API Key
description: Detected a JumpCloud API Key, potentially compromising access to JumpCloud services and sensitive data.
helpText: A JumpCloud API Key provides programmatic control over a company's core identity and access management infrastructure. An attacker with this key could create, modify, or delete users and user groups, reset passwords, manage system access, and alter single sign-on (SSO) configurations. This level of access could lead to a complete takeover of an organization's user directory, unauthorized access to countless integrated applications and systems, and widespread denial of service, making it a critical security risk.

title: JSON Web Token
description: Detected a JSON Web Token, which may lead to unauthorized access to web application and sensitive data.
helpText: A JSON Web Token (JWT) is a compact, URL-safe means of representing claims to be transferred between two parties. It is often used for authentication and authorization in web applications. A compromised JWT could be used to access protected resources or perform actions on behalf of the user, leading to unauthorized access to web applications and sensitive data.

title: Kanban Tool API Token
description: Detected a Kanban Tool API Token, risking unauthorized access to Kanban Tool services and sensitive user data.
helpText: A Kanban Tool API Token grants full read and write access to a user's project boards, tasks, comments, and attachments. An attacker could exfiltrate sensitive project data, including intellectual property, internal plans, or credentials stored in tasks. They could also disrupt operations by modifying or deleting critical project information, making this a high-severity threat.

title: Kanban Tool Sensitive URL
description: Detected a Kanban Tool domain pattern, which is used to construct sensitive API URLs, potentially exposing Kanban Tool services and data.
helpText: A Sensitive URL for Kanban Tool, such as a private share link, grants unauthenticated, read-only access to a specific project board. An attacker with this URL could view all tasks, comments, attachments, and user assignments on the board. This could lead to the exposure of highly sensitive information, including intellectual property, project roadmaps, internal discussions, or details about security vulnerabilities, resulting in a significant data breach.

title: KarmaCRM API Key
description: Identified a potential KarmaCRM API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to KarmaCRM services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A KarmaCRM API Key provides programmatic access to a company's entire customer and sales database. An attacker with this key could exfiltrate sensitive customer PII, read confidential sales data, and modify or delete critical business records. This level of access to a core business system constitutes a critical security risk, leading to major data breaches and business disruption.

title: Kickbox API Key
description: Identified a potential Kickbox API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Kickbox email verification services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Kickbox API Key allows for programmatic email verification. An attacker with this key can use the associated account's paid credits to verify their own email lists. This leads to direct financial loss for the key owner and can cause a denial of service for the legitimate application once the verification quota is exhausted. The key does not grant access to historical verification data or user lists, so the primary impact is financial and service disruption.

title: Klaviyo API Key
description: Identified a Klaviyo API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Klaviyo services and sensitive marketing data.
helpText: A Klaviyo private API Key (which can be prefixed with 'pk_') grants extensive permissions to access and manage customer data and marketing campaigns. An attacker with this key could exfiltrate highly sensitive Personally Identifiable Information (PII) for all customers, including names, email addresses, phone numbers, and purchase history. They could also send malicious or fraudulent email/SMS campaigns to the company's entire customer base, leading to a massive data breach, severe reputational damage, and potential financial loss. The ability to both read sensitive data and perform destructive actions makes this a critical vulnerability.

title: Klipfolio API Key
description: Detected a Klipfolio API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Klipfolio services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Klipfolio API Key provides programmatic access to manage data sources, dashboards, and users. A compromised key could allow an attacker to exfiltrate sensitive business data from all connected sources, such as financial records, customer PII, and sales metrics. While it doesn't grant direct access to the underlying infrastructure, the potential for a large-scale sensitive data breach justifies a high severity rating.

title: Knapsack Pro API Token
description: Detected a Knapsack Pro API Token, potentially compromising continuous integration and deployment workflows.
helpText: A Knapsack Pro API Token allows communication with their service to manage and distribute test suites for parallel execution. An attacker with this token could disrupt the CI/CD pipeline by manipulating the test queue, potentially causing builds to fail or run inefficiently. As Knapsack Pro is a paid service, an attacker could also abuse the API to exhaust the plan's quota, leading to financial loss or a denial of service for the testing process. The token does not grant access to source code or production systems, but the potential for development workflow disruption and minor financial impact warrants a MEDIUM severity.

title: Kontent API Key
description: Identified a Kontent API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to content delivery services and sensitive content.
helpText: A Kontent API Key could be a Management API key, granting full programmatic control over the content within a project. An attacker with this key could create, modify, or delete content, leading to website defacement, injection of malicious scripts, or deletion of all content. It could also be a Preview API key, allowing access to sensitive, unpublished content. Due to the potential for significant data manipulation and exfiltration, the severity is rated as HIGH.

title: LarkSuite Client ID
description: Detected a LarkSuite Client ID, posing a risk of compromised LarkSuite integrations and data breaches.
helpText: A Lark Suite Client ID (also known as App ID) is a public identifier for an application. It is used in conjunction with a secret key (App Secret) to authenticate and obtain access tokens. By itself, the Client ID does not grant any access to data or functionality and is often considered public information. An attacker would need the corresponding App Secret to compromise the application's access.

title: LarkSuite Client Secret
description: Discovered a potential LarkSuite Client Secret, which, if exposed, could allow unauthorized LarkSuite service access and data manipulation.
helpText: A Lark Suite Client Secret is used with a Client ID to authenticate an application to the Lark platform. If exposed, an attacker could use it to impersonate the application and obtain access tokens. Depending on the permissions (scopes) granted to the application, this could allow the attacker to read sensitive messages, access confidential documents and spreadsheets, exfiltrate employee PII from contacts, and potentially send messages on behalf of the application. Gaining access to a company's central nervous system of communication and collaboration represents a critical security risk.

title: LaunchDarkly API Key
description: Uncovered a Launchdarkly API Key, potentially compromising feature flag management and application functionality.
helpText: A LaunchDarkly API Key provides programmatic access to manage feature flags, environments, and projects. An attacker with this key could enable or disable application features in real-time, potentially causing massive service disruptions, enabling unfinished or insecure features in production, bypassing security controls, or exposing sensitive data. This level of control over live application logic makes the secret critical.

title: Leadfeeder API Key
description: Identified a Leadfeeder API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Leadfeeder services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Leadfeeder API Key provides programmatic read and write access to sensitive business intelligence data, including information about companies visiting a website, their browsing activity, and potential sales leads. An attacker could exfiltrate this valuable lead data for corporate espionage or disrupt the sales pipeline by manipulating lead assignments and CRM integrations. The potential for a significant breach of sensitive business data warrants a HIGH severity rating.

title: Lemlist API Key
description: Identified a Lemlist API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Lemlist services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Lemlist API Key grants extensive permissions to manage campaigns and leads. An attacker can use this key to exfiltrate the entire contact list, which contains sensitive Personally Identifiable Information (PII) like names, email addresses, and company details. Furthermore, they can modify or delete existing campaigns and leads, or send emails on behalf of the company, causing severe data breaches, business disruption, and reputational damage.

title: Lemon Squeezy API Key
description: Detected a Lemon Squeezy API Key, potentially compromising e-commerce platform integrations and sensitive sales data.
helpText: A Lemon Squeezy API Key provides full programmatic access to a merchant's store. An attacker with this key could exfiltrate sensitive customer data (PII), manage and cancel subscriptions, alter product pricing, and create fraudulent discounts, leading to direct financial loss and significant business disruption. This level of control over a core payment and e-commerce system makes it a critical secret.

title: Less Annoying CRM API Key
description: Detected a Less Annoying CRM API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to CRM services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: The Less Annoying CRM API Key provides full programmatic access to a user's entire CRM account. An attacker with this key can read, create, update, and delete all contacts, companies, sales pipeline data, notes, and files. This constitutes a complete compromise of sensitive customer data (PII) and core business operations, leading to a severe data breach and potential business disruption.

title: Linear API Key
description: Identified a Linear API key, which could compromise project management tools and sensitive task data.
helpText: A Linear API key grants programmatic access to a user's Linear workspace with the same permissions as the user who created it. An attacker could use this key to read sensitive information about unreleased features, internal discussions, and tracked security vulnerabilities. They could also disrupt development workflows by creating, modifying, or deleting issues and comments. This constitutes a significant risk of intellectual property theft and operational disruption.

title: LINE Notify API Token
description: Detected a LINE Notify API Token, risking unauthorized access to notification services and message delivery.
helpText: A leaked LINE Notify API Token allows an attacker to send messages to the specific user or group associated with the token. While it does not grant access to read messages or user data, it can be abused to spam, harass, or, most significantly, conduct targeted phishing attacks by sending malicious links disguised as legitimate notifications. This potential for social engineering elevates the risk beyond a simple nuisance.

title: LinkPreview API Key
description: Identified a LinkPreview API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to LinkPreview services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised LinkPreview API Key allows an attacker to make requests on behalf of the legitimate user's account. This can lead to financial costs by exhausting the quota of a paid plan or incurring overage charges. It also enables a denial-of-service attack against the application's link preview functionality. While the key does not expose sensitive user data, the potential for direct financial loss and service disruption justifies a medium severity.

title: LiveAgent API Key
description: Identified a potential LiveAgent API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to LiveAgent services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A LiveAgent API Key provides extensive programmatic access to the help desk system. An attacker could use this key to read, modify, or delete sensitive customer support tickets, conversations, and contact information, leading to a major breach of personally identifiable information (PII). They could also disrupt customer support operations by creating or deleting data, or impersonate agents. The potential for a significant PII data breach and service disruption warrants a high severity rating.

title: LiveAgent Sensitive URL
description: Detected a LiveAgent domain pattern, potentially exposing internal LiveAgent instances or sensitive URLs.
helpText: A Sensitive URL for LiveAgent could provide direct, unauthenticated access to specific tickets, customer conversations, or API endpoints. Since LiveAgent is used for customer support, these conversations often contain personally identifiable information (PII) and other sensitive data. An attacker could exploit this URL to exfiltrate customer data, leading to a significant data breach and loss of customer trust.

title: LocationIQ API Key
description: Identified a LocationIQ API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to geospatial services and sensitive location data.
helpText: A compromised LocationIQ API Key allows an attacker to make requests against the geocoding and mapping APIs on behalf of the account owner.
