As LocationIQ operates on a tiered pricing model with request quotas, an attacker could abuse the key to exhaust the usage quota, causing a denial of service for the application's location features, or incur significant financial costs on a paid plan. The key itself does not expose sensitive user data, making the primary impact financial and operational.

title: Loggly Access Token
description: Identified a Loggly Access Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to log management services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Loggly Access Token can be used to send data to a Loggly account or, more critically, to access the Loggly API. If the token grants read access, an attacker could exfiltrate aggregated logs which often contain sensitive information like PII, session tokens, internal IP addresses, and application errors. This presents a significant risk of sensitive data exposure and system reconnaissance.

title: Loggly Sensitive URL
description: Detected a Loggly domain, potentially exposing log management endpoints and sensitive data.
helpText: This sensitive URL contains a Loggly Customer Token, which is used to send log data to an account. An attacker with this token can inject large volumes of fake or malicious logs, polluting the data and making analysis difficult. More critically, they can exhaust the account's data plan, causing a denial of service for the logging system. This would prevent legitimate logs from being ingested, effectively blinding security and operations teams to production issues or other concurrent attacks. While it doesn't grant read access to existing logs, the ability to disrupt a crucial monitoring tool poses a high-impact security risk.

title: Loginradius API Key
description: Identified a Loginradius API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Loginradius services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A LoginRadius API Key, paired with its secret, provides administrative access to the user identity management system. An attacker with this key could read, modify, and delete all user profile data, including PII. They could also perform account takeovers by changing passwords or other credentials. This level of access is equivalent to having full write permissions on a production user database and authentication service, representing a complete compromise of the application's user base.

title: LogzIO API Token
description: Identified a LogzIO API Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to LogzIO services and sensitive log data.
helpText: A Logz.io API Token can grant an attacker access to query and read all log data sent to the platform from various applications and infrastructure. Logs frequently contain highly sensitive information, including PII, user credentials, internal IP addresses, application errors revealing vulnerabilities, and other secrets. A compromise could lead to a massive data breach, enable lateral movement by discovering other credentials, and completely undermine an organization's security monitoring and incident response capabilities. The potential for exfiltrating a wide range of sensitive production data makes this a critical finding.

title: Lokalise API Token
description: Identified a Lokalise API Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to Lokalise services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Lokalise API Token grants extensive permissions to manage localization projects. An attacker could use this token to read, modify, or delete all translation keys and their corresponding strings. This could lead to the exfiltration of sensitive or unreleased product text, service disruption by deleting content, or reputational damage by injecting malicious or offensive text into the application's user interface.

title: Loyverse API Token
description: Uncovered a possible Loyverse API Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to Loyverse services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Loyverse API Token provides full programmatic access to a merchant's store data. An attacker with this token could read, create, update, and delete sensitive information, including customer PII (names, emails, phone numbers), detailed sales history (receipts), and inventory levels. This could lead to a significant customer data breach, financial loss through price or inventory manipulation, and severe disruption to business operations.

title: LunchMoney API Key
description: Uncovered a possible LunchMoney API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to financial data and account management.
helpText: A LunchMoney API Key provides full programmatic access (read, write, update, delete) to a user's entire financial dataset. An attacker with this key can exfiltrate all transactions, asset balances, and net worth information, leading to a massive breach of highly sensitive personal financial information. They could also maliciously alter or delete the user's financial records. This level of access to such sensitive data justifies a critical severity rating.

title: Luno Access ID
description: Identified a Luno Access ID, which could lead to unauthorized access to Luno accounts and financial data.
helpText: A Luno API Key, composed of an Access ID and a secret, provides programmatic access to a user's cryptocurrency account. Depending on the permissions granted, an attacker could view balances, execute trades, and most critically, withdraw funds to an external wallet.

title: Luno API Key
description: Identified a Luno API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Luno services and financial data.
helpText: A Luno API Key, paired with its secret, allows programmatic access to a user's cryptocurrency account. Depending on the permissions granted, an attacker could execute trades, view sensitive financial information, and most importantly, withdraw cryptocurrency to an external wallet. The potential for direct and irreversible theft of funds makes this secret critical.

title: MadKudu API Key
description: Identified a MadKudu API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to MadKudu services and sensitive data.
helpText: A MadKudu API Key grants access to sensitive customer and lead data, which often includes Personally Identifiable Information (PII) like names and emails, along with valuable business intelligence such as lead scores and behavioral signals. An attacker could use this key to exfiltrate an entire database of leads and customers, resulting in a major data breach. The key could also be used to poison the lead scoring model by injecting false data, thereby disrupting sales and marketing operations. The risk of widespread PII exfiltration and business process disruption makes this a high-severity finding.

title: MagicBell Access ID
description: Detected a MagicBell user email, which could compromise access to notification preferences and sensitive user data.
helpText: A MagicBell Access ID, in email format when paired with an API Key grants full programmatic access to the notification system. An attacker could exploit this to send malicious or spam notifications to all users, potentially for phishing attacks. They could also read, modify, or delete user data and notification history within MagicBell, leading to a significant breach of sensitive user information and service disruption.

title: MagicBell API Key
description: Identified a MagicBell API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to MagicBell services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A MagicBell API Key, paired with its secret, grants programmatic access to manage and send notifications. An attacker could use this key to send malicious or phishing notifications to the application's entire user base, impersonating the legitimate service. They could also read notification data, which may contain sensitive user information, or delete users and topics, causing a denial of service for the notification system. The potential for widespread phishing and sensitive data exfiltration warrants a high severity rating.

title: Mailchimp API Key
description: Identified a Mailchimp API Key, potentially compromising email marketing campaigns and subscriber data.
helpText: A Mailchimp API Key provides full programmatic access to an account. An attacker with this key can exfiltrate entire customer/subscriber lists, which often contain sensitive PII like names and email addresses. They could also send malicious phishing campaigns to all subscribers, severely damaging the company's reputation and potentially leading to significant data breaches and financial loss for end-users.

title: Mailerlite API Key
description: Identified a Mailerlite API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Mailerlite services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Mailerlite API Key provides full programmatic access to a user's account. An attacker can exfiltrate the entire subscriber list, which often contains sensitive Personally Identifiable Information (PII) like names and email addresses. Furthermore, the key allows the attacker to send email campaigns to the subscriber list, enabling phishing attacks or spam under the guise of the legitimate account holder. It also allows for the deletion of subscribers and campaigns, leading to significant data loss and business disruption. The potential for a large-scale data breach and reputational damage classifies this as a high-severity secret.

title: MailGun Signing Key
description: Identified a MailGun Webhook Signing Key, potentially compromising email automation and data integrity.
helpText: A Mailgun Signing Key is used to verify the authenticity of webhooks and email events. If compromised, an attacker can inject fake 'payment received' or 'user created' events into your system, tricking your application into providing services or shipping goods without actual payment.

title: MailGun Private API Token
description: Detected a MailGun Private API Token, risking unauthorized email service operations and data breaches.
helpText: A Mailgun Private API Token grants full administrative access to your account. If leaked, an attacker can send millions of spam emails using your domain, delete your mailing lists, and read your outgoing message logs, resulting in your domain being blacklisted by email providers like Gmail and Outlook.

title: MailGun Public Key
description: Detected a MailGun Public Key, which could expose email verification processes and associated data.
helpText: A Mailgun Public Key is specifically used for address validation services. While less powerful than a private key, a leak allows an attacker to drain your validation credits, leading to unexpected costs and the disruption of your website's signup or checkout forms.

title: MailJetSMS API Token
description: Detected a MailJetSMS API Token, potentially risking unauthorized access to SMS services and communication data.
helpText: A compromised Mailjet SMS API Token allows an attacker to send SMS messages on behalf of the user. This can lead to significant financial costs by exhausting the account's credit, reputational damage by sending spam or malicious messages, and potential data exfiltration by accessing contact lists (PII). The ability to send SMS from a trusted account is also a powerful tool for sophisticated phishing (smishing) attacks.

title: Mailsac API Key
description: Identified a Mailsac API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Mailsac services and sensitive email data.
helpText: A Mailsac API Key allows programmatic access to read, list, and delete emails in associated inboxes. Since Mailsac is often used in development and testing, these inboxes may receive sensitive information such as password reset links, account verification tokens, magic links, or test credentials. An attacker with this key could intercept these emails to take over developer or test user accounts, potentially leading to unauthorized access to source code, staging environments, or other critical systems.

title: Mandrill API Key
description: Detected a Mandrill API Key, potentially compromising email delivery services and sensitive user data.
helpText: A Mandrill API Key allows an attacker to send emails on behalf of the compromised account. This can be abused to send large-scale phishing campaigns or spam from a trusted domain, leading to reputational damage and potential fraud. The key also grants access to sensitive information, including email templates and sending history, which may contain customer email addresses (PII). The ability to both exfiltrate sensitive user data and launch attacks against a user base constitutes a high-severity risk.

title: Marketstack Access Key
description: Identified a Marketstack Access Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to financial market data and analytics.
helpText: A Marketstack Access Key is used to authenticate requests for financial market data. An attacker with this key could exhaust the user's API request quota, causing a denial of service for the application relying on this data. This abuse consumes a paid resource, potentially leading to minor financial loss or service disruption, but does not expose sensitive user data or grant access to critical systems.

title: Mavenlink API Key
description: Detected a Mavenlink API Key, potentially compromising project management and collaboration data.
helpText: A Mavenlink API Key provides programmatic access to an organization's core business data. Depending on the permissions granted, an attacker could read, create, modify, or delete sensitive information such as projects, client data, financial records (invoices, expenses), and user information. This could lead to a major data breach, disruption of business operations, and direct financial loss, making its exposure a critical risk.

title: MaxMind License Key
description: Discovered a potential MaxMind License Key, risking unauthorized access to MaxMind services.
helpText: A MaxMind License Key is used to authenticate and download GeoIP databases (both free and paid versions) and to use their web services, such as the minFraud service. An attacker with this key could download paid databases the account is subscribed to or exhaust the query quota for web services, potentially incurring financial costs for the owner and causing a denial of service for the application's IP intelligence functionality. It does not, however, grant access to sensitive user data or critical infrastructure.

title: MediaStack Access Key
description: Detected a MediaStack Access Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to MediaStack news services and data exposure.
helpText: A MediaStack Access Key allows a user to fetch news data. An attacker could use a compromised key to make requests on behalf of the legitimate user. If the key is associated with a paid plan, this would exhaust the request quota, leading to a denial of service for the application relying on the API and potential financial loss for the key owner. The key does not expose sensitive user data, but the potential for service disruption and financial cost warrants a MEDIUM severity.

title: Mesibo API Token
description: Identified a Mesibo API Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to Mesibo services and user data.
helpText: A Mesibo API Token (App Token) grants administrative access to the backend API. An attacker with this token can perform critical actions such as reading all user messages, sending messages, managing (creating/deleting) users and groups, and generating access tokens to impersonate any user. This constitutes a full compromise of the application's communication data and integrity, leading to massive data breaches of potentially sensitive conversations and complete service disruption.

title: MessageBird Access Key
description: Identified a MessageBird Access Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to communication platforms and message data.
helpText: A MessageBird Access Key provides full programmatic access to an account's communication channels. An attacker could send messages (SMS, WhatsApp, etc.) on behalf of the compromised account, leading to phishing attacks, reputational damage, and significant financial costs. More critically, they could read all incoming and outgoing messages, potentially intercepting sensitive information such as one-time passwords (OTPs), password resets, and private user PII. This enables account takeovers and major data breaches, making its compromise critical.

title: Metrilo API Key
description: Identified a Metrilo API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Metrilo analytics services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Metrilo API Key provides full programmatic access to an account's data, including customers, orders, and events. An attacker with this key could exfiltrate the entire customer database, including sensitive PII such as names, email addresses, and purchase history. This constitutes a major data breach. The key could also be used to read sensitive business metrics or manipulate analytics data, making its exposure a high-severity risk.

title: Mixmax API Token
description: Detected a Mixmax API Token, potentially compromising access to Mixmax services and user data.
helpText: A Mixmax API Token provides extensive access to a user's email-related data and functionality. An attacker can read, modify, and delete contacts, email templates, and sequences. Most critically, the API allows sending emails on behalf of the user, enabling an attacker to conduct highly convincing phishing campaigns, spread malware, or exfiltrate sensitive communication data. This level of access and ability to impersonate the user through their email account represents a severe security risk.

title: Mockaroo API Key
description: Identified a Mockaroo API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Mockaroo services and data generation.
helpText: A Mockaroo API Key allows an attacker to programmatically interact with a user's account. This includes listing and viewing all saved data schemas, which could expose the structure of sensitive internal application models or database tables. Additionally, an attacker could abuse the key to generate data, potentially exhausting the account's API quota on a paid plan and causing a denial of service for legitimate testing pipelines. While it does not expose real user data, the potential for information disclosure and service disruption warrants a medium severity.

title: Monday Access Key
description: Detected a Monday.com Access Key (JWT), potentially compromising access to project management and sensitive data.
helpText: A Monday.com Access Key (API v2 Token) provides programmatic access to a user's account with the same permissions as that user. An attacker with this key could read, create, update, and delete sensitive data including boards, items, and user information. Given that Monday.com is often a central hub for a company's operations, a leaked key could lead to a major data breach, exfiltration of strategic plans or customer data, and severe operational disruption.

title: Moonclerk API Key
description: Identified a Moonclerk API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Moonclerk services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Moonclerk API Key provides extensive access to sensitive customer and payment data. According to its API documentation, an attacker with this key can retrieve lists of customers and their payment histories, exposing PII and financial details. Most importantly, the API allows for issuing refunds on payments. This capability enables an attacker to cause direct and significant financial loss, placing this key in the critical category alongside live payment gateway credentials.

title: Moosend API Key
description: Identified a Moosend API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Moosend services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Moosend API Key provides full programmatic access to an account's email marketing functions. An attacker could use a compromised key to exfiltrate sensitive subscriber lists containing Personally Identifiable Information (PII), such as names and email addresses. Furthermore, they could send unauthorized email campaigns (e.g., for phishing or spam) to the entire customer base, causing severe reputational damage and potential fraud. This combination of PII access and the ability to abuse a trusted communication channel makes it a high-severity secret.

title: Moralis API Key
description: Identified a Moralis API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Moralis services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Moralis API Key is used to access Web3 data services. A leaked key would not directly lead to the theft of cryptocurrency assets, as it does not control private wallet keys. However, an attacker could abuse the key to make a large volume of requests, potentially incurring significant financial costs for the account owner on a paid plan or exhausting the rate limits, leading to a denial of service for the application that relies on it.

title: Mux API Key
description: Detected a Mux API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Mux video services and content management.
helpText: A Mux API Key provides programmatic control over a user's video assets and infrastructure. Depending on its permissions, an attacker could delete all video assets and live streams, causing a major service outage. They could also access and exfiltrate potentially sensitive video content, or incur significant financial costs by creating new video processing jobs. The potential for complete destruction of video services and data access makes this a critical risk.

title: Mux Secret Key
description: Discovered a potential Mux Secret Key, risking unauthorized access to Mux services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Mux Secret Key, paired with its Access Token ID, provides full programmatic access to the Mux API. An attacker with this key could delete all video assets and live streams, causing irreversible data loss and service disruption. They could also access private video content, create new content, and manage signing keys, effectively taking full control over the account's video infrastructure. This level of control over core production data and functionality justifies a critical severity.

title: NeutrinoAPI Access ID
description: Detected a NeutrinoAPI Access ID, which could lead to unauthorized access to NeutrinoAPI services and sensitive user data.
helpText: A NeutrinoAPI Access ID is the 'User ID' portion of the authentication credentials. It must be paired with a secret API Key to make authenticated requests. By itself, the Access ID is a public identifier and does not grant any access to the API, thus posing a minimal security risk. It only reveals that the application uses this service.

title: NeutrinoAPI API Key
description: Identified a NeutrinoAPI API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to NeutrinoAPI services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A NeutrinoAPI API Key allows authenticated access to their various data tools, which operate on a credit-based system. An attacker could use a compromised key to exhaust the account's API credits, leading to direct financial costs from overages on paid plans and causing a denial of service for the legitimate application relying on this API. The impact is primarily financial and service disruption rather than direct data exfiltration.

title: New Relic API Key
description: Detected a New Relic Personal API Key, risking unauthorized access to application performance data and analytics.
helpText: A New Relic Personal API Key is tied to a user's account and inherits their permissions. If the user has significant privileges, an attacker could use this key to access highly sensitive performance data, including error logs, database queries, and potentially PII from production applications. The key can also be used to manage and disable alerting policies, effectively blinding the organization to outages or security incidents. This broad access to sensitive data and control over critical monitoring functions represents a critical risk.

title: Newscatcher API Key
description: Identified a Newscatcher API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Newscatcher services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A leaked Newscatcher API Key allows an attacker to make requests on behalf of the user. This can lead to financial loss by exhausting the API request quota on a paid plan or incurring overage charges. It can also cause a denial-of-service for the application's news-fetching functionality. The key does not expose sensitive user data or grant access to critical systems.

title: Nexmo API Key
description: Detected a potential Nexmo API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Nexmo services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Nexmo (Vonage) API Key, paired with its Secret, grants extensive control over the communication platform. An attacker could send fraudulent SMS messages or make voice calls, leading to significant financial costs. They could also access sensitive data by reading message logs, which may contain PII, two-factor authentication codes, or private conversations. The potential for direct financial fraud and exfiltration of highly sensitive user data makes this a critical secret.

title: Nexmo Secret Key
description: Discovered a potential Nexmo Secret Key, risking unauthorized access to communication services and sensitive user data.
helpText: A Nexmo API Key and Secret grant full access to the associated account's communication capabilities. An attacker could send fraudulent SMS messages or make calls, leading to significant financial costs. They could also access sensitive communication logs containing user data like phone numbers and message content, and potentially disrupt critical services like two-factor authentication.

title: Nightfall API Key
description: Detected a Nightfall API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Nightfall services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Nightfall API Key authenticates requests to the DLP scanning service. An attacker with this key could potentially access scan results, which contain a detailed inventory of where sensitive data (PII, credentials, etc.) is located within the organization's integrated systems. This information would provide a roadmap for an attacker to exfiltrate highly sensitive data. While the key does not grant direct access to the source systems, its ability to reveal the location of sensitive findings makes it a critical secret.

title: Nimble Access Token
description: Identified a Nimble Access Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to Nimble services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Nimble Access Token provides full read and write access to a company's CRM data via its API. An attacker could exfiltrate the entire contact list, including sensitive customer PII, and view, modify, or delete sales deals, notes, and tasks. This could lead to a major data breach, significant financial loss, and severe business disruption, making it a critical-risk secret.

title: Noticeable API Key
description: Detected a Noticeable API Key, potentially compromising access to Noticeable services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Noticeable API Key provides programmatic control over a project's publications. An attacker with this key could create, modify, or delete official product announcements. This could be used to deface the changelog, spread misinformation, or, more severely, publish malicious updates containing phishing links to trick users into revealing credentials or downloading malware. The potential for direct reputational damage and the ability to launch phishing attacks against a company's user base makes this a high-severity finding.

title: Notion API Token
description: Detected a Notion API Token, potentially compromising access to Notion services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Notion API Token can grant read and write access to pages and databases within a workspace. Since Notion is often used as a central knowledge base, it may contain highly sensitive information, including strategic plans, intellectual property, PII, and even other leaked credentials. An attacker with this token could exfiltrate or maliciously alter a vast amount of critical company data, leading to severe operational disruption and data breaches. The potential for the workspace to act as a repository for other secrets makes its compromise particularly dangerous.

title: NPM Access Token
description: Detected a NPM Access Token, potentially compromising access to NPM services and sensitive data.
helpText: A NPM Access Token provides full programmatic access to a user's NPM data, including all packages and dependencies. Since NPM is often used to store sensitive credentials (API keys, auth tokens) for other services, an attacker can exfiltrate these secrets. This can lead to a cascading compromise of numerous other applications and infrastructure, including production systems, making the impact critical.

title: Numverify API Key
description: Identified a Numverify API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Numverify services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A leaked Numverify API key could allow an attacker to make a large volume of phone number validation requests under the victim's account. This could lead to financial loss by incurring costs on a paid plan or cause a denial of service for the legitimate application by exhausting the API request quota. The key does not grant access to sensitive user data, so the primary impact is financial and operational.

title: Nutritionix API Key
description: Identified a Nutritionix API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Nutritionix services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Nutritionix API Key grants access to its extensive food and nutrition database. While the data itself is not sensitive, a leaked key can be abused by an attacker to make requests against the owner's account. This could lead to hitting API rate limits, causing a denial of service for the application's nutrition-related features, or incurring financial costs if the account is on a paid plan.

title: Nutritionix Client ID
description: Detected a pattern that resembles a Nutritionix Client ID, posing a risk of compromised Nutritionix integrations and data breaches.
helpText: A Nutritionix Client ID (or App ID) is a public identifier for an application. According to the Nutritionix API documentation, it must be paired with a secret App Key to make authenticated requests. On its own, the Client ID does not grant any access to data or billable actions. It is considered a public token with no inherent risk if exposed.

title: Nylas Access Token
description: Identified a Nylas Access Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to Nylas services and sensitive communication data.
helpText: A Nylas Access Token grants full programmatic access to a user's connected email, calendar, and contact data. An attacker with this token can read, send, and delete emails, access and modify calendar events, and manage contacts. This constitutes a complete takeover of the user's communication data, which can be used to steal highly sensitive information, impersonate the user for phishing attacks, or trigger password resets for other services, making its exposure critical.

title: Omnisend API Key
description: Identified an Omnisend API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to marketing automation services and sensitive customer data.
helpText: An Omnisend API Key provides extensive programmatic access to an account's marketing operations. An attacker can exfiltrate the entire list of contacts, which often includes sensitive PII like names, emails, and phone numbers. They could also send malicious or spam campaigns to all customers on behalf of the company, causing severe reputational damage, potential legal issues under regulations like GDPR/CCPA, and direct financial costs from SMS usage. This level of access to sensitive customer data and direct communication channels is considered critical.

title: OnepageCRM Access ID
description: Detected a potential OnepageCRM Access ID, which could lead to unauthorized access to CRM data when combined with a secret key.
helpText: A OnePageCRM API credential provides full programmatic access to an organization's CRM data. An attacker could use this secret to read, create, modify, or delete sensitive customer information, including contact details (PII), sales deals, and private communications. This could lead to a major data breach, business disruption, and industrial espionage.

title: OnepageCRM API Key
description: Uncovered a possible OnepageCRM API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to CRM data and sensitive information.
helpText: A OnePageCRM API Key provides full programmatic read and write access to a company's CRM data. An attacker with this key could exfiltrate highly sensitive customer data (PII), view and manipulate sales pipelines, and disrupt business operations by deleting or modifying records. This direct access to a core repository of sensitive business and personal data warrants a high severity rating.

title: Onfleet API Key
description: Identified an Onfleet API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Onfleet services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An Onfleet API Key provides full programmatic access to an organization's delivery operations. An attacker with this key can access, modify, or delete sensitive data, including customer PII (names, addresses, phone numbers), driver information, and delivery routes. They could disrupt operations by deleting tasks, reroute deliveries to steal goods, create fake drivers, or exfiltrate the entire customer and delivery history. The potential for massive data breach, operational paralysis, and direct financial loss makes this a critical-level secret.

title: OOPSpam API Key
description: Detected an OOPSpam API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to OOPSpam services and data exposure.
helpText: A leaked OOPSpam API Key could allow an attacker to make requests on behalf of the legitimate user. This could exhaust the API request quota associated with the account, leading to a denial of service for the spam protection feature on the user's website. If the plan includes overages, it could also result in unexpected financial costs. The key does not grant access to sensitive user data, but the potential for service disruption and financial impact warrants a medium severity.

title: OpenAI API Key
description: Identified an OpenAI API Key, which may compromise AI assistant integrations and expose sensitive data to unauthorized access.
helpText: An OpenAI API Key grants programmatic access to their powerful AI models like GPT. An attacker with this key could make API calls on behalf of the legitimate user, leading to significant and potentially uncapped financial costs. Furthermore, if the application uses the API to process sensitive data (like PII, source code, or internal documents), this information could be exposed. This combination of significant financial risk and potential for sensitive data exfiltration warrants a high severity rating.

title: OpenCageData API Key
description: Identified an OpenCageData API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to geocoding services and sensitive location data.
helpText: An OpenCage Data API Key is used to make geocoding requests against a user's subscription plan. An attacker with this key could exhaust the request quota, leading to a denial of service for the legitimate application. For users on paid plans, this abuse could also incur significant financial costs through overage charges. The key does not expose sensitive user data, but the potential for service disruption and financial loss warrants a MEDIUM severity.

title: OpenWeather API Key
description: Identified an OpenWeather API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to weather data and services.
helpText: An OpenWeather API Key provides access to weather data services. While this data is not sensitive, many OpenWeather plans are paid and have usage quotas. A compromised key could be abused by an attacker to make excessive API calls, potentially leading to financial costs from overage charges or a denial-of-service for the application's weather-related features by exhausting the API quota.

title: Opsgenie API Key
description: Detected an Opsgenie API Key, risking unauthorized access to alert management and operational data.
helpText: An Opsgenie API Key can grant extensive control over a company's incident management process. An attacker could read, create, or suppress critical alerts, effectively blinding teams to production issues, system failures, or security breaches. They could also exfiltrate sensitive information contained within alert details and disrupt on-call schedules. This level of control over a company's operational awareness and response capability represents a high security risk.

title: Optimizely API Key
description: Detected an Optimizely API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Optimizely services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An Optimizely API Key provides programmatic control over experiments and feature flags. An attacker with this key could manipulate live A/B tests to redirect users to malicious sites, inject malicious client-side scripts, or alter content, leading to direct financial loss and severe reputational damage. The ability to directly modify the live user experience in a way that can compromise end-users makes this a critical vulnerability.

title: PackageCloud API Key
description: Uncovered a possible PackageCloud API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to package repositories and sensitive data.
helpText: A PackageCloud API Key grants full access to a user's repositories. An attacker with this key can publish new packages, modify, or delete existing ones. This presents a severe supply chain attack risk, as an attacker could inject malicious code into a package that is then distributed and installed on production servers or developer machines, leading to remote code execution and widespread system compromise.

title: Pagar.me API Key
description: Identified a Pagar.me API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to payment processing services and sensitive financial data.
helpText: A Pagar.me API Key, especially one with the 'ak_live_' prefix indicating a live environment, grants full programmatic access to a merchant's payment processing account. An attacker can create charges, issue fraudulent refunds, access sensitive customer and transaction data, and potentially alter bank account information to redirect payouts. The ability to directly steal funds and access sensitive financial data makes this a critical-severity secret.

title: PagerDuty API Key
description: Detected a PagerDuty API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to PagerDuty services and sensitive incident management data.
helpText: A PagerDuty API Key can grant extensive control over an organization's incident response process. An attacker could use this key to trigger false incidents, suppress or resolve real alerts, and access sensitive operational data like user information, on-call schedules, and service configurations. This could blind a company to critical production failures, disrupt operations, and facilitate further attacks by hiding malicious activity, making its exposure a high-severity risk.

title: Pandadoc API Key
description: Detected a PandaDoc API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to PandaDoc services and sensitive document management.
helpText: A PandaDoc API Key provides programmatic access to a user's account. An attacker could use this key to list, download, and delete sensitive business documents such as contracts, proposals, and quotes. This could lead to a major data breach of confidential business information and personal identifiable information (PII). Additionally, an attacker could create and send documents on behalf of the user, potentially enabling fraud or causing significant reputational damage.

title: PandaScore API Token
description: Identified a PandaScore API Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to PandaScore services and sensitive data.
helpText: A PandaScore API Token grants access to its eSports data feeds. If leaked, an attacker could abuse the key to make requests on behalf of the legitimate user. This could lead to a denial of service for the user's application by exhausting API rate limits. For users on paid plans, this abuse could also incur financial costs by consuming the subscription's request quota.

title: Paperform API Key
description: Detected a Paperform API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Paperform services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Paperform API Key provides programmatic access to an account's forms and, most importantly, all of their submissions. An attacker with this key could exfiltrate all data collected through the forms, which often includes sensitive Personally Identifiable Information (PII) like names, emails, addresses, and other personal details. This potential for a major data breach of sensitive information warrants a HIGH severity.

title: Parsehub API Key
description: Uncovered a possible Parsehub API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Parsehub projects and data.
helpText: A Parsehub API Key provides full programmatic access to a user's account. An attacker can use it to list all scraping projects, run new scrapes, and, most importantly, download all scraped data. This data could be sensitive business intelligence, proprietary information, or even PII, leading to a significant data breach. The key also allows for deleting data and incurring costs by using up account quotas, making its exposure a high-risk event.

title: Parseur API Token
description: Identified a potential Parseur API Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to Parseur services and data manipulation.
helpText: A Parseur API Token grants full programmatic access to an account. An attacker could use this token to read and download all parsed data and original documents (such as emails and PDFs), which often contain sensitive business information, customer PII, or financial details. They could also disrupt data processing workflows by manipulating parsing templates or injecting malicious documents. The potential for a significant sensitive data breach makes this a high-severity finding.

title: Partnerstack API Token
description: Identified a Partnerstack API Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to Partnerstack services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Partnerstack API Token provides unrestricted programmatic access to a company's partnership program. An attacker could exfiltrate sensitive data, including partner and customer PII, and manipulate financial records by creating fraudulent transactions or rewards. The ability to directly impact revenue and payouts, leading to significant financial loss and data breaches, classifies this secret as critical.

title: Pastebin API Key
description: Detected a Pastebin API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Pastebin services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Pastebin API Key allows an attacker to act on behalf of a user's account. This includes creating, deleting, and, most importantly, listing all of the user's pastes. The primary risk is that an attacker can access and exfiltrate the content of private pastes, which may contain highly sensitive information such as other credentials, personal data (PII), or proprietary code. This direct access to potentially confidential data warrants a HIGH severity.

title: Paydirt API Key
description: Identified a potential Paydirt API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Paydirt services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Paydirt API Key provides full programmatic access to a user's account. An attacker could use this key to read, create, modify, and delete sensitive business data including client information, project details, time entries, and invoices. This could lead to a significant data breach of client PII, disruption of business operations, and financial loss through data destruction or invoice manipulation.

title: Paymongo API Key
description: Identified a Paymongo API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Paymongo services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Paymongo API Key provides full, unrestricted access to the associated account's payment functionalities. An attacker with this key can create charges, issue refunds, and initiate payouts, allowing them to directly steal funds. The key also grants access to sensitive transaction history and customer data. The potential for direct financial theft makes this a critical-severity secret.

title: Paypal Client Secret
description: Detected a potential Paypal Client Secret, which, if exposed, could allow unauthorized access to PayPal services and financial data.
helpText: A PayPal Client Secret, when combined with its corresponding Client ID, is used to generate access tokens for the PayPal REST API. A compromised secret can grant an attacker the ability to perform critical financial operations on behalf of the account holder. This includes processing payments, issuing refunds, accessing sensitive transaction history, and potentially initiating payouts. The direct potential for financial theft and exfiltration of sensitive customer financial data makes this a critical-severity secret.

title: Paypal Client ID
description: Detected a pattern that resembles a PayPal Client ID, posing a risk of compromised PayPal integrations and data breaches.
helpText: A PayPal Client ID is a public identifier for an application. It is not a secret and is often found in client-side code. By itself, it does not grant any access to an account or allow for unauthorized actions like making payments. It must be paired with a confidential Client Secret to authenticate and obtain an access token. Therefore, its exposure has a minimal security impact.

title: Paystack Secret Key
description: Discovered a potential Paystack Secret Key, risking unauthorized access to payment processing and financial data.
helpText: A Paystack Secret Key provides full, privileged access to the Paystack API on behalf of an account. An attacker with this key can perform critical actions such as initiating transfers to their own bank accounts (stealing funds), issuing fraudulent refunds, accessing sensitive customer PII and transaction histories, and charging saved customer payment methods. The potential for direct financial theft and major data breaches makes this a critical-level secret.

title: PdfLayer API Key
description: Identified a PdfLayer API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to PdfLayer services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised PdfLayer API Key allows an attacker to generate PDF documents using the legitimate user's account. This can lead to financial loss by consuming the subscription's API call quota or incurring overage charges. It could also cause a denial of service for the application's PDF generation functionality. The key does not grant access to sensitive data or core systems, making the impact MEDIUM.

title: Percy API Token
description: Detected a Percy API Token, potentially compromising visual testing and review workflows.
helpText: A Percy API Token is a write-only key used to upload new builds and snapshots to a specific Percy project. An attacker with this token could disrupt the visual testing process by pushing malicious or garbage snapshots. This could also exhaust the account's snapshot quota, leading to a denial of service for legitimate builds and potentially incurring financial costs. The token does not grant read access to existing data or project settings, which prevents data exfiltration but still allows for service disruption and financial impact.

title: Pinata API Key
description: Identified a Pinata API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Pinata services and data exposure.
helpText: A Pinata API Key grants full programmatic access to a user's account. An attacker can upload files, incurring financial costs by exceeding storage quotas. More critically, an attacker can list and unpin all files associated with the account. For services like NFT projects or decentralized applications that rely on Pinata for data persistence, this could lead to permanent data loss and a complete service disruption, making the key highly sensitive.

title: Pinata Secret Key
description: Discovered a potential Pinata Secret Key, risking unauthorized access to Pinata services and sensitive data.
helpText: A leaked Pinata Secret Key, along with the API key, grants an attacker full administrative control over the user's pinned content on IPFS. They can unpin (effectively delete) existing files, which could render NFTs or other decentralized application assets permanently inaccessible. Additionally, an attacker could upload large amounts of data, incurring significant financial costs for the account owner. This combination of destructive capability and potential for financial abuse warrants a high severity rating.

title: Pipedream Access Token
description: Detected a Pipedream Access Token, risking unauthorized access to Pipedream services and sensitive workflow data.
helpText: A Pipedream Access Token grants full programmatic access to a user's account with the same permissions as the user. An attacker can read, create, modify, and delete workflows. Since workflows often contain sensitive business logic and handle data from other integrated services (like cloud providers, payment gateways, or databases), an attacker could modify a workflow to exfiltrate sensitive data, abuse connected service credentials, or execute malicious code. This makes it a central pivot point for compromising multiple systems, classifying it as critical.

title: Pipedrive API Token
description: Uncovered a possible Pipedrive API Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to Pipedrive services and sensitive CRM data.
helpText: A Pipedrive API Token provides full programmatic access to a user's account, mirroring their permissions in the UI. Since Pipedrive is a CRM, it contains highly sensitive data, including customer PII, deal values, and sales activities. An attacker with this token could exfiltrate the entire customer database, modify or delete critical sales data causing operational chaos and direct financial loss, or impersonate the user. The potential for a complete takeover of a core business system and its sensitive data makes this a critical vulnerability.

title: Pivotal Tracker API Token
description: Identified a Pivotal Tracker API Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to project management services and sensitive project data.
helpText: A Pivotal Tracker API Token grants full programmatic access to a user's projects, equivalent to their permissions in the web interface. An attacker could use this token to read sensitive project information, such as unpatched security vulnerabilities, intellectual property, and internal plans. They could also modify or delete project data, causing significant disruption to the development lifecycle. The potential for exfiltration of highly sensitive business data makes this a high severity finding.

title: Pixabay API Key
description: Identified a potential Pixabay API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Pixabay services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Pixabay API Key allows for searching and downloading royalty-free media from their public library. The API is free to use and does not grant access to any private user data, account settings, or sensitive information. An attacker could only use the key to make requests, potentially leading to the account hitting its rate limit. The impact is minimal, as it does not expose sensitive data or incur financial costs.

title: Planyo API Key
description: Identified a potential Planyo API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Planyo services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Planyo API Key provides extensive administrative access to the booking system. An attacker could use this key to list, create, modify, and even delete customer reservations, leading to significant business disruption and revenue loss. More importantly, the key grants access to sensitive customer data, including Personally Identifiable Information (PII) associated with bookings, resulting in a severe data breach. The combination of data exfiltration and operational sabotage makes this a critical risk.

title: PollsAPI API Key
description: Identified a potential PollsAPI API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to PollsAPI services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A leaked PollsAPI API Key grants full programmatic access to an account's polls and survey data. An attacker could read potentially sensitive poll results, which may include user feedback or PII. They could also create, modify, or delete polls, leading to data loss, data manipulation, and service disruption for any application relying on the service. The potential for sensitive data exfiltration and destruction warrants a high severity rating.

title: Polygon API Key
description: Identified a Polygon API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Polygon services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A leaked Polygon.io API Key allows an attacker to query financial market data using the victim's account. This can lead to financial loss by exhausting the paid plan's quota or incurring overage charges. It can also cause a denial of service for the legitimate application that relies on this data feed. It does not expose sensitive user data or grant access to critical systems.

title: PositionStack Access Key
description: Identified a PositionStack Access Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to PositionStack's geospatial services and sensitive location data.
helpText: A PositionStack Access Key is used to authenticate requests to its geocoding API. The service operates on tiered plans, including a free plan with a request limit and paid plans with higher quotas. If an attacker obtains this key, they could exhaust the account's monthly request limit, causing a denial of service for the legitimate application. For paid plans, this abuse could also lead to unexpected financial costs for the account holder. The key does not expose sensitive user data, but the potential for service disruption and financial loss warrants a medium severity.

title: PostageApp API Key
description: Identified a potential PostageApp API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to email services and sensitive communication data.
helpText: A PostageApp API Key allows an attacker to send emails on behalf of the application or organization. This could be abused to send spam or sophisticated phishing emails from a trusted domain, potentially leading to further compromise of user accounts. It can also cause significant reputational damage, service disruption, and financial costs from excessive email sending. Depending on API permissions, the key might also grant access to the history of sent emails, exposing sensitive user data.

title: PostHog API Key
description: Detected a PosthogApp API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to PosthogApp services and sensitive event data.
helpText: A PostHog Personal API Key (prefixed with 'phx_') grants full API access equivalent to the user's account permissions. An attacker with this key can access highly sensitive user data, including event histories and session recordings, which can expose PII, credentials, and other confidential information. They can also manipulate feature flags and A/B tests, causing significant disruption to the application's functionality. The potential for a major data breach and service disruption makes this a critical finding.

title: Postman API Key
description: Uncovered a Postman API Key, potentially compromising API testing and development workflows.
helpText: A Postman API Key provides full programmatic access to a user's Postman data, including all collections and environments. Since Postman is commonly used to store sensitive credentials (API keys, auth tokens) for other services, an attacker can exfiltrate these secrets. This can lead to a cascading compromise of numerous other applications and infrastructure, including production systems, making the impact critical.

title: Postmark API Key
description: Detected a Postmark API Key, potentially compromising email delivery services and sensitive communication data.
helpText: A Postmark API Key allows for sending emails and, crucially, reading the content of recently sent messages. An attacker could exploit this to send phishing emails from the victim's trusted domain, leading to reputation damage and fraud. More critically, they could intercept highly sensitive information contained in transactional emails, such as password reset links, user PII, and financial details. Intercepting a password reset link could allow an attacker to take over user accounts, potentially including administrative accounts, leading to a full application compromise.

title: Powrbot Secret Key
description: Discovered a potential Powrbot Secret Key, risking unauthorized access to Powrbot services and sensitive data.
helpText: A compromised Powrbot Secret Key would allow an attacker to programmatically access the user's account. This could be used to generate content, potentially exhausting API quotas or incurring financial costs on the user's subscription. Furthermore, an attacker may be able to access existing content, templates, and source materials within the account, leading to a potential leak of proprietary information.

title: Private Key
description: Detected a Private Key, risking unauthorized access to private resources and data.
helpText: A private key is used to decrypt data encrypted with the corresponding public key. If an attacker obtains this key, they can decrypt sensitive data, access encrypted files, and potentially gain unauthorized access to systems or services protected by the key.

title: Prefect API Token
description: Detected a Prefect API Token, risking unauthorized access to workflow management and automation services.
helpText: A Prefect API Token grants programmatic access to manage and execute data orchestration workflows. An attacker with this token could view sensitive data within flow runs and logs, modify existing data pipelines to exfiltrate data or inject malicious code, and trigger workflows that could manipulate production data, disrupt critical business processes, or access credentials for other connected systems (like databases or cloud services). This level of control over core data infrastructure makes it a critical vulnerability.

title: Prodpad API Token
description: Detected a Prodpad API Token, risking unauthorized access to Prodpad services and project management data.
helpText: A Prodpad API Token provides full programmatic access to a company's product management data. An attacker with this token could read, modify, or delete highly sensitive strategic information, including product roadmaps, unreleased features, and customer feedback. This could lead to theft of intellectual property, severe disruption of business strategy, and competitive disadvantage, making the impact critical.

title: ProspectCRM Access Token
description: Detected a ProspectCRM Access Token, risking unauthorized access to ProspectCRM services and sensitive data.
helpText: A ProspectCRM Access Token grants programmatic access to the CRM's API. An attacker with this token could read, create, modify, and delete highly sensitive customer data (including PII), sales opportunities, orders, and quotes. This level of access could lead to a major data breach, significant business disruption, and financial loss, making its exposure a critical risk.

title: ProtocolsIO API Token
description: Identified a ProtocolsIO API Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to ProtocolsIO services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Protocols.io API Token grants full programmatic access to a user's account. An attacker could use this token to read, modify, or delete a user's private and public scientific protocols. This could lead to the theft of sensitive intellectual property, the sabotage of ongoing research by altering procedures, and the permanent loss of valuable scientific data. The potential for irreversible data destruction and theft of core intellectual property makes this a critical-level secret.

title: ProxyCrawl API Key
description: Identified a ProxyCrawl API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to ProxyCrawl services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A ProxyCrawl API Key authenticates requests for its scraping and storage services. An attacker with this key could consume the user's paid plan credits by making extensive scraping requests, leading to financial loss and denial of service for the legitimate application. More critically, the key also grants access to the user's ProxyCrawl Cloud Storage, allowing an attacker to read, exfiltrate, or delete potentially sensitive data that has been previously scraped and stored.

title: PubNub API Key
description: Detected a PubNub Subscribe Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to PubNub messaging services and sensitive data.
helpText: The detected key is a PubNub Subscribe Key (indicated by the 'sub-c-' prefix). This key allows an attacker to subscribe and listen to messages on any PubNub channels that are not protected by PubNub's Access Manager (PAM). If sensitive data such as private messages, PII, or credentials are being transmitted over these channels, an attacker could eavesdrop and exfiltrate this information. This potential for large-scale, real-time data exfiltration warrants a HIGH severity rating.

title: PubNub Public Key
description: Identified a PubNub Publish Key, which could lead to unauthorized message publishing and data manipulation on PubNub channels.
helpText: The key is a PubNub Publish Key, identified by the 'pub-c-' prefix. This key authorizes sending messages.

title: Pulumi API Token
description: Found a Pulumi API Token, posing a risk to infrastructure as code services and cloud resource management.
helpText: A Pulumi API Token grants programmatic access to the Pulumi Cloud, which manages the state of an organization's infrastructure. An attacker with this token can execute commands to modify, destroy, or create new infrastructure (e.g., databases, servers, networks). This could lead to a complete system compromise, major service outage, massive data loss, and significant financial costs. The token can also be used to reveal other secrets managed within the Pulumi state, such as database credentials or cloud provider keys.

title: PushBullet Access Token
description: Detected a Pushbullet Access Token, risking unauthorized access to Pushbullet services and user data.
helpText: A Pushbullet Access Token grants extensive control over a user's account and connected devices. An attacker can read all mirrored notifications, which may include sensitive data like two-factor authentication (2FA) codes, password reset links, and private messages. The token also allows an attacker to send SMS messages from the user's phone and push malicious files or links to all connected devices. This level of access can lead to the compromise of other accounts and significant personal data exfiltration.

title: Pusher API Key
description: Identified a Pusher API key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Pusher services and data manipulation.
helpText: A leaked Pusher API Key allows an attacker to authenticate to the Pusher API on behalf of the application. This enables them to send arbitrary messages on any channel, potentially leading to widespread phishing, misinformation, or service disruption for all connected users. They can also query information about users connected to presence channels, leading to a sensitive user information leak. The ability to manipulate the core real-time functionality of an application and impersonate the server makes this a high-severity finding.

title: Pusher Client ID
description: Identified a Pusher Client ID, which may lead to unauthorized integrations and data exposure in Pusher applications.
helpText: A Pusher Client ID (also known as the App Key) is a public key intended for use in client-side code. It allows a client to connect to the Pusher service and subscribe to public channels. It does not grant access to private channels, nor can it be used to trigger events, as those actions require the secret key. The only potential risk is an attacker using the key to exhaust connection or message quotas, leading to a minor denial of service for the real-time functionality.

title: PyPI Upload Token
description: Identified a PyPI Upload Token, potentially compromising Python package distribution and repository integrity.
helpText: A PyPI Upload Token provides full programmatic access to the PyPI repository, allowing an attacker to upload, modify, or delete packages. This can lead to the distribution of malicious packages, which could compromise the integrity of the repository and the security of users' projects.

title: Qase API Token
description: Detected a Qase API Token, posing a risk to test management services and sensitive project data.
helpText: A Qase API Token provides full programmatic access to all projects, test cases, and defect reports within a user's account. An attacker could exfiltrate highly sensitive information, such as details of unpatched security vulnerabilities, proprietary business logic, and system architecture. They could also sabotage the QA process by deleting or modifying test data, potentially allowing flawed or vulnerable code to be deployed. This level of access to sensitive pre-production data warrants a high severity rating.

title: RabbitMQ Password
description: Discovered a potential RabbitMQ Password, risking unauthorized access to message queues and sensitive data.
helpText: A RabbitMQ Password, combined with a username, grants access to the message broker. An attacker could connect to the broker to read sensitive data from queues (e.g., PII, session tokens), inject malicious messages to trigger unauthorized actions in connected services, or delete queues and exchanges, causing a widespread denial of service. As RabbitMQ is often a central component of an application's architecture, these credentials provide critical access.

title: RapidAPI API Key
description: Uncovered a RapidAPI API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to various APIs and data services.
helpText: A RapidAPI API Key authenticates a user to the entire RapidAPI marketplace, not just a single end-service. An attacker with this key can make requests to any API the legitimate user is subscribed to. This could lead to significant financial costs by exhausting paid API quotas. More critically, if the user is subscribed to APIs that process or return sensitive data (e.g., PII, business data, financial information), the attacker could exfiltrate that data. The potential for access to a wide variety of services and sensitive data types makes this a high-severity secret.

title: Rawg API Key
description: Identified a potential Rawg API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Rawg services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A RAWG API Key provides access to a public database of video game information. The API is free for non-commercial use and is rate-limited. A compromised key does not expose any sensitive user data or grant access to modify systems. The only potential impact is an attacker exhausting the API request quota, which could cause a temporary denial-of-service for the specific application feature that relies on RAWG data. This impact is minimal and contained.

title: Razorpay API Key
description: Identified a Razorpay API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to payment processing services and financial data.
helpText: A Razorpay API Key is used to authenticate requests to its payment processing API. The 'rzp_live_' prefix indicates this is a production key. An attacker with this key could perform critical actions such as processing payments, issuing fraudulent refunds to their own accounts, and accessing sensitive transaction data including customer PII. The potential for direct financial theft and sensitive data exfiltration makes this a critical finding.

title: Razorpay Secret Key
description: Discovered a potential Razorpay Secret Key, risking unauthorized financial transactions and data breaches.
helpText: A Razorpay live Secret Key, in combination with its corresponding key ID, grants full programmatic access to a merchant's payment account. An attacker with this key could perform critical actions such as issuing fraudulent refunds, initiating payouts to their own accounts (stealing funds directly), and accessing sensitive transaction details and customer PII. The potential for direct financial theft and major data breaches makes this a critical-level secret.

title: ReadMe API Key
description: Detected a ReadMe API Key, risking unauthorized documentation management and content exposure.
helpText: A ReadMe API Key provides full administrative access to an organization's documentation via the API. An attacker could use this key to read, create, update, or delete all documentation content, API specifications, and changelogs. This could lead to service disruption for developers using the API, reputational damage through vandalism of public-facing content, or the leakage of information about unreleased or internal APIs. The ability to maliciously alter or destroy a company's entire documentation set warrants a high severity rating.

title: Rebrandly API Key
description: Identified a Rebrandly API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to URL shortening services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Rebrandly API Key grants full programmatic control over an account's branded links. An attacker can create, delete, and, most importantly, modify the destination of existing short links. This allows for link hijacking, where legitimate traffic from links in active campaigns, social media, or documentation can be silently redirected to malicious websites for phishing or malware distribution. This poses a direct threat to end-users and can cause severe, immediate reputational damage to the brand, making its exposure critical.

title: Refiner API Key
description: Identified a Refiner API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Refiner services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Refiner API Key provides programmatic access to manage user data and retrieve survey results. An attacker with this key could exfiltrate all survey responses, which may contain sensitive customer feedback and Personally Identifiable Information (PII). They could also inject or manipulate user data and events, which would corrupt analytics and skew survey targeting. The ability to read potentially sensitive user-submitted data classifies this as a high-severity secret.

title: Rentman Access Token
description: Uncovered a possible Rentman Access Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to Rentman services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Rentman Access Token provides programmatic access to a company's core operational data. An attacker could use this token to read, modify, or delete sensitive information including customer data (PII), project details, financial records (quotes, invoices), and equipment inventory. This could lead to a significant data breach, financial loss, and severe business disruption.

title: Replicate API Token
description: Detected a Replicate API Token, risking unauthorized access to AI model deployment and management services.
helpText: A Replicate API Token grants full programmatic access to a user's account. An attacker could use a compromised token to run expensive AI models, leading to significant financial costs for the account owner. Additionally, the token allows access to the history of predictions, including inputs and outputs, which could expose sensitive or private data processed by the models. This combination of potential financial abuse and data exfiltration warrants a HIGH severity.

title: Reply.io API Key
description: Detected a Reply.io API Key, potentially compromising access to Reply.io services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Reply.io API Key provides programmatic access to a user's account data. An attacker with this key could exfiltrate sensitive PII of contacts and leads (names, emails, phone numbers), access proprietary email templates and sales strategies, and send emails on behalf of the user. This could lead to a significant data breach, reputational damage, and be used to launch phishing attacks against the user's customers.

title: Request Finance API Key
description: Identified a potential Request Finance API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to RequestFinance services and sensitive financial data.
helpText: A Request Finance API Key allows programmatic access to manage financial operations like creating and viewing invoices, tracking payments, and handling expenses. An attacker with this key could exfiltrate sensitive financial data, including customer information and transaction details. More critically, they could create fraudulent invoices to redirect payments from the company's clients, leading to direct financial theft and significant reputational damage. The ability to manipulate a company's core financial records makes this a critical-level secret.

title: Restpack Access Token
description: Identified a Restpack Access Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to Restpack services and sensitive data.
helpText: A compromised Restpack Access Token allows an attacker to use the account's subscription for web scraping, screenshot generation, or PDF conversion. This can lead to direct financial loss by exhausting the API quota and incurring overage charges. It can also cause a denial of service for the legitimate application that relies on the Restpack API, as the service operates on a usage-based pricing model.

title: RiteKit Client ID
description: Detected a pattern that resembles a RiteKit Client ID, posing a risk of compromised RiteKit integrations and data breaches.
helpText: A RiteKit Client ID is a public identifier used in the OAuth 2.0 authentication flow to identify an application. On its own, it does not grant any access to user data or account functionality. An attacker would also need the corresponding Client Secret to authenticate. Therefore, its exposure has a minimal direct impact.

title: Roaring Client Secret
description: Detected a potential Roaring Client Secret, risking unauthorized access to Roaring services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Roaring Client Secret is used to authenticate to the Roaring API. If compromised, an attacker could gain access to the account and make API calls to retrieve sensitive business and personal information, such as financial statements, credit reports, and details on company officials. This would lead to a significant data breach and could also incur substantial financial costs for the legitimate user through fraudulent API usage.

title: RocketReach API Key
description: Identified a RocketReach API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to RocketReach services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised RocketReach API Key allows an attacker to programmatically perform contact lookups using the victim's account. This can lead to financial loss by consuming the account's paid credits or quota. While it doesn't expose the victim's own sensitive data or infrastructure, the potential for service abuse and incurring costs makes it a medium severity risk.

title: Rockset API Key
description: Identified a Rockset API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Rockset services and data manipulation.
helpText: A Rockset API Key grants programmatic access to manage and query data within the Rockset service. Depending on the permissions assigned to the key, an attacker could read, modify, or delete sensitive data from collections, exfiltrating potentially vast amounts of production data. They could also manage integrations, collections, and users, leading to service disruption or a full data breach. Due to its potential for direct access to a core data store, this key is considered critical.

title: Route4me API Key
description: Identified a potential Route4me API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Route4me services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Route4Me API Key provides full programmatic access to an account's logistics data and operations. An attacker could use this key to read, create, update, or delete routes, access sensitive customer addresses (PII), view historical and real-time GPS tracking data of vehicles and personnel, and severely disrupt business operations. The combination of sensitive data exposure and potential for major operational sabotage makes this a critical risk.

title: RubyGems API Token
description: Identified a Rubygem API Token, potentially compromising Ruby library distribution and package management.
helpText: A RubyGems API Token grants permissions to manage gems on the platform. A compromised token can be used to publish new, malicious versions of a gem (a supply chain attack), yank (delete) existing versions, or add/remove owners. An attacker could inject malicious code into a project's dependencies, leading to widespread compromise of developer machines, CI/CD pipelines, and production servers that use the gem. The potential for arbitrary code execution in downstream systems makes this a critical vulnerability.

title: RunRun.it Access Token
description: Detected a RunRun.it Access Token, potentially compromising access to RunRun.it services and user data.
helpText: A Runrun.it Access Token grants programmatic access to a user's account. An attacker could use this token to read sensitive project details, tasks, internal comments, and client information. They could also modify or delete data, severely disrupting business operations and causing a significant breach of confidential business information. The scope of access makes this a high-severity finding.

title: RunRun.it API Key
description: Detected a RunRun.it API Key, potentially compromising access to project management and task tracking services.
helpText: A RunRun.it API Key can grant programmatic access to an organization's workspace. An attacker with this key could read sensitive business data, including project details, tasks, client lists, and user information. They could also disrupt operations by creating, modifying, or deleting tasks and manipulating time tracking records. This significant access to sensitive operational data and potential for disruption warrants a high severity rating.

title: Salesblink API Key
description: Identified a Salesblink API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Salesblink services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Salesblink API Key provides programmatic access to a user's account, including the ability to read, create, update, and delete contacts, companies, and outreach campaigns. An attacker could exfiltrate sensitive lead data containing PII, delete critical business information, or launch malicious email campaigns from the compromised account, causing a significant data breach and severe reputational damage.

title: Satismeter API Key
