description: Identified a Satismeter API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Satismeter services and sensitive user data.
helpText: A Satismeter API Key is used to send user and survey data to a specific Satismeter project. While often public in client-side code, a malicious actor could abuse a leaked key to send large volumes of fake data, corrupting feedback analytics and potentially leading to bad business decisions. This abuse could also exhaust the account's response quota, leading to a denial of service for legitimate feedback collection and possible minor financial costs.

title: Scaleway API Token
description: Detected a Scaleway API Token, risking unauthorized access to cloud services and infrastructure management.
helpText: A Scaleway API Token, consisting of an access key and a secret key, grants full programmatic access to a user's Scaleway account. An attacker with this token could manage and destroy critical infrastructure like compute instances and databases, access or exfiltrate all data stored in object storage, and incur significant financial costs by provisioning new resources. This level of control over a cloud environment represents a complete infrastructure compromise.

title: ScraperAPI API Key
description: Identified a potential ScraperAPI API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to web scraping services and data retrieval.
helpText: A compromised ScraperAPI Key allows an attacker to make web scraping requests using the victim's account. This can lead to financial loss by exhausting the paid API request quota and potentially incurring overage charges. It can also cause a denial of service for the legitimate application that relies on this key, but does not expose sensitive user data stored within the ScraperAPI platform itself.

title: ScrapeStack Access Key
description: Identified a potential ScrapeStack Access Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to ScrapeStack services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised ScrapeStack Access Key allows an attacker to make web scraping requests using the victim's account. This can lead to direct financial loss by exhausting the paid API request quota or incurring overage charges. This also results in a denial of service for the legitimate user's scraping functionality, but it does not expose sensitive internal data or grant access to critical systems.

title: Scrapfly API Key
description: Identified a potential Scrapfly API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Scrapfly services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A leaked Scrapfly API Key allows an attacker to make web scraping requests on behalf of the authenticated user. This can lead to financial costs by consuming the user's paid plan and incurring overage charges. It can also cause a denial of service for the legitimate application by exhausting the API request quota. The key does not typically grant access to sensitive PII but enables service abuse.

title: ScrapingBee API Key
description: Identified a ScrapingBee API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to web scraping services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised ScrapingBee API Key allows an attacker to make requests on behalf of the user. This can be abused to exhaust the paid API request quota, leading to a denial of service for the legitimate application and potential financial loss for the victim due to overage charges. While it does not expose sensitive user data, the potential for service disruption and financial cost makes it a medium severity risk.

title: ScreenshotAPI API Key
description: Identified a potential ScreenshotAPI API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to screenshot generation services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised ScreenshotAPI API Key allows an attacker to make requests on behalf of the victim's account. This can lead to financial loss by exhausting the paid API quota or incurring overage charges. It can also cause a denial-of-service for the legitimate application relying on this integration. The key does not grant access to sensitive user data or critical systems, but the potential for direct financial impact and service disruption warrants a medium severity.

title: ScreenshotLayer API Key
description: Detected a ScreenshotLayer API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to ScreenshotLayer services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised ScreenshotLayer API Key allows an attacker to use the account's subscription to generate website screenshots. This can lead to financial loss by exhausting the paid monthly quota and potentially incurring overage charges. It can also cause a denial of service for the legitimate application that relies on this key, preventing it from generating screenshots. The key does not grant access to sensitive data, so the impact is primarily financial and operational within the scope of this single service.

title: Scrutinizer CI Access Token
description: Detected a Scrutinizer CI Access Token, risking unauthorized access to code quality analysis services.
helpText: A Scrutinizer CI Access Token grants programmatic access to the CI/CD environment. An attacker could use this token to access private source code, view build logs, and potentially exfiltrate other sensitive secrets, such as API keys or database credentials, stored as environment variables within the build process. This level of access could lead to a complete compromise of the application and its infrastructure.

title: SelectPDF API Key
description: Identified a SelectPDF API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to SelectPDF services for PDF conversion and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised SelectPDF API Key allows an attacker to use the associated account's subscription to perform conversions. This can lead to financial loss by exhausting the paid API quota or incurring overage charges. It also causes a denial of service for the PDF generation feature once the quota is depleted. The key does not grant access to previously generated documents or other sensitive user data.

title: Semaphore API Key
description: Identified a potential Semaphore API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Semaphore services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Semaphore API Key provides programmatic access to the CI/CD platform. An attacker with this key can view and manage projects, trigger or stop builds, and, most importantly, access and exfiltrate all secrets stored within Semaphore. These secrets often include credentials for production databases, cloud provider accounts (AWS, GCP, Azure), and other sensitive services. This allows for a full compromise of the application's infrastructure and data, making it a critical risk.

title: Sendbird Access ID
description: Discovered a Sendbird Access ID, which could compromise chat and messaging platform integrations.
helpText: A Sendbird Access ID, when paired with an Access Token grants full administrative access to a Sendbird application. An attacker with this key could read all user conversations, access user data (PII), send messages as any user, ban users, and delete channels or messages. This represents a total compromise of the application's chat functionality and a severe breach of user privacy.

title: Sendbird API Token
description: Detected a Sendbird Organization API Token, risking unauthorized access to Sendbird services and sensitive application data.
helpText: A Sendbird API Token provides administrative access to an application's chat service via the Platform API. An attacker with this token can read all user conversations, access user PII, send messages as any user, and delete users, channels, or messages. This level of access can lead to a complete compromise of user privacy, massive sensitive data exfiltration, and severe service disruption, making it a critical vulnerability.

title: SendGrid API Token
description: Detected a SendGrid API Token, posing a risk of unauthorized email service operations and data exposure.
helpText: A SendGrid API Token grants programmatic access to send emails and manage account data. An attacker with this token could send emails on behalf of the organization's domain, enabling sophisticated phishing attacks, spreading malware, and causing severe reputational damage. The token could also be used to access and exfiltrate sensitive data, including customer email lists (PII) and internal email templates. The potential for direct impersonation and large-scale data theft makes this a critical risk.

title: Sentry API Key
description: Detected a Sentry API Key, risking unauthorized access to Sentry projects and sensitive application data.
helpText: A Sentry API Key (Auth Token) grants programmatic access to the Sentry API. Depending on its configured permissions, an attacker could use this token to read detailed error reports which often contain highly sensitive data, such as stack traces, request bodies, cookies, user PII, and even other application secrets. This access could lead to a significant data breach. An attacker might also be able to delete error data, hindering incident response, or modify project settings.

title: Serphouse API Key
description: Identified a Serphouse API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Serphouse services and data manipulation.
helpText: A compromised Serphouse API Key allows an attacker to make search requests using the legitimate user's account. This can lead to financial loss by exhausting the paid API request quota or incurring overage charges. This would also cause a denial of service for the feature relying on this API. However, it does not expose sensitive user data or grant access to critical systems.

title: SerpStack Access Key
description: Identified a potential SerpStack Access Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to SerpStack services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised SerpStack Access Key allows an attacker to make API requests on behalf of the user. This can lead to financial costs by exhausting the subscription's request quota and potentially incurring overage charges on paid plans. While it does not expose sensitive user data, the potential for service disruption (for features relying on SerpStack) and financial loss justifies a medium severity.

title: Shipday API Key
description: Identified a Shipday API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Shipday services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Shipday API Key provides complete programmatic access to an account's delivery operations. An attacker can use this key to read, create, update, and delete sensitive data, including customer PII (names, addresses, contact info), driver details, and order histories. This could lead to massive data breaches, operational chaos by manipulating or deleting orders, and reputational damage. The ability to exert full control over a core business function and its sensitive data justifies a critical severity rating.

title: Shodan API Key
description: Identified a potential Shodan API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Shodan services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Shodan API Key allows an attacker to perform extensive reconnaissance against any target, including the key owner's organization. They can discover internet-facing assets, identify open ports, and find known vulnerabilities. This information is highly valuable for planning a targeted attack. If the key is linked to a paid 'Shodan Monitor' account, an attacker could also view the specific assets the organization is monitoring, revealing their critical infrastructure and potentially disabling alerts, making this a significant intelligence leak.

title: Shopify Shared Secret
description: Identified a Shopify Shared Secret, posing a risk to application authentication and e-commerce platform security.
helpText: A Shopify Shared Secret is used to verify webhooks and sign OAuth requests. If compromised, an attacker can spoof genuine Shopify notifications, allowing them to trick your system into fulfilling unpaid orders or bypassing security checks for fraudulent transactions.

title: Shopify Access Token
description: Identified a Shopify Access Token, which could lead to unauthorized e-commerce platform access and data breaches.
helpText: A Shopify Access Token grants programmatic access to a store's data based on specific scopes. If leaked, an attacker can export customer lists, modify product prices, and access order history, leading to massive data breaches and potential loss of merchant reputation.

title: Shopify Custom Access Token
description: Detected a Shopify Custom Access Token, potentially compromising custom app integrations and e-commerce data security.
helpText: A Shopify Custom Access Token provides dedicated API access for store-specific integrations. Because these tokens are often 'permanent,' a leak allows for persistent unauthorized control over your inventory, customer records, and checkout settings until the app is manually uninstalled.

title: Shopify Private App Access Token
description: Identified a Shopify Private App Access Token (deprecated as of January 2022), which could lead to unauthorized access to private app data and store operations.
helpText: A Shopify Private App Token (legacy) grants broad, administrative access to a store's API. If leaked, it provides an attacker with a 'backdoor' to manipulate your entire storefront, steal payment metadata, and redirect customers to phishing sites—all while bypassing standard admin login protections.

title: Shortcut API Token
description: Detected a Shortcut API Token, potentially compromising project management and collaboration data.
helpText: A Shortcut API Token provides extensive programmatic access to a user's workspace. An attacker with this token could read all project data, including potentially sensitive information about unreleased features, security vulnerabilities, and strategic plans. They could also modify or delete data, such as stories and epics, which would severely disrupt the development workflow. This represents a significant risk of intellectual property theft and operational sabotage.

title: Shotstack API Key
description: Identified a Shotstack API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Shotstack services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A leaked Shotstack API Key allows an attacker to create video renders using the victim's account, potentially incurring significant financial costs or exhausting monthly quotas. It would also grant access to previously rendered videos and uploaded assets (images, audio, video clips), which could contain sensitive or proprietary information. This combination of potential financial abuse and data leakage within a third-party service warrants a MEDIUM severity.

title: Shutterstock Access Token
description: Detected a Shutterstock Access Token, risking unauthorized access to Shutterstock services and user data.
helpText: A Shutterstock Access Token allows an attacker to perform actions on behalf of the user. The most significant risk is financial, as the token can be used to license expensive media (images, videos, music) using the victim's account, which could incur substantial costs against their credit card or subscription plan. Additionally, an attacker could access the user's license history and manage their collections. The potential for direct and significant financial loss classifies this as a high-severity secret.

title: Signable API Token
description: Detected a Signable API Token, potentially compromising access to e-signature services and sensitive document data.
helpText: A Signable API Token grants complete programmatic access to an account. An attacker with this token could access, download, and delete all sent and signed documents, which often contain highly sensitive personal, financial, or legal information (PII). They could also send fraudulent documents on behalf of the user, leading to potential fraud and significant data breaches. This level of full access to a system managing legally-binding documents and their sensitive contents is critical.

title: Signalwire Access ID
description: Detected a Signalwire Access ID, which could compromise access to Signalwire services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Signalwire Access ID, when combined with an API token, grants full programmatic access to an organization's communication infrastructure. An attacker could send fraudulent SMS messages and make calls, leading to significant financial costs and reputational damage. More critically, they could access sensitive data such as call logs, message content, and voice recordings, resulting in a severe data breach. The ability to control and eavesdrop on a core business communication channel justifies a critical severity.

title: SignalWire Secret Key
description: Discovered a potential SignalWire Secret Key, risking unauthorized access to communication services and sensitive data.
helpText: A SignalWire Secret Key, combined with its Project ID, provides full programmatic access to an account's communication infrastructure. An attacker could use this key to send fraudulent SMS messages, make expensive international calls, or purchase phone numbers, leading to significant financial loss. They could also access and exfiltrate sensitive communication logs, including message content and call records, resulting in a severe data breach. The potential for direct financial abuse and access to sensitive PII makes this a critical secret.

title: SignalWire Sensitive URL
description: Uncovered a SignalWire Sensitive URL, which could expose internal endpoints or sensitive service access.
helpText: A SignalWire Sensitive URL typically embeds the Account SID and Auth Token, granting full programmatic access to the account. An attacker could use this to send messages, make calls, access sensitive call/message logs and recordings, and incur significant financial costs through toll fraud. This level of access to potentially sensitive communication data and the potential for major financial abuse makes this a critical exposure.

title: Signaturit Access Token
description: Identified a Signaturit Access Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to e-signature services and sensitive document data.
helpText: A Signaturit Access Token grants full programmatic access to a user's account. An attacker with this token could download all existing signed documents, which often contain highly sensitive personal information (PII) and confidential business data from legally binding contracts. They could also create, modify, or delete signature requests, leading to potential fraud, significant data breaches, and severe legal and reputational damage. The ability to access and manipulate such sensitive documents makes this a critical risk.

title: SignUpGenius API Key
description: Identified a potential SignUpGenius API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to SignUpGenius services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A leaked SignUpGenius API Key allows an attacker to programmatically access and manage sign-up data. This includes reading potentially sensitive personal information (PII) of all participants, such as names and email addresses. An attacker could also disrupt events by maliciously adding, modifying, or deleting sign-ups. The ability to exfiltrate PII and disrupt core service functionality warrants a high severity rating.

title: SimplyNoted API Token
description: Detected a SimplyNoted API Token, potentially risking unauthorized access to SimplyNoted services and sensitive data.
helpText: A SimplyNoted API Token grants an attacker the ability to perform significant actions on an account. They could exfiltrate sensitive Personally Identifiable Information (PII), including names, physical addresses, and the content of past messages, by accessing the user's order history and address book. Additionally, the attacker could send new notes at the account owner's expense, leading to financial loss and potential reputational damage.

title: Sirv Client ID
description: Identified a Sirv Client ID, which could lead to unauthorized access to Sirv services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Sirv Client ID is used with a Client Secret to generate an authentication token for its API. If an attacker obtains both, they can gain full administrative access to the account's digital assets. This allows them to read, download, modify, and delete all stored files, such as product images, marketing materials, or internal documents. This can lead to massive data exfiltration, permanent data loss, and complete disruption of any website or application relying on Sirv for its media content.

title: Sirv Client Secret
description: Discovered a potential Sirv Client Secret, which, if exposed, could allow unauthorized Sirv service access and data manipulation.
helpText: A Sirv Client ID and Client Secret are used to obtain an API access token. This token grants full administrative control over the Sirv account, including the ability to read, upload, modify, and delete all stored media assets. An attacker could destroy a website's visual content, exfiltrate potentially sensitive files, manage account users (enabling account takeover), and incur significant financial costs, making this a critical vulnerability.

title: Siteleaf API Key
description: Identified a Siteleaf API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Siteleaf services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Siteleaf credential (API key and secret) provides full programmatic access to a user's sites and content. An attacker can use this secret to read, create, modify, and delete all content, assets, and pages. Most importantly, they can trigger a publish, allowing them to deface the live website, destroy its content entirely, or use it to distribute malware. This level of control over a production website constitutes a critical risk.

title: Skrapp.io API Key
description: Identified a Skrapp.io API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Skrapp.io services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Skrapp.io API key provides programmatic access to find and verify email addresses. An attacker with this key could exhaust the account's paid credits, leading to financial loss. More importantly, the API can be used to access and exfiltrate the user's saved lead lists, which often contain valuable business intelligence and Personally Identifiable Information (PII) such as names, job titles, and contact details. This potential for significant data theft classifies the secret as HIGH severity.

title: SkyBiometry API Key
description: Identified a potential SkyBiometry API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to SkyBiometry services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A SkyBiometry API Key grants access to its face detection and recognition services. An attacker could use a compromised key to access, modify, or delete highly sensitive biometric data, including facial images and any associated PII stored by the user. This exposure constitutes a significant data breach. Additionally, the attacker could abuse the API, leading to financial costs by exhausting the account's quota or disrupting the legitimate service by corrupting the recognition database.

title: Slack Bot Token
description: Identified a Slack Bot Token, which may compromise bot integrations and communication channel security.
helpText: A Slack Bot Token grants programmatic access to act as an app. If leaked, an attacker can read message history, post as the bot, and access files within the channels where the bot is present, leading to data exfiltration and social engineering attacks.

title: Slack User Token
description: Identified a Slack User Token, posing a risk of unauthorized user impersonation and data access within Slack workspaces.
helpText: A Slack User Token allows an attacker to impersonate a specific user. This provides full access to that user's private DMs, private channel history, and the ability to post messages or delete data as if they were that individual.

title: Slack App Level Token
description: Detected a Slack App-level token, risking unauthorized access to Slack applications and workspace data.
helpText: A Slack App-Level Token grants access to organization-wide events and WebSocket connections. A leak allows an attacker to monitor app interactions across all workspaces in an Enterprise Grid, potentially exposing sensitive metadata and organizational workflows.

title: Slack Configuration Token
description: Detected a Slack Configuration Token, risking unauthorized access to Slack applications and workspace data.
helpText: A Slack Configuration Token allow for the remote management of an app's settings and permissions. If compromised, an attacker can modify app scopes, redirect webhooks, or hijack the app's functionality to gain deeper access to the workspace

title: Slack Configuration Refresh Token
description: Discovered a Slack Configuration refresh token, potentially allowing prolonged unauthorized access to configuration settings.
helpText: A Slack Configuration Refresh Token allow for the remote management of an app's settings and permissions. If compromised, an attacker can modify app scopes, redirect webhooks, or hijack the app's functionality to gain deeper access to the workspace

title: Slack Legacy Bot Token
description: Identified a Slack Legacy Bot Token (deprecated as of March 31, 2025), which could lead to compromised legacy bot operations and data exposure.
helpText: A Slack Legacy Bot Token (deprecated as of March 31, 2025) grants broad, often unscoped access to a workspace. Because they lack modern granular permissions, a leak typically gives an attacker wide-ranging power to read all public channels and perform administrative actions without restriction.

title: Slack Legacy Workspace Token
description: Identified a Slack Legacy Workspace Token (deprecated as of March 31, 2025), potentially compromising access to workspace data and legacy features.
helpText: A Slack Legacy Workspace Token (deprecated as of March 31, 2025) grants programmatic access to act as an app. If leaked, an attacker can read message history, post as the bot, and access files within the channels where the bot is present, leading to data exfiltration and social engineering attacks.

title: Slack Legacy Token
description: Detected a Slack Legacy Token (deprecated as of March 31, 2025), risking unauthorized access to older Slack integrations and user data.
helpText: A Slack Legacy Token (deprecated as of March 31, 2025) grants broad, often unscoped access to a workspace. Because they lack modern granular permissions, a leak typically gives an attacker wide-ranging power to read all public channels and perform administrative actions without restriction.

title: Slack Webhook URL
description: Discovered a Slack Webhook, which could lead to unauthorized message posting and data leakage in Slack channels.
helpText: A Slack Webhook URL allows anyone with the link to post messages directly into a specific channel. If leaked, an attacker can send malicious links, phishing attempts, or misinformation while appearing as a legitimate 'App' integration.

title: Smartsheet API Key
description: Identified a potential Smartsheet API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Smartsheet services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Smartsheet API Key grants programmatic access to the data and functionality of the associated user's account. An attacker could use this token to read, modify, exfiltrate, or delete sensitive business data, including project plans, customer information (PII), financial records, and internal documents. Given that Smartsheet often serves as a central hub for critical business operations, a compromised key could lead to severe data breaches, operational disruption, and significant reputational damage.

title: SmartyStreets Access ID
description: Identified a SmartyStreets Access ID, which could lead to unauthorized access to SmartyStreets services and sensitive location data.
helpText: A SmartyStreets Access ID is used with a secret token to authenticate to their API. If an attacker obtains both, they could perform address lookups at the owner's expense. This could lead to significant financial costs by exhausting paid quotas or incurring pay-as-you-go charges, and could cause a denial of service for the legitimate application. The potential for financial abuse and service disruption warrants a medium severity.

title: SmartyStreets API Key
description: Identified a SmartyStreets API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to SmartyStreets services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A SmartyStreets API Key authenticates requests for address validation services, which are typically paid per lookup. If compromised, an attacker could use the key to perform lookups against the owner's subscription, leading to financial loss by exhausting the purchased quota or incurring pay-as-you-go charges. This would also cause a denial of service for the legitimate application's address verification feature. The key does not grant access to sensitive user databases or critical infrastructure.

title: Smooch API Key
description: Identified a Smooch API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Smooch services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Smooch (Sunshine Conversations) API Key provides full administrative access to the associated account. An attacker with this key can read all historical and real-time customer conversations, which often contain sensitive PII. They can also impersonate the business by sending messages to customers, potentially for fraud or phishing, and can delete users or alter configurations, causing a complete outage of the company's messaging services. This level of access to sensitive data and critical communication infrastructure is considered critical.

title: Smooch Secret Key
description: Discovered a potential Smooch Secret Key, risking unauthorized access to communication services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Smooch (Zendesk Sunshine Conversations) Secret Key, paired with its Key ID, provides full administrative access to the associated messaging app via the API. An attacker can read all past and present user conversations, which may contain highly sensitive PII. They can also send messages on behalf of the business, impersonate users, delete data, and reconfigure integrations to intercept all future messages. This level of access can lead to massive data breaches, reputational damage, and complete disruption of customer communication channels, making its compromise critical.

title: Snyk API Token
description: Uncovered a Snyk API Token, potentially compromising software vulnerability scanning and code security.
helpText: A Snyk API Token provides programmatic access to the Snyk platform. An attacker with this token could view a comprehensive list of all known vulnerabilities across an organization's scanned projects. This information is extremely sensitive as it acts as a direct roadmap for exploiting unpatched security flaws in applications and infrastructure, potentially leading to a full system compromise. The token grants access to the organization's entire security posture, making it a critical asset.

title: SonarCloud API Token
description: Uncovered a SonarCloud API Token, potentially compromising software vulnerability scanning and code security.
helpText: A SonarCloud API Token grants access to the SonarCloud API with the permissions of the user who created it. A compromised token could allow an attacker to access sensitive information, including the full source code of private projects, detailed vulnerability reports, and security hotspots. This information could be used to identify and exploit weaknesses in the application, leading to a full system compromise or intellectual property theft. The direct access to source code and its known flaws makes this a critical exposure.

title: Sourcegraph Access Token
description: Identified a Sourcegraph Access Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to Sourcegraph services and data.
helpText: A Sourcegraph Access Token allows programmatic access to the Sourcegraph instance. Since Sourcegraph indexes an organization's private source code repositories, a compromised token would grant an attacker read access to all the source code that the token's owner can see. This could lead to the exfiltration of entire codebases, exposing intellectual property, business logic, and other embedded secrets, making it a critical risk.

title: SpeechText.AI API Key
description: Detected a SpeechText.AI API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to SpeechText.AI services and data exposure.
helpText: A leaked SpeechText.AI API Key allows an attacker to access, list, and delete all audio/video transcriptions associated with the account. Since these transcriptions can contain highly sensitive information such as private conversations, confidential business meetings, or personal data (PII), the potential for a severe data breach is high. Additionally, an attacker could abuse the key to incur financial costs by submitting their own files for transcription on the victim's account.

title: Splunk Observability API Key
description: Detected a Splunk Observability API Key, risking unauthorized access to monitoring and observability services.
helpText: A Splunk Observability API Key (or Org Access Token) is used to send data to the Splunk platform. A compromised key could allow an attacker to send false or malicious data (metrics, traces, logs), which could disrupt monitoring, obscure a real attack, or trigger false alerts. If the token has 'signalflow' permissions, it could also be used to read and exfiltrate sensitive operational and performance data, providing an attacker with valuable reconnaissance on the internal workings and potential vulnerabilities of the system. This level of access to sensitive operational data and potential for monitoring disruption warrants a HIGH severity.

title: Spoonacular API Key
description: Identified a Spoonacular API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Spoonacular services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A leaked Spoonacular API Key allows an attacker to make requests against the food API on behalf of the user. This can lead to financial costs by exhausting the quotas of a paid plan or cause a denial of service for applications relying on the API by using up the daily points on a free or paid plan. It does not provide access to sensitive user data, but the potential for financial impact and service disruption warrants a medium severity.

title: Sportsmonk API Token
description: Identified a Sportsmonk API Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to Sportsmonk services and sports data.
helpText: A Sportsmonk API Token grants access to their sports data API, which is a paid service with different subscription tiers. An attacker with this token could exhaust the API request quota of the legitimate user's plan, leading to financial loss or a denial of service for the application relying on this data. The data itself is not sensitive, but the potential for abuse of a paid service warrants a MEDIUM severity.

title: Square Access Token
description: Detected a Square Access Token, risking unauthorized payment processing and financial transaction exposure.
helpText: A Square Access Token provides programmatic access to a merchant's Square account. An attacker with this token could perform critical actions such as processing fraudulent payments, issuing refunds to their own accounts, accessing and exfiltrating sensitive customer PII, and manipulating business data like orders and product catalogs. The direct potential for financial theft and major data breaches makes this secret critical.

title: Square Client ID
description: Detected a potential Square Client ID, posing a risk of compromised SquareApp integrations and data breaches.
helpText: A Square Client ID (prefix 'sq0i') is a public identifier used to initiate the OAuth authorization flow. It is not a secret and is intended to be publicly visible, often embedded in client-side applications. It does not grant any access to sensitive data or payment functions on its own; a corresponding client secret is required for authentication. Therefore, its exposure carries a low risk.

title: Square Client Secret
description: Detected a potential Square Client Secret, which, if exposed, could allow unauthorized Square service access and data manipulation.
helpText: A Square Client Secret, such as an Access Token or Application Secret, provides direct, authenticated access to the Square API. Depending on its permissions, an attacker could process fraudulent payments, issue refunds, exfiltrate sensitive customer and transaction data, or disrupt business operations by modifying the item catalog. The potential for direct financial theft and a major data breach makes this a critical finding.

title: Statuscake API Token
description: Detected a Statuscake API Token, potentially compromising monitoring services and website status data.
helpText: A Statuscake API Token provides full programmatic control over a user's monitoring account. An attacker with this token can create, view, modify, and delete all uptime checks, effectively blinding an organization to real outages of their critical services. The attacker could also exfiltrate the list of monitored infrastructure endpoints and the contact details used for alerting. This ability to disrupt monitoring and exfiltrate sensitive operational data represents a significant security risk.

title: Statuspage API Key
description: Detected a Statuspage API Key, potentially compromising status page management and incident communication.
helpText: A Statuspage API Key grants programmatic access to manage a company's status page. An attacker could use this key to create false incidents, deface the page, or post misleading information, causing significant reputational damage and customer confusion. Crucially, the API can also be used to list all subscribers to the status page, potentially exposing sensitive user data like email addresses and phone numbers, leading to a PII breach.

title: Stitch Data Access Token
description: Identified a Stitch Data Access Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to Stitch Data services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Stitch Data Access Token provides administrative control over an organization's data pipelines via the Stitch API. An attacker with this token could view, modify, or delete data integrations, leading to a complete disruption of data flow to analytics platforms and causing data loss. More severely, they could extract sensitive data from connected sources (like production databases or SaaS tools like Salesforce) by triggering replication jobs. This level of control over critical data infrastructure warrants a critical severity.

title: Stockdata API Key
description: Identified a potential Stockdata API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Stockdata services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A leaked Stockdata API Key could be abused by an attacker to make requests against the user's paid subscription plan. This would lead to the exhaustion of API call quotas, potentially causing a denial of service for the legitimate application and incurring financial costs for the account owner if the plan has overage charges. The key does not grant access to sensitive personal data or critical systems, but the potential for financial impact and service disruption warrants a MEDIUM severity.

title: Storecove API Key
description: Uncovered a possible Storecove API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Storecove services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Storecove API Key provides full programmatic access to a company's invoicing system. An attacker with this key could read all incoming and outgoing invoices, exposing highly sensitive financial data, customer information, and transaction details. More importantly, an attacker could create and send fraudulent invoices on behalf of the company, potentially redirecting payments to their own accounts and causing direct, significant financial loss. The ability to manipulate a core financial process and steal funds makes this secret critical.

title: Stormboard API Key
description: Identified a Stormboard API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Stormboard services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Stormboard API Key grants programmatic access to a user's collaborative spaces, known as 'Storms'. An attacker with this key could read, modify, and delete sensitive information contained within these boards, such as business strategies, project plans, and intellectual property. This constitutes a significant data exfiltration and integrity risk, leading to a HIGH severity rating.

title: Stormglass API Key
description: Identified a Stormglass API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Stormglass services and sensitive weather data.
helpText: A leaked Stormglass API Key can be abused by an attacker to make requests against the user's account. This can lead to financial costs by exhausting the API request quota on a paid plan or cause a denial-of-service for the legitimate application relying on this data. The key does not grant access to sensitive data, but the potential for financial loss and service disruption gives it a medium severity.

title: Storyblok API Token
description: Uncovered a Storyblok API Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to content management services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Storyblok API Token, specifically a Personal Access Token (Management API), grants full programmatic read, write, and delete access to all content, assets, and settings within a Storyblok space. An attacker with this token could exfiltrate unpublished or sensitive content, deface a website by modifying published content, or cause a major service disruption by deleting all content and assets. This significant impact on data integrity and availability warrants a HIGH severity.

title: Strava Client ID
description: Discovered a potential Strava Client ID, risking unauthorized access to Strava integrations and user data.
helpText: A Strava Client ID is a public identifier for an application that integrates with the Strava API. It's used to initiate the OAuth 2.0 authentication flow, where a user grants the application permission to access their data. By itself, the Client ID is not a secret and does not grant any access to user data or protected API endpoints. It must be paired with a confidential Client Secret and a user's authorization to perform any meaningful actions. Since it is a public-facing identifier, its exposure carries minimal to no risk.

title: Strava Client Secret
description: Discovered a potential Strava Client Secret, which could lead to unauthorized access to Strava integrations and user data.
helpText: A Strava Client Secret is used in the OAuth 2.0 flow to authenticate an application when it exchanges a user's authorization code for an access token. An attacker with this secret can impersonate the legitimate application. If they can also intercept a user's authorization code (e.g., through phishing or another vulnerability), they can gain an access token and read sensitive user data, including personal profile information, private activities, and GPS location history. This potential for exfiltration of highly sensitive PII makes it a high-severity finding.

title: Stripe Secret Key
description: Found a Stripe Secret Key, posing a risk to payment processing services and sensitive financial data.
helpText: A Stripe Access Token with the pk_live_, sk_live_, rk_live prefix is a live, production key. An attacker with this key can perform any API request on behalf of the account, including creating charges, issuing refunds, accessing sensitive customer and transaction data, and initiating payouts to their own bank accounts. This represents a direct and immediate threat of significant financial theft and a major data breach.

title: Stripe Secret Key
description: Discovered a potential Stripe Secret Key, risking unauthorized access to payment processing services and sensitive financial data in a test environment.
helpText: A Stripe Sandbox Secret Key doesn't allow real changes to be made. In a sandbox, card networks and payment providers don’t process payments.

title: Stripo Access Token
description: Uncovered a possible Stripo Access Token, which may compromise access to Stripo API services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Stripo Access Token grants programmatic access to a user's account. An attacker could use this token to read, create, modify, or delete email templates. This could lead to the exfiltration of sensitive business information, disruption of marketing campaigns, or the injection of malicious content into emails sent to customers, representing a significant data and operational risk.

title: Supabase API Token
description: Detected a Supabase API Token, potentially compromising database access and sensitive data management.
helpText: A Supabase API Token with the 'sbp_' prefix is a Personal Access Token (PAT) for the Supabase Management API. This token grants administrative control over the user's entire account, allowing an attacker to create, delete, or modify projects, reset database passwords, and access all project data and secrets. A compromise of this token can lead to complete infrastructure destruction, total data exfiltration, and significant financial loss, making its exposure a critical risk.

title: Supernotes API Key
description: Identified a potential Supernotes API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Supernotes services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Supernotes API Key provides full programmatic access to a user's account. An attacker with this key can read, create, update, and delete all of the user's notes and associated data. Since notes can contain highly sensitive personal, financial, or proprietary information, a compromised key could lead to a significant data breach, exfiltration of sensitive content, and irreversible data loss for the user.

title: SurveySparrow API Token
description: Identified a SurveySparrow API Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to SurveySparrow services and sensitive data.
helpText: A SurveySparrow API Token provides extensive programmatic access to an account's resources. An attacker can use this token to read, modify, and delete all surveys, contacts (which can contain PII), and collected survey responses. This could lead to a significant data breach of potentially sensitive customer or employee information, as well as disruption of data collection activities by deleting data.

title: Survicate API Key
description: Identified a Survicate API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to survey data and analytics.
helpText: A Survicate API Key grants programmatic access to manage surveys, respondents, and retrieve survey responses. Since survey responses can contain sensitive customer feedback and Personally Identifiable Information (PII), a leaked key could lead to a significant data breach, allowing an attacker to exfiltrate sensitive user data. The ability to read this confidential information justifies a high severity rating.

title: Tatum.io API Key
description: Identified a Tatum.io API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Tatum.io services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Tatum.io API key provides programmatic access to manage blockchain operations, including creating wallets, managing private keys, and transferring cryptocurrency and NFTs. An attacker with this key could steal digital assets (cryptocurrency) from wallets associated with the account, leading to direct and potentially significant financial loss. This level of access to financial assets makes the key's exposure critical.

title: TaxJar API Key
description: Detected a TaxJar API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to tax calculation services and sensitive financial data.
helpText: A TaxJar API Key provides full programmatic access to a company's sales tax data. An attacker with this key can read, create, update, and delete tax transactions and customer information. This could lead to the corruption of financial records, resulting in incorrect tax filings, significant legal and financial penalties, and the exfiltration of sensitive customer data. The ability to manipulate core financial compliance data makes this a critical risk.

title: TechnicalAnalysisAPI API Key
description: Identified a potential TechnicalAnalysisAPI API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to financial analysis services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised TechnicalAnalysisAPI API Key could be used by an attacker to make requests on behalf of the user, potentially exhausting the API quota on a paid plan. This could lead to financial costs due to overages or a denial-of-service for the application relying on this data. The key does not grant access to sensitive user data or critical systems.

title: Telegram Bot Token
description: Detected a Telegram Bot Token, risking unauthorized bot operations and message interception on Telegram.
helpText: A leaked Telegram Bot Token grants an attacker complete control over the bot. They can read all messages sent to the bot (including in private chats and groups), send messages on the bot's behalf, and modify or delete the bot's messages. This could lead to the exfiltration of sensitive user data, PII, or confidential conversations, as well as impersonation for phishing or spreading misinformation.

title: Teletype API Token
description: Detected a Teletype API Token, risking unauthorized access to Teletype services and sensitive message data.
helpText: A Teletype API Token provides full programmatic access to the Dialogs API. An attacker could use this token to read all historical and ongoing customer conversations, potentially exposing vast amounts of sensitive data and PII. They could also send messages, impersonating the company to its customers for phishing or social engineering, and manage users and project settings. The potential for a massive data breach and severe reputational damage makes this a critical-severity secret.

title: Telnyx API Key
description: Identified a Telnyx API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Telnyx services and sensitive communication data.
helpText: A Telnyx API Key provides full programmatic access to an account's communication services. An attacker could exploit this key to send fraudulent SMS messages or make unauthorized calls, leading to significant financial loss. They could also access sensitive communication logs containing PII, or hijack phone numbers to intercept messages and calls (e.g., for 2FA bypasses), making it a critical risk.

title: Terraform Cloud Access Token
description: Detected a Terraform Cloud Personal Access Token, risking unauthorized access to Terraform Cloud services and infrastructure management.
helpText: A Terraform Cloud Personal Access Token provides API access with the same permissions as the user who created it. An attacker with this token could potentially trigger Terraform runs to modify or destroy critical infrastructure, read sensitive state files, and exfiltrate other secrets (like cloud provider credentials) stored as variables within Terraform Cloud. This represents a direct and high-impact threat to an organization's entire cloud environment.

title: TestingBot API Key
description: Identified a TestingBot API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to TestingBot services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A leaked TestingBot API Key allows an attacker to access detailed information about all tests run on the account, including test metadata, screenshots, and video recordings. This can expose sensitive pre-release application features, proprietary business logic, or internal test data. Additionally, an attacker can use the key to consume testing minutes by running their own tests, leading to financial loss and a denial of service for legitimate development and QA activities.

title: The Odds API Key
description: Identified a potential The Odds API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to sports data and services.
helpText: A compromised The Odds API key could allow an attacker to consume the API request quota associated with a paid plan. This would lead to a denial of service for the legitimate application relying on the odds data and could incur financial costs if the plan has overage charges. The key does not grant access to sensitive data or critical systems, making the impact primarily financial and operational for a single integration.

title: ThousandEyes Access ID
description: Identified a potential ThousandEyes Access ID (email), which could lead to unauthorized access to ThousandEyes services and sensitive data.
helpText: A ThousandEyes Access ID when paired with a password grants programmatic access to the network intelligence platform. An attacker could use this token to view sensitive network topology data, disrupt or delete critical performance and availability tests (blinding the organization to outages or attacks), and manipulate alert rules.

title: ThousandEyes API Key
description: Identified a ThousandEyes API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to ThousandEyes services and sensitive monitoring data.
helpText: A ThousandEyes API key provides programmatic access to an organization's network and application monitoring data. An attacker could use this key to exfiltrate sensitive network topology and performance information, which is valuable for reconnaissance. Furthermore, they could delete or alter monitoring tests, effectively blinding the organization to outages or performance degradation of its critical services. Depending on the user permissions associated with the key, the impact could range from data leakage to complete disruption of network visibility.

title: TicketMaster API Key
description: Identified a potential TicketMaster API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to TicketMaster services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Ticketmaster API Key grants read-only access to public data, such as event listings, venues, and attractions, via the Discovery API. It does not provide access to user accounts, purchasing capabilities, or any sensitive information. The primary risk of exposure is an attacker using the key to hit API rate limits, which could cause a minor, temporary denial of service for the application relying on it. The key can be easily revoked and replaced.

title: Tickettailor Secret Key
description: Discovered a potential Tickettailor Secret Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Tickettailor services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Tickettailor Secret Key provides full programmatic access to an organizer's account. An attacker can use this key to view, modify, or delete events, issue refunds for tickets causing direct financial loss, and access sensitive attendee data including Personally Identifiable Information (PII). This level of access allows for complete account takeover, data theft, and financial fraud, making its exposure a critical risk.

title: Tiingo API Key
description: Identified a Tiingo API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Tiingo services and financial data.
helpText: A compromised Tiingo API Key allows an attacker to access financial data feeds under the user's subscription. This can lead to the exhaustion of API quotas on a paid plan, resulting in a denial of service for the legitimate user's applications and potential minor financial loss due to the theft of a paid resource. The key does not expose sensitive user data (PII) or provide control over critical infrastructure.

title: TimeCamp API Key
description: Identified a TimeCamp API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to TimeCamp services and sensitive data.
helpText: A TimeCamp API Key provides extensive programmatic access to an organization's account. An attacker could use this key to read, create, modify, or delete sensitive business data, including time entries, project details, client lists, user information (PII), and invoices. This could lead to significant data exfiltration, disruption of business operations by destroying records, and potential financial fraud through invoice manipulation. The level of access to sensitive operational and user data warrants a high severity rating.

title: Tmetric API Token
description: Detected a Tmetric API Token, potentially compromising access to time tracking and project management data.
helpText: A Tmetric API Token provides extensive programmatic access to a user's workspace. An attacker with this token could read, create, modify, and delete sensitive business data, including time entries, project details, client lists, and invoices. This could lead to the exfiltration of confidential business information, financial data, and PII, as well as the disruption of billing and payroll processes by manipulating time records. The potential for a significant data breach and operational disruption warrants a HIGH severity rating.

title: Todoist API Key
description: Uncovered a possible Todoist API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Todoist projects and sensitive task information.
helpText: A Todoist API Key grants full programmatic access to a user's account. An attacker with this key can read, create, modify, and delete all of the user's tasks, projects, and comments. This could lead to the exfiltration of highly sensitive personal or business information, as well as significant data loss and disruption for the user. While the scope is limited to a single user's account, the potential for a complete compromise of their data warrants a high severity rating.

title: Tokeet Access ID
description: Identified a Tokeet Access ID, which could lead to unauthorized access to Tokeet services and sensitive account data.
helpText: A Tokeet Access ID, when paired with an API Key grants extensive programmatic access to a user's vacation rental management account. An attacker could use this key to view, modify, or delete rental listings and bookings; access sensitive guest PII (personally identifiable information) like names and contact details; and potentially manipulate financial data such as invoices and payments.

title: Tokeet API Key
description: Identified a Tokeet API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Tokeet services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Tokeet API Key provides extensive programmatic access to a user's rental management account. An attacker with this key could read sensitive guest PII (names, contact info), view and manipulate bookings, and most critically, initiate financial transactions by creating charges against guest payment methods. The ability to directly access sensitive personal data and cause financial theft makes this a critical-risk secret.

title: Tomorrow.io API Key
description: Identified a potential Tomorrow.io API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Tomorrow.io services and sensitive weather data.
helpText: A compromised Tomorrow.io API key allows an attacker to make requests for weather data. If the key is associated with a paid plan, this could lead to financial loss by exhausting the API request quota or incurring overage charges. This would also cause a denial-of-service for the application's weather-related features. However, it does not grant access to sensitive user or application data.

title: TomTom API Key
description: Identified a potential TomTom API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to TomTom services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A leaked TomTom API Key can be used by an attacker to make requests against the owner's account. This can lead to financial costs by exhausting the free quota and incurring charges on a pay-as-you-go plan. It can also cause a denial of service for the legitimate application by using up the daily API request limit. The key does not typically expose sensitive user data, but the potential for service disruption and financial loss warrants a medium severity.

title: Tradier API Token
description: Identified a Tradier API Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to financial trading services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Tradier API Token provides programmatic access to a user's brokerage account. An attacker with this token could view sensitive financial information like account balances, positions, and transaction history. Most critically, they could execute trades, potentially liquidating the account's assets or making unauthorized transactions, leading to direct and significant financial loss.

title: TravelPayouts API Token
description: Uncovered a possible TravelPayouts API Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to travel data and services.
helpText: A TravelPayouts API Token grants access to sensitive affiliate data, including performance statistics like bookings, clicks, and crucially, income details. An attacker with this token could exfiltrate confidential business and financial information, gaining insight into an affiliate's revenue and business strategy. This exposure of sensitive financial data represents a significant breach of confidentiality, making the severity HIGH.

title: Travis CI Access Token
description: Identified a Travis CI Access Token, potentially compromising continuous integration services and codebase security.
helpText: A Travis CI Access Token provides API access to the CI/CD pipeline. An attacker could use this token to read sensitive information from build logs, exfiltrate secrets stored as environment variables (e.g., cloud credentials, database passwords), or trigger malicious builds. This could lead to a full supply chain attack, allowing an attacker to inject malicious code into the application or gain access to production environments. Due to its ability to compromise the software build process and expose other critical secrets, its severity is CRITICAL.

title: Trello API Key
description: Identified a Trello API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Trello boards and sensitive project data.
helpText: A Trello API Key is a public identifier for an application. By itself, it does not grant access to any user data. To access a user's Trello boards and cards, an attacker would also need a separate, user-authorized token. Since the API key alone provides no access to sensitive information or functionality, its exposure represents a low security risk.

title: Trello API Token
description: Detected a Trello API Token, potentially compromising access to Trello boards and sensitive data.
helpText: A Trello API Token grants programmatic access to a user's account, including all boards, lists, and cards they have permission to view and edit. An attacker with this token could read sensitive project data, customer information, internal discussions, and potentially even other secrets stored on boards. They could also modify or delete data, causing significant disruption to projects and workflows. The potential for sensitive data exfiltration and service disruption makes this a high-severity finding.

title: Twilio API Key
description: Identified a Twilio API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Twilio services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Twilio API Key grants full programmatic control over your account's communication services. If leaked, an attacker can intercept private messages, spoof your phone numbers to send spam, and trigger 'toll fraud'—sending thousands of high-cost international calls or SMS in seconds, which can result in massive, immediate charges on your billing account.

title: Twist Access Token
description: Detected a Twist Access Token, risking unauthorized access to Twist services and user data.
helpText: A Twist Access Token provides API access to a user's account. An attacker with this token could read sensitive private conversations and direct messages, exfiltrating proprietary information, internal discussions, or PII. Depending on the token's scope, they could also impersonate the user by posting messages, potentially spreading misinformation or malicious links within the organization. This level of access to sensitive communications constitutes a high-severity risk.

title: Twitch Client Secret
description: Identified a Twitch Client Secret, which may lead to unauthorized integrations and data exposure in Twitch applications.
helpText: A Twitch Client Secret is used with a Client ID to authenticate an application and obtain access tokens. An attacker with this secret can impersonate the application. While an app access token generated with this secret only provides access to public data, the primary risk lies in its use to obtain user access tokens. If an attacker can also trick a user into authorizing the application (e.g., through phishing), they can exchange the authorization code for a user access token, potentially gaining access to sensitive user data and the ability to perform actions on behalf of the user, such as modifying their channel.

title: Typeform API Token
description: Uncovered a Typeform API Token, which could lead to unauthorized survey management and data collection.
helpText: A leaked Typeform API Token grants full programmatic access to an account's forms, themes, and responses. An attacker can use this token to retrieve all submitted responses, which often contain sensitive user data and Personally Identifiable Information (PII). Furthermore, they can create, modify, or delete forms and all associated data, or alter webhooks to exfiltrate new submissions in real-time. This direct access to read, exfiltrate, and destroy sensitive user data constitutes a critical security risk.

title: Ubidots API Key
description: Identified a potential Ubidots API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Ubidots services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Ubidots API Key (prefix BBFF-) grants full programmatic access to an account's data sources, variables, and events. An attacker with this token can read potentially sensitive sensor data (e.g., location tracking, industrial process data) and, more importantly, write or modify data. Injecting false data can disrupt operations, trigger false alarms, or be used to manipulate physical systems controlled by the IoT devices, leading to potentially severe real-world consequences. This level of read/write access to critical operational data justifies a CRITICAL severity.

title: uClassify API Key
description: Identified a uClassify API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to classification services and sensitive data processing.
helpText: A compromised uClassify API Key allows an attacker to interact with text classifiers. A write-access key could be used to delete or maliciously alter a user's trained models, disrupting application logic (e.g., a spam filter or sentiment analysis). It could also expose the training data, which may be sensitive. At a minimum, the key could be abused to exhaust API quotas, leading to financial costs or denial of service for the classification feature.

title: Unplugg Access Token
description: Uncovered a possible Unplugg Access Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to Unplugg services and data.
helpText: An Unplugg Access Token grants programmatic access to the core functionalities of an e-commerce store. An attacker with this token could potentially read, create, update, and delete sensitive customer data (PII), order information, and product catalogs. This could lead to massive data breaches, fraudulent orders, and complete disruption of business operations, representing a direct and severe threat to the business.

title: Unsplash Client ID
description: Detected an Unsplash Client ID, which could lead to unauthorized access to Unsplash services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An Unsplash Client ID, also known as an Access Key, is a public identifier used to access Unsplash's public API endpoints. It does not provide access to any sensitive user data or private account information. The Unsplash documentation explicitly states that this key is not a secret and can be embedded in client-side code. The only potential for abuse is exhausting the very low public rate limit (50 requests/hour), causing a minor, temporary disruption to the service's ability to fetch images. Therefore, the impact of exposure is minimal.

title: Unsplash Secret Key
description: Detected an Unsplash Secret Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Unsplash services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An Unsplash Secret Key is a secret key used to access Unsplash's private API endpoints. It is used to authenticate requests to the Unsplash API and is not a public identifier.

title: UPCDatabase API Key
description: Identified a potential UPCDatabase API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to UPCDatabase services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A UPCDatabase API Key grants access to query public product information based on barcodes. A leaked key could be used by an attacker to perform lookups, potentially exhausting the API quota on the user's plan. However, it does not expose sensitive data, PII, or provide access to modify systems. The impact is limited to potential service disruption for the API user or minor costs if a paid plan's quota is exceeded, making it a low severity risk.

title: UploadCare API Key
description: Uncovered a possible UploadCare API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to UploadCare services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: An UploadCare Secret API Key grants full administrative access to an UploadCare project's files. An attacker with this key can list, download, modify, and permanently delete all stored files, which could include sensitive user data, private documents, or critical application assets. This could lead to a catastrophic data breach, irreversible data loss, and a complete service disruption for any application relying on these files.

title: UptimeRobot API Key
description: Identified an UptimeRobot API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to UptimeRobot monitoring services and sensitive data.
helpText: A compromised UptimeRobot API Key allows an attacker to view, create, modify, and delete all monitoring checks. This can lead to a significant information leak, as an attacker could discover a company's entire monitored infrastructure (servers, websites, APIs). Furthermore, an attacker could delete all monitors, effectively blinding the organization to real-time outages, which could be a precursor to a larger attack. This ability to disrupt critical operational monitoring and exfiltrate infrastructure data warrants a high severity rating.

title: Upwave API Token
description: Detected an Upwave API Token, potentially risking unauthorized access to Upwave services and data.
helpText: An Upwave API Token provides full programmatic access to a user's account. An attacker with this token could read, modify, and delete all data the user has access to, including projects, tasks, comments, and attachments. This could lead to the exfiltration of sensitive business plans, intellectual property, and internal communications, as well as severe disruption to business operations by destroying project data. The potential for a complete compromise of a critical business application makes this a critical risk.

title: urlscan.io API Key
description: Identified a potential urlscan.io API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to urlscan.io services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised urlscan.io API Key allows an attacker to submit URLs for scanning under the victim's account, potentially exhausting API quotas and incurring costs on paid plans. More importantly, it grants access to the user's scan history, which could expose sensitive information such as unlisted websites, internal applications, or security investigation targets that have been scanned by the user.

title: UserStack Access Key
description: Identified a UserStack Access Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to UserStack services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised UserStack Access Key allows an attacker to make requests to the API. This does not expose sensitive user data, but if the key is for a paid plan, an attacker could exhaust the API request quota. This would cause a denial of service for the feature relying on the API and could lead to financial costs for the account owner.

title: Vagrant Cloud Personal Access Token
description: Detected a Vagrant Cloud Personal Access Token, risking unauthorized access to Vagrant Cloud services and resources.
helpText: A Vagrant Cloud Personal Access Token grants full API access with the same permissions as the user it belongs to. An attacker with this token could delete, modify, or replace existing Vagrant boxes. The ability to upload a malicious version of a box creates a severe supply chain attack vector. Anyone who pulls and runs the compromised box (e.g., developers, CI/CD pipelines) could unknowingly execute malicious code, leading to widespread system compromise.

title: VatLayer API Key
description: Identified a potential VatLayer API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to VatLayer services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised VatLayer API Key allows an attacker to perform VAT number validations and rate lookups. If the key is associated with a paid plan, an attacker could exhaust the monthly request quota, leading to a denial of service for the legitimate application and potentially incurring financial costs through overage fees. The key does not grant access to sensitive user data.

title: Vbout API Key
description: Identified a Vbout API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Vbout services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Vbout API Key provides extensive programmatic access to a user's marketing data and operations. An attacker with this key could exfiltrate sensitive customer contact lists (PII), send unauthorized emails or social media posts, disrupt marketing campaigns, and access e-commerce data. This constitutes a significant data breach risk and can lead to major reputational damage.

title: Vercel Access Token
description: Detected a Vercel Access Token, risking unauthorized access to Vercel services and deployments.
helpText: A Vercel Access Token provides programmatic access to the Vercel API. An attacker with this token could deploy malicious code to production, delete entire projects and deployments, and access or modify sensitive environment variables, which often contain other secrets like database credentials or third-party API keys. This level of control over production infrastructure and data warrants a critical severity.

title: Veriphone API Key
description: Identified a Veriphone API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Veriphone services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Veriphone API Key allows users to validate phone numbers. An attacker with this key could abuse it to exhaust the API quota, leading to a denial of service for the legitimate application and potential financial costs for the account owner if it's on a paid plan. While it doesn't expose stored sensitive data, it can be used to validate lists of phone numbers.

title: Viewneo Access Token
description: Detected a Viewneo Access Token, risking unauthorized access to Viewneo services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Viewneo Access Token grants full administrative control over an account, including the ability to create, modify, and delete all content, playlists, devices, and users. An attacker with this token could cause a complete service disruption by deleting all assets or inflict severe reputational damage by displaying malicious or inappropriate content on public-facing screens. The ability to completely take over a public communication system is a critical risk.

title: VirusTotal API Key
description: Identified a potential VirusTotal API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to VirusTotal services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A VirusTotal API Key allows for programmatic interaction with the service. While a free, public key has limited impact (rate-limiting abuse), a compromised private (Enterprise) API key is a significant threat. An attacker could access premium features, but more critically, they could search for and download sensitive files and intellectual property that the organization previously submitted for analysis. This potential for sensitive data exfiltration classifies the severity as high.

title: Visual Crossing API Key
description: Identified a potential Visual Crossing API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to weather data and services.
helpText: A Visual Crossing API Key allows users to fetch weather data. A leaked key does not expose sensitive user data but could be abused by an attacker to make requests against the owner's account. This could lead to financial costs by exceeding paid plan quotas or cause a denial of service for the application's weather-related features by exhausting the API limit.

title: Voiceflow API Key
description: Detected a Voiceflow API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Voiceflow services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Voiceflow API Key (prefix `vf.`) provides programmatic access to a workspace, allowing an attacker to manage projects, agents, and knowledge bases. Most importantly, it can be used to read all conversation transcripts, which may contain highly sensitive Personally Identifiable Information (PII) or other confidential data shared by users. An attacker could also modify or delete the AI agents, causing service disruption or making the agent provide malicious responses. The potential for a major data breach and application compromise makes this a critical risk.

title: Voicegain Access Token
description: Detected a Voicegain Access Token, risking unauthorized access to Voicegain services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Voicegain Access Token (JWT) grants full programmatic access to the associated account's API. An attacker could use this token to access, download, and delete sensitive audio recordings and their transcriptions. This data could contain PII, confidential business information, or other sensitive content. The attacker could also submit new jobs, leading to financial loss by exhausting paid quotas and causing a denial of service for the legitimate user. The potential for a significant sensitive data breach makes this a high-severity finding.

title: Vpnapi.io API Key
description: Identified a potential Vpnapi.io API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to VPN API services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A leaked Vpnapi.io API Key can be used to make requests against the owner's account. This could lead to the exhaustion of API request quotas, causing a denial of service for the legitimate application relying on this API. For paid plans, an attacker's usage could also result in unexpected financial costs for the account owner. The key does not expose sensitive user data or grant access to core systems.

title: Vultr API Key
description: Identified a potential Vultr API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Vultr cloud services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Vultr API Key provides full programmatic control over a user's cloud account.
