An attacker with this key could create, modify, or delete critical infrastructure like servers and databases, leading to service disruption and data loss. They could also provision expensive resources, resulting in significant financial costs, or access sensitive data stored on Vultr's services. The ability to completely manage a cloud environment makes this a critical exposure.

title: Vyte API Key
description: Identified a potential Vyte API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Vyte services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Vyte API Key provides access to a user's scheduling data. An attacker could use this key to read sensitive meeting details, access contact lists, and create or cancel appointments on the user's behalf. This represents a significant breach of personal data (PII) and could lead to service disruption, making the severity HIGH.

title: Walk Score API Key
description: Identified a potential Walk Score API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to WalkScore services and sensitive location data.
helpText: A compromised Walk Score API Key allows an attacker to make requests on behalf of the legitimate user. Since Walk Score offers paid plans for commercial use or high-volume requests, an attacker could abuse the key to incur financial costs for the owner. For free-tier keys, the abuse would lead to hitting the daily API quota, causing a denial of service for the specific feature. The key does not grant access to sensitive data, but the potential for financial loss warrants a MEDIUM severity.

title: WeatherBit API Key
description: Identified a potential WeatherBit API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to WeatherBit services and sensitive weather data exposure.
helpText: A WeatherBit API Key is used to access weather data endpoints. If compromised, an attacker could use the key to make requests, potentially exhausting the API quota on the user's paid plan. This could lead to minor financial loss through overage charges and cause a denial of service for the application relying on the weather data. The key does not grant access to sensitive user information or account management.

title: WeatherStack API Key
description: Identified a potential WeatherStack API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to WeatherStack services and sensitive weather data.
helpText: A compromised WeatherStack API Key grants access to make requests on behalf of the subscribed user. An attacker could abuse this key to exhaust the API request quota, causing a denial of service for the weather-related features of the application. If the account is on a paid plan, this abuse could also lead to unexpected financial costs. The key does not expose any sensitive user data.

title: Web3.Storage Access Token
description: Detected a Web3.Storage Access Token, potentially compromising access to Web3.Storage services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Web3.Storage Access Token grants full programmatic control over an account's data. An attacker can upload arbitrary data, potentially incurring significant storage costs for the victim. They can also list all data previously uploaded by the account, leading to a leak of file identifiers (CIDs), and remove existing uploads from the account's management list, causing data availability issues for applications relying on the service. This combination of potential financial abuse, data leakage, and service disruption warrants a high severity rating.

title: Webflow API Token
description: Uncovered a Webflow API Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to Webflow services and website management.
helpText: A Webflow API Token provides programmatic access to a Webflow site. Depending on its permissions, an attacker could read, modify, or delete all CMS content, including blog posts and pages. For e-commerce sites, the token could be used to access sensitive customer and order data (PII) or manipulate products and inventory. An attacker could also publish malicious changes, effectively defacing or taking over the live website. This level of control over a production website and its sensitive data constitutes a critical risk.

title: Web Scraper API Token
description: Detected a Web Scraper API Token, potentially compromising web scraping services and data collection.
helpText: A Web Scraper API Token grants full programmatic access to a user's account. An attacker could use this token to download all previously scraped data, which may contain sensitive PII, competitive business intelligence, or other proprietary information. The token also allows an attacker to delete sitemaps and scraping jobs, causing data loss and service disruption, and to run new jobs, which would incur costs for the victim. The primary risk is the potential for significant data exfiltration.

title: Websitepulse Access ID
description: Identified a Websitepulse Access ID, which could lead to unauthorized access to Websitepulse services and sensitive contact data.
helpText: A Websitepulse Access ID, when paired with its corresponding Secret Key, grants full programmatic access to a user's monitoring configuration. An attacker could view the entire list of monitored servers and websites, which is sensitive infrastructure information. More critically, they could add, modify, or delete monitoring targets, effectively blinding the legitimate user to outages or performance degradation. This could be used to conceal a separate attack, making it a high-severity threat.

title: Websitepulse API Key
description: Identified a potential Websitepulse API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Websitepulse services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Websitepulse API Key grants full programmatic control over an account's monitoring setup. An attacker could delete existing monitors, effectively blinding the user to outages or performance issues on their critical infrastructure. They could also read sensitive operational data, such as server statuses, performance metrics, and error logs, which could be used to plan further attacks. The ability to disrupt a core security and operational function like monitoring warrants a high severity rating.

title: Whoxy API Key
description: Identified a potential Whoxy API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Whoxy services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A compromised Whoxy API Key can be used to make queries against their WHOIS database, billing them to the victim's account. This can lead to direct financial loss by exhausting the paid query quota and potentially incurring overage fees. It also causes a denial-of-service for the legitimate application that relies on this key. As the key's abuse leads to financial costs and service disruption but does not expose sensitive user data or system access, its severity is MEDIUM.

title: Worksnaps API Key
description: Identified a Worksnaps API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Worksnaps services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Worksnaps API Key provides access to highly sensitive employee and project data. An attacker could exfiltrate time logs, project details, and, most critically, screenshots of employee desktops, leading to a major breach of corporate and personal privacy. The API may also allow for modification or deletion of data, enabling an attacker to disrupt business operations that rely on this time-tracking information.

title: WorldCoinIndex API Key
description: Identified a potential WorldCoinIndex API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to WorldCoinIndex services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A WorldCoinIndex API Key is used to access cryptocurrency market data. A compromised key, especially one tied to a paid plan, could be abused by an attacker to exhaust API request quotas. This could lead to financial costs from overage fees or cause a denial of service for application components that rely on this data. The key does not grant access to sensitive user data or trading capabilities.

title: World Weather Online API Key
description: Identified a potential World Weather Online API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to weather data services and sensitive information exposure.
helpText: A compromised World Weather Online API Key allows an attacker to make requests on behalf of the user. This can lead to financial loss by exhausting the query quota on a paid plan or cause a denial of service for the application relying on the weather data. The key does not grant access to sensitive user data, but the potential for financial abuse and service disruption warrants a medium severity.

title: Yandex API Key
description: Discovered a Yandex API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Yandex services and data manipulation.
helpText: A Yandex API Key can provide programmatic access to a wide variety of services. The most severe case is a key for Yandex.Cloud, which would grant an attacker control over cloud resources similar to AWS or GCP. This includes managing virtual machines, databases, and object storage, potentially leading to complete infrastructure compromise, sensitive data exfiltration, and significant financial loss. Due to this potential for direct control over critical infrastructure, the severity is classified as CRITICAL.

title: Yelp API Token
description: Identified a Yelp API Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to Yelp services and data.
helpText: A Yelp API Token provides read-only access to public business data, such as listings, reviews, and photos. It does not grant access to any private user data or provide write permissions. A leaked token could be abused by an attacker to hit the daily API call limit, causing a temporary denial of service for the application relying on it, but the impact is minimal as no sensitive data is exposed and no direct financial loss is incurred.

title: YNAB Access Token
description: Detected a YNAB Access Token, risking unauthorized access to financial management services and sensitive budget data.
helpText: A YNAB access token grants full programmatic access to a user's entire budget. According to the YNAB API documentation, this includes the ability to read all linked accounts, balances, and a complete history of transactions, as well as create or modify transactions. An attacker with this token could exfiltrate a victim's complete financial history, which is extremely sensitive personal data. This exposure could lead to identity theft, targeted phishing attacks, or blackmail. The ability to read and write such sensitive financial data warrants a critical severity.

title: YouSign API Key
description: Uncovered a possible YouSign API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to YouSign services and sensitive data.
helpText: A YouSign API Key grants full programmatic access to an organization's e-signature environment. An attacker could use this key to read, download, or delete highly sensitive documents such as contracts, legal agreements, and financial records. They could also initiate fraudulent signature requests, disrupt business operations by canceling legitimate requests, or manage users. The potential for a major data breach of sensitive information and facilitating fraud makes this a critical risk.

title: Youtube Access ID
description: Detected a YouTube Access ID, which could lead to unauthorized access to YouTube channel data and services.
helpText: A YouTube Access ID when paired with an API Key provides access to the YouTube Data API. While it typically only allows access to public data, an attacker can abuse a leaked key to exhaust the API's usage quota. This would cause a denial of service for the legitimate application relying on the key. If the Google Cloud project is linked to a billing account, this abuse could also incur financial costs.

title: Youtube API Key
description: Detected a YouTube API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to YouTube services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A YouTube API Key is used to access public data via the YouTube Data API. It does not grant access to private user information or allow management actions like uploading or deleting videos, which require OAuth 2.0. However, a leaked key can be abused by an attacker to make requests against the API, exhausting the daily quota. This can lead to a denial of service for the legitimate application and potentially incur financial costs for the project owner if the usage exceeds the free tier.

title: Zenkit API Key
description: Detected a potential Zenkit API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Zenkit services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Zenkit API Key grants full programmatic access to the associated user's account. An attacker with this key could read, create, modify, and delete all data within the user's Zenkit workspaces, including tasks, files, and comments. This could lead to the exfiltration of sensitive business information, intellectual property, or personal data, and cause significant disruption to project management and team collaboration.

title: ZenRows API Key
description: Detected a ZenRows API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to ZenRows services and data scraping operations.
helpText: A compromised ZenRows API Key allows an attacker to use the account's paid subscription to perform web scraping. This can lead to financial loss by consuming the user's API request quota or incurring overage charges. It also creates a denial-of-service risk for the legitimate user's applications that rely on ZenRows. The key does not grant direct access to sensitive data, but the potential for financial abuse and service disruption warrants a medium severity.

title: Zenscrape API Key
description: Identified a Zenscrape API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Zenscrape services and data scraping operations.
helpText: A Zenscrape API Key allows users to perform web scraping requests through their service. If compromised, an attacker could abuse the key to exhaust the user's request quota on their paid plan. This would result in a denial of service for the legitimate application relying on Zenscrape and could incur financial costs for the account owner, but it does not expose sensitive user data or core infrastructure.

title: Zenserp API Key
description: Identified a Zenserp API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Zenserp services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A Zenserp API Key is used to access their paid search scraping service. If leaked, an attacker could exhaust the user's API request quota, leading to direct financial costs from overages or forcing a plan upgrade. It could also cause a denial of service for any application relying on Zenserp. The key does not expose sensitive user data, but the potential for financial loss and service disruption justifies a medium severity.

title: Zeplin Access Token
description: Uncovered a Zeplin Access Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to Zeplin design collaboration services and sensitive project data.
helpText: A Zeplin Access Token provides programmatic access to a user's projects, styleguides, and design assets. An attacker could use this token to exfiltrate sensitive intellectual property, such as unreleased product designs and proprietary UI/UX details. While it doesn't grant access to production systems or source code, the potential for significant intellectual property theft warrants a high severity.

title: ZeroTier API Token
description: Identified a ZeroTier API Token, which could lead to unauthorized access to ZeroTier network management services and sensitive network configurations.
helpText: A ZeroTier API Token provides full administrative control over a user's virtual networks. An attacker with this token can create or delete networks, modify network rules and routing, and—most critically—authorize their own malicious devices onto private networks or de-authorize legitimate ones. This allows for direct network intrusion, lateral movement to compromise connected systems, traffic interception, and complete denial of service. The ability to fundamentally control network infrastructure access makes this a critical risk.

title: ZipAPI Access ID
description: Detected a ZipAPI email pattern, which could be used as an access ID for authentication, risking unauthorized access to ZipAPI services.
helpText: A leaked ZipAPI Access ID could allow an attacker to make unauthorized queries against the service. If the key is associated with a paid plan, this could lead to financial costs from quota exhaustion or overage charges. It could also cause a denial of service for the application relying on this API for ZIP code lookups. The key does not provide access to sensitive data.

title: ZipAPI API Key
description: Identified a potential ZipAPI API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to ZipAPI services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A ZipAPI Key grants access to an API for looking up US ZIP code information. The data provided is public and not sensitive. While a leaked key could be abused by an attacker to exhaust the API request quota on a paid plan, leading to a denial of service for the legitimate application and potentially minor financial costs, it does not expose any sensitive data or provide access to critical systems. The impact is minimal and contained.

title: ZipAPI Password
description: Discovered a potential ZipAPI Password, risking unauthorized access to ZipAPI services and sensitive data.
helpText: A compromised ZipAPI Key/Password allows an attacker to make requests to the ZipAPI service. Since ZipAPI operates on a tiered pricing model with request quotas, an attacker could exhaust the account's quota, leading to a denial of service for the application's features that rely on it and potentially incurring financial costs for the owner.

title: ZipCodeAPI API Key
description: Identified a potential ZipCodeAPI API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to ZipCodeAPI services and sensitive location data exposure.
helpText: A compromised ZipCodeAPI API Key allows an attacker to make requests on behalf of the user. This could be used to exhaust the request quota on a free or paid plan, leading to a denial of service for the legitimate application. For users on paid plans, this abuse could also result in unexpected financial costs. The key does not expose sensitive user data.

title: Zipcodebase API Key
description: Identified a Zipcodebase API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Zipcodebase services and sensitive data exposure.
helpText: A leaked Zipcodebase API Key does not grant access to sensitive user data. However, the service operates on a tiered pricing model based on API request volume. An attacker could abuse the key to make a large number of requests, potentially exhausting the monthly quota and incurring financial costs for the legitimate owner through overage charges or forcing an upgrade to a more expensive plan. This constitutes a potential for minor financial loss and a denial-of-service for the feature relying on this API.

title: ZulipChat Access ID
description: Identified a ZulipChat Access ID (email), which could lead to unauthorized access to ZulipChat accounts and communication data.
helpText: A Zulip access id can refer to unique identifiers like User IDs, Stream IDs, or API keys, used for authentication, API calls. Zulip's documentation states the API key is used as the password for HTTP Basic Authentication. An attacker can use this key to read all private messages and messages in subscribed channels, exfiltrating potentially sensitive corporate data, intellectual property, or other credentials. The key also allows the attacker to send messages as the compromised user, enabling social engineering attacks or spreading misinformation within the organization. This level of access to a central communication platform represents a critical threat.

title: Zulip API Key
description: Detected a Zulip API Key, which could lead to unauthorized access to Zulip services and sensitive data.
helpText: A Zulip API Key provides full programmatic access to a user's account. An attacker with this key can read all the user's private messages and messages in streams they have access to, send messages on their behalf, and download files. This could lead to the exfiltration of sensitive internal communications, intellectual property, or other credentials, and enable social engineering attacks within the organization. The potential for a significant data breach and impersonation justifies a high severity rating.

title: Zulip Sensitive URL
description: Detected a Zulip domain pattern, which could expose sensitive API endpoints and facilitate unauthorized access when combined with other credentials.
helpText: A sensitive Zulip URL, such as an incoming webhook URL, allows anyone who has it to post messages to a specific channel or user within a Zulip organization. An attacker could exploit this to send spam, spread misinformation, or conduct highly effective phishing attacks by impersonating a trusted service or user. This could lead to credential theft or malware infection within the organization, posing a significant security risk as it bypasses traditional email security and abuses an internal, trusted communication channel.

title: Clojars API Token
description: Uncovered a possible Clojars API token, risking unauthorized access to Clojure libraries and potential code manipulation.
helpText: A Clojars API Token provides programmatic access to publish and manage Clojure and ClojureScript libraries on the Clojars repository. If leaked, an attacker can bypass standard authentication to upload malicious updates to your existing packages. This enables a devastating 'supply chain' attack, where downstream developers and automated build systems unknowingly pull and execute the compromised code, potentially breaching countless connected applications and environments.

title: Crates.IO API Token
description: Uncovered a possible Crates.IO API token, risking unauthorized publishing or yanking of Rust packages and potential supply chain compromise.
helpText: A Crates.IO API Token provides programmatic access to publish, manage, and yank Rust packages (crates) on the official Rust registry. If leaked, an attacker can bypass standard authentication to upload malicious updates to your existing crates. This enables a devastating 'supply chain' attack, where downstream developers and automated build systems unknowingly compile and execute the compromised Rust code, potentially breaching countless connected applications and environments.

title: Dropbox API Token
description: Identified a Dropbox API App Key or Secret, which could lead to unauthorized file access and data breaches in Dropbox storage.
helpText: A Dropbox API Token provides programmatic access to read, write, and manage files within a user's or team's Dropbox workspace. If leaked, an attacker can bypass standard authentication to quietly exfiltrate sensitive corporate documents, intellectual property, or PII. Furthermore, an attacker could alter or replace files with malicious payloads, leveraging your trusted file-sharing infrastructure to distribute malware or ransomware internally and to downstream clients.

title: Dropbox Long-Lived API Token
description: Detected a legacy Dropbox Long-Lived API Token, risking permanent, unauthorized file access and data exfiltration from your Dropbox account.
helpText: A Dropbox Long-Lived API Token provides permanent, programmatic access to a Dropbox workspace. Unlike modern tokens, these do not expire automatically. If leaked, an attacker gains persistent access to exfiltrate sensitive documents, intellectual property, or PII. They can also alter files to distribute malicious payloads, leveraging your trusted file-sharing infrastructure to spread malware or ransomware internally and to external clients.

title: Dropbox Short-Lived API Token
description: Detected a Dropbox Short-Lived API Token, potentially risking temporary file access and rapid data exfiltration from your Dropbox account.
helpText: A Dropbox Short-Lived API Token provides temporary (usually 4-hour) programmatic access to a Dropbox workspace. While the window of opportunity is small, if caught actively by an attacker, they can rapidly exfiltrate sensitive documents or inject malicious payloads into shared folders. Automated attacks can leverage this brief access to scrape highly sensitive PII or intellectual property before the token naturally expires.

title: Dynatrace API Token
description: Detected a Dynatrace API token, potentially risking application performance monitoring and data exposure.
helpText: A Dynatrace API Token provides programmatic access to your organization's observability platform and Application Performance Monitoring (APM) environment. If leaked, an attacker can bypass standard authentication to exfiltrate highly sensitive telemetry data, which frequently includes application logs, user session data, and plain-text environmental variables. Additionally, an attacker could maliciously alter or disable alerting rules to 'blind' your security and operations teams, effectively covering their tracks while they execute a broader breach.

title: Alibaba Access Key ID
description: Detected an Alibaba Cloud Access Key ID, posing a risk of unauthorized cloud resource access and potential data compromise.
helpText: An Alibaba Cloud Access Key ID acts as a public identifier for a programmatic user or role. While it requires the corresponding Secret Key to authenticate, exposing the AccessKey ID hands attackers half of the puzzle. It allows malicious actors to conduct targeted reconnaissance, identify the specific Alibaba Cloud (Aliyun) tenant, and aggressively hunt for the matching secret in your surrounding repositories. If paired with the leaked secret, an attacker gains deep programmatic access to provision rogue infrastructure (like cryptomining botnets), permanently delete critical resources, or exfiltrate sensitive databases from OSS and RDS.

title: Alibaba Secret Key
description: Detected an Alibaba Cloud Secret Key, posing a risk of unauthorized cloud resource access and potential data compromise.
helpText: An Alibaba Cloud Secret Key, when paired with an AccessKey ID, provides deep programmatic access to an Alibaba Cloud (Aliyun) environment. If leaked, an attacker can bypass standard authentication to provision unauthorized infrastructure (such as cryptomining botnets), exfiltrate sensitive databases from OSS or RDS, or permanently delete critical cloud resources, resulting in devastating operational and financial damage.

title: Anthropic Admin API Key
description: Detected an Anthropic Admin API Key, risking unauthorized access to administrative functions and sensitive AI model configurations.
helpText: An Anthropic Admin API Key grants elevated, programmatic access to an organization's Anthropic workspace and Claude AI models. Unlike a standard token, an admin key allows attackers to manage billing, invite malicious users, and alter workspace configurations. Furthermore, they can abuse the API to process enormous volumes of data at your expense, or intercept highly sensitive proprietary data, PII, and source code previously fed into the models by legitimate internal users.

title: Atlassian Classic API Token
description: Detected a Classic Atlassian API Token, posing a threat to project management and collaboration tool security and data confidentiality.
helpText: An Atlassian Classic API Token grants programmatic access to a user's Atlassian suite, including Jira, Confluence, and Bitbucket. If leaked, an attacker can bypass standard authentication to scrape highly sensitive internal documentation, IT support tickets, and proprietary source code. This internal knowledge acts as a roadmap for lateral movement, allowing the attacker to identify infrastructure vulnerabilities, extract hardcoded secrets from tickets, and deeply compromise the broader corporate network.

title: Atlassian API Token
description: Detected an Atlassian API token, posing a threat to project management and collaboration tool security and data confidentiality.
helpText: A modern Atlassian API Token grants scoped or full programmatic access to an organization's Atlassian suite (Jira, Confluence, etc.). If leaked, an attacker can bypass standard authentication to silently scrape internal wikis, strategic roadmaps, and IT incident reports. This exposes a wealth of operational intelligence, providing a devastating roadmap for lateral movement where attackers can discover infrastructure flaws, extract credentials from issue trackers, and execute a broader network breach.

title: BitBucket Client ID
description: Detected a BitBucket OAuth Client ID, risking targeted phishing or reconnaissance against your source code management integrations.
helpText: A BitBucket Client ID (OAuth Consumer Key) acts as a public identifier for a custom application integrated with BitBucket. While it cannot authenticate on its own, exposing it allows attackers to conduct reconnaissance on your internal tooling. If an attacker also obtains the matching Client Secret, they can impersonate your application to hijack OAuth flows, tricking users into granting malicious access to private repositories, pull requests, and proprietary source code.

title: BitBucket Client Secret
description: Detected a BitBucket OAuth Client Secret, risking application impersonation and unauthorized access to private source code repositories.
helpText: A BitBucket Client Secret, when paired with its Client ID, authenticates an OAuth application to the BitBucket API. If leaked, an attacker can completely impersonate your trusted internal application. This allows them to forge OAuth tokens, bypass standard authentication, and gain deep programmatic access to scrape proprietary source code, tamper with deployment scripts, or inject malicious code into private repositories, setting the stage for a devastating supply chain attack.

title: CircleCI Personal Access Token
description: Detected a CircleCI Personal Access Token (PAT), risking unauthorized CI/CD pipeline execution and severe supply chain compromise across multiple projects.
helpText: A CircleCI Personal Access Token (PAT) inherits the permissions of the user who created it, providing programmatic access across multiple projects and organizations. If leaked, an attacker can bypass standard authentication to trigger unauthorized builds, inject malicious code into your CI/CD pipelines, and extract highly sensitive production secrets (like cloud credentials) injected during the build process. This enables a severe supply chain attack and direct lateral movement into your production infrastructure.

title: CircleCI Project Token
description: Detected a CircleCI Project Token, risking unauthorized pipeline access and malicious deployment artifact tampering.
helpText: A CircleCI Project Token provides scoped programmatic access to a specific repository's CI/CD pipeline. While limited to a single project, a leaked token still allows an attacker to trigger malicious builds, poison deployment artifacts, and exfiltrate production secrets tied specifically to that pipeline. This completely compromises the integrity of the affected project and provides a dangerous stepping stone for lateral movement into the environments where the project is deployed.

title: Cohere API Key
description: Identified a Cohere API Key, posing a risk of unauthorized access to AI services and data manipulation.
helpText: A Cohere API Key provides programmatic access to an organization's AI models, embeddings, and fine-tuning datasets. If leaked, an attacker can bypass standard authentication to scrape highly sensitive proprietary data or PII previously fed into the models. Furthermore, attackers can hijack your trusted infrastructure to generate malicious content, poison your Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) datasets, or consume massive amounts of API quota, resulting in significant financial damage.

title: Databricks Personal Access Token
description: Detected a Databricks Personal Access Token (PAT), risking unauthorized access to data workspaces, clusters, and sensitive data.
helpText: A Databricks Personal Access Token (PAT) grants programmatic access to a Databricks workspace with the permissions of the user who generated it. If leaked, an attacker can execute arbitrary code on your clusters, spin up expensive compute resources, or exfiltrate massive amounts of sensitive data from connected data lakes and databases.

title: Databricks OAuth Client Secret
description: Detected a Databricks OAuth Client Secret, risking automated Machine-to-Machine (M2M) unauthorized access to data lakes and compute clusters.
helpText: A Databricks OAuth Client Secret (DOSE token) is used by Service Principals to authenticate Machine-to-Machine (M2M) workloads. If leaked, an attacker can completely impersonate your automated systems to silently spin up expensive compute resources or exfiltrate massive volumes of proprietary data from Unity Catalog and connected data lakes, bypassing standard human identity checks.

title: Docker Hub Personal Access Token
description: Identified a Docker Hub PAT, risking unauthorized container registry access and severe supply chain compromise.
helpText: A Docker Hub Personal Access Token (PAT) grants programmatic access to an organization's Docker container registries. If leaked, an attacker can bypass standard authentication to pull private, proprietary images (which frequently contain hardcoded secrets or proprietary source code). Crucially, an attacker could also push malicious, backdoored images over your trusted tags (such as 'latest'). This triggers a devastating supply chain attack where your CI/CD pipelines and Kubernetes clusters unknowingly pull and execute the compromised containers in production environments.

title: GCP API Key
description: Detected a Google API Key, risking unauthorized access to GCP resources, AI models, and severe quota exhaustion.
helpText: A GCP API Key provides programmatic access to an organization's Google Cloud services, which can include GCP infrastructure, Google Maps, or generative AI models like Gemini. If leaked, an attacker can bypass standard authentication to make unauthorized API calls. Because these keys are often tied to billing accounts, an attacker can rapidly consume your API quota, resulting in massive financial charges. Furthermore, if the key is unrestricted, it could be used to manipulate connected cloud resources or exfiltrate data from loosely permissioned storage buckets.

title: GitLab CICD Job Token
description: Identified a GitLab CI/CD Job Token, risking unauthorized internal API access and source code exposure during pipeline execution.
helpText: A GitLab CI/CD Job Token provides temporary programmatic access during a pipeline run. If leaked, an attacker can access the same internal APIs and project repositories as the running job. This allows them to download sensitive source code, access internal container registries, or potentially poison build artifacts before they are deployed.

title: GitLab Deploy Token
description: Identified a GitLab Deploy Token, risking unauthorized read/write access to repositories, package registries, and severe supply chain attacks.
helpText: A GitLab Deploy Token provides long-lived, scoped access to read or write to a repository and its associated package/container registries. If leaked, an attacker can silently pull proprietary source code. Worse, if the token has write access, they can replace legitimate packages or containers with malicious versions, triggering a devastating supply chain attack against any environment pulling from that registry.

title: GitLab Feature Flag Client Token
description: Identified a GitLab Feature Flag Client Token, risking exposure of unreleased application features and internal operational configurations.
helpText: A GitLab Feature Flag Client Token allows an application to communicate with GitLab's feature flag API. If leaked, an attacker can query your application's feature flags and map out unreleased features, internal user lists, or hidden endpoints. While the operational impact is limited, it provides valuable reconnaissance data for targeted attacks.

title: GitLab Feed Token
description: Identified a GitLab Feed Token, risking unauthorized read-only access to sensitive project discussions and user activity.
helpText: A GitLab Feed Token authenticates RSS feeds for issues, merge requests, and activity. If leaked, an attacker can subscribe to these feeds to scrape sensitive internal discussions, project roadmaps, and bug reports, exposing proprietary operational intelligence without requiring an active GitLab account.

title: GitLab Incoming Mail Token
description: Identified a GitLab Incoming Mail Token, risking unauthorized manipulation of issues and merge requests via email spoofing.
helpText: A GitLab Incoming Mail Token allows users to create issues or merge requests via email. If leaked, an attacker can impersonate legitimate users to inject spam, phishing links, or malicious payloads directly into your project's issue tracker, potentially tricking developers into clicking harmful links or downloading backdoored files.

title: GitLab Kubernetes Agent Token
description: Identified a GitLab Kubernetes Agent token, risking direct compromise of connected Kubernetes clusters and GitOps workflows.
helpText: A GitLab Kubernetes Agent Token connects a GitLab instance directly to a Kubernetes cluster for GitOps deployments. If leaked, an attacker compromises the bridge between your source control and production infrastructure. They could potentially extract cluster credentials, manipulate live deployments, or leverage the agent to pivot directly into the connected Kubernetes cluster.

title: GitLab OAUTH App Secret
description: Identified a GitLab OIDC Application Secret, risking OAuth app impersonation and unauthorized access to user accounts and repositories.
helpText: A GitLab OAuth Application Secret authenticates external apps to GitLab's OAuth/OIDC provider. If leaked, an attacker can completely impersonate your trusted application. This allows them to forge OAuth tokens, bypass standard authentication, and trick users into granting malicious access to their private GitLab repositories and account data.

title: GitLab Personal Access Token Routable
description: Identified a routable GitLab PAT, risking unauthorized access to partitioned GitLab instances, source code theft, and codebase manipulation.
helpText: A routable GitLab Personal Access Token (PAT) grants programmatic access to a partitioned GitLab instance on behalf of a specific user. If leaked, an attacker inherits the exact permissions of the victim, allowing them to bypass authentication to steal proprietary source code, alter CI/CD pipelines, and inject malicious code into production repositories.

title: GitLab Pipeline Trigger Token
description: Found a GitLab Pipeline Trigger Token, risking unauthorized CI/CD pipeline execution and potential variable manipulation.
helpText: A GitLab Pipeline Trigger Token allows programmatic triggering of a specific project's CI/CD pipeline via an API call. If leaked, an attacker can repeatedly trigger unauthorized builds, potentially exhausting CI runner minutes (denial-of-service) or manipulating pipeline variables to force the build process to expose hardcoded secrets.

title: GitLab Runner Registration Token
description: Discovered a GitLab Runner Registration Token, risking the attachment of rogue runners and total compromise of CI/CD secrets.
helpText: A GitLab Runner Registration Token is used to attach new compute nodes (runners) to a project or instance. If leaked, an attacker can register their own malicious runner to your environment. Your GitLab instance will then blindly send upcoming CI/CD jobs—along with all injected production deployment secrets and source code—directly to the attacker's machine.

title: GitLab Runner Authentication Token
description: Discovered a GitLab Runner Authentication Token, risking CI/CD pipeline interception and production secret extraction.
helpText: A GitLab Runner Authentication Token is used by an already-registered CI/CD runner to request jobs from the GitLab instance. If leaked, an attacker can impersonate a legitimate runner to intercept and execute your CI/CD pipelines. This exposes your proprietary source code, build artifacts, and highly sensitive production deployment secrets directly to the attacker.

title: GitLab Runner Authentication Token Routable
description: Discovered a routable GitLab Runner Authentication Token, risking CI/CD pipeline interception and production secret extraction in partitioned instances.
helpText: A routable GitLab Runner Authentication Token is used by a registered CI/CD runner to pull jobs in massive, partitioned GitLab environments. If leaked, an attacker can hijack the runner's identity, intercepting your CI/CD pipelines. This grants them direct access to steal source code, intercept build artifacts, and exfiltrate critical cloud secrets injected during your deployment process.

title: GitLab SCIM Token
description: Discovered a GitLab SCIM Token, risking unauthorized identity provisioning and widespread organizational takeover.
helpText: A GitLab SCIM Token provides programmatic access to manage users and identities across a GitLab Group or instance. If leaked, an attacker can bypass Identity Providers (like Azure AD or Okta) to provision rogue administrator accounts, delete legitimate users, or modify access controls, leading to an immediate and widespread organizational takeover.

title: GitLab Session Cookie
description: Discovered a GitLab Session Cookie, risking immediate session hijacking and full user account impersonation.
helpText: A GitLab Session Cookie represents an active, authenticated web session for a user. If leaked (e.g., via Cross-Site Scripting or log exposure), an attacker can inject the cookie into their own browser to perform a complete session hijacking. They will immediately gain access to the victim's GitLab account via the web UI without needing a password or 2FA, allowing full codebase manipulation.

title: Grafana API Key
description: Detected a legacy Grafana API Key, risking unauthorized access to monitoring dashboards and connected data sources.
helpText: A Grafana API Key (legacy) provides programmatic access to a specific Grafana instance. If leaked, an attacker can bypass standard authentication to manipulate monitoring dashboards or alter alerting rules to 'blind' your security teams. Crucially, because Grafana acts as a proxy to your underlying data sources, an attacker can use this token to query and exfiltrate sensitive operational data directly from your connected databases (like Prometheus, MySQL, or Elasticsearch).

title: Grafana Cloud API Token
description: Found a Grafana Cloud API token, risking unauthorized SaaS infrastructure management and severe quota exhaustion.
helpText: A Grafana Cloud API Token (Access Policy Token) grants programmatic control over your organization's overarching Grafana Cloud (SaaS) account. Unlike instance-level keys, this token can be used to spin up or permanently delete entire Grafana deployments, manage users, or flood your centralized logging (Loki) and metrics endpoints, resulting in massive financial charges and complete observability outages.

title: Harness API Key
description: Identified a Harness Access Token (PAT or SAT), risking unauthorized access to a Harness account.
helpText: A Harness Access Token (Personal or Service Account) grants programmatic control over your software delivery pipelines. If leaked, an attacker can bypass standard deployment approvals to inject malicious code directly into your production environments. Furthermore, because Harness manages cloud deployments, the attacker can leverage this pipeline access to extract highly sensitive, injected cloud credentials (like AWS keys or Kubernetes configs), enabling widespread lateral movement.

title: Hashicorp Tf API Token
description: Uncovered a HashiCorp Terraform user/org API token, which may lead to unauthorized infrastructure management and security breaches.
helpText: A HashiCorp Terraform API Token grants programmatic access to Terraform Cloud or Enterprise workspaces. If leaked, an attacker can provision rogue cloud infrastructure, modify existing routing rules to intercept traffic, or completely destroy your production environments. Crucially, this token also grants access to Terraform 'state' files, which notoriously store all deployed database passwords, API keys, and cloud credentials in plain text, handing the attacker the keys to the entire kingdom.

title: HashiCorp Vault API Token
description: Identified a HashiCorp Vault token, risking unauthorized access to centralized secrets management and massive credential exposure.
helpText: HashiCorp Vault is a centralized secrets management system designed to protect an organization's most critical credentials. If a Vault token (such as a Service, Batch, or Recovery token) is leaked, an attacker gains programmatic access to query and extract the secrets stored within. This effectively acts as a 'master key,' allowing the attacker to steal database passwords, cloud IAM keys, and TLS certificates, enabling devastating lateral movement across your entire infrastructure.

title: Intercom Access Token
description: Detected an Intercom Access Token, risking unauthorized access to customer communication data and private chat history.
helpText: An Intercom Access Token grants programmatic access to an Intercom workspace's API. If leaked, an attacker can bypass standard authentication to read private customer chats, exfiltrate sensitive PII (Personally Identifiable Information) of your user base, or send unauthorized messages on behalf of your customer support team.

title: Heartland Secret API Key
description: Detected a Heartland Secret API Key, risking unauthorized payment processing, fraudulent refunds, and financial data exposure.
helpText: A Heartland Secret Key (starting with skapi_) is a highly privileged credential meant only for backend servers. If leaked, an attacker can completely bypass standard authentication to issue fraudulent refunds, charge saved customer payment methods, and extract sensitive transaction histories, leading to immediate financial theft and PCI-DSS compliance violations.

title: Heartland Public API Key
description: Detected a Heartland Public API Key. While meant for frontend use, hardcoding should be reviewed for proper environment variable management.
helpText: A Heartland Public Key (starting with pkapi_) is designed to be public and is used by frontend clients to tokenize payment details. While exposing it does not allow an attacker to process payments or steal money, hardcoding it directly into source code is an anti-pattern. It should be injected via environment variables to cleanly separate certification and production payment environments.

title: xAI API Key
description: Detected an xAI API Key, risking unauthorized access to Grok AI models and severe quota exhaustion.
helpText: An xAI API Key provides programmatic access to xAI's Grok models (like Grok-3 and Grok-Vision). If leaked, an attacker can bypass standard authentication to make unauthorized inference requests, rapidly consuming your API quota and incurring significant financial charges. They could also potentially hijack your trusted integration to generate malicious content or scrape your proprietary prompt data.

title: xAI Management Key
description: Detected an xAI Management Key, risking unauthorized administrative control over your AI infrastructure and billing.
helpText: An xAI Management Key (often used in enterprise proxy setups or custom wrappers) grants elevated administrative control over an AI workspace. If leaked, an attacker can manipulate billing limits, delete fine-tuned datasets, exfiltrate usage logs, or provision unauthorized API keys, leading to a complete compromise of your AI ecosystem.

title: Groq API Key
description: Detected a Groq API Key, risking unauthorized access to ultra-fast AI inference resources and severe API quota exhaustion.
helpText: A Groq API Key provides programmatic access to Groq's high-speed Language Processing Unit (LPU) inference engine. If leaked, an attacker can bypass authentication to route massive LLM inference workloads through your account. Because Groq processes tokens exceptionally fast, an attacker can rapidly drain your API quota and rack up massive financial charges in a very short window. They could also potentially hijack your trusted integration to generate malicious content.

title: Ionic Personal Access Token
description: Detected an Ionic Personal Access Token, risking unauthorized access to Appflow CI/CD pipelines and mobile cloud builds.
helpText: An Ionic Personal Access Token (frequently used as the IONIC_TOKEN environment variable) authenticates the Ionic CLI or third-party applications to Ionic Appflow. If leaked, an attacker can hijack your mobile CI/CD pipelines, access proprietary mobile application source code, or trigger malicious cloud builds that could be deployed directly to iOS and Android app stores.

title: LangSmith V2 API Key
description: Detected a modern LangSmith V2 API Key, risking unauthorized access to AI observability data, prompts, and datasets.
helpText: A LangSmith V2 key (Personal Access Token prefixed with lsv2_pt_ or Service Key prefixed with lsv2_sk_) grants programmatic access to an organization's LangChain observability platform. If leaked, an attacker can extract proprietary prompt templates, read sensitive customer conversations logged in your LLM traces, or manipulate evaluation datasets used to train your AI agents.

title: LangSmith Legacy API Key
description: Detected a legacy LangSmith API Key. While deprecated in cloud environments, it may still compromise self-hosted instances.
helpText: A legacy LangSmith API key (prefixed with ls__). LangChain officially deprecated these for cloud users in October 2024. While a leaked key will likely be rejected by LangSmith Cloud, it could still be actively used to compromise older, self-hosted enterprise deployments of the platform, granting attackers access to LLM traces and prompts.

title: Mapbox Secret Access Token
description: Detected a Mapbox Secret Access Token, risking unauthorized modification of map resources and severe API billing abuse.
helpText: A Mapbox Secret Access Token is a highly privileged credential designed strictly for server-side applications. It can contain powerful secret scopes (like `styles:write`, `datasets:write`, or `downloads:read`). If leaked, an attacker can permanently delete your custom maps, alter geospatial datasets, and consume expensive paid APIs (like Geocoding or Matrix routing), resulting in severe billing charges and operational disruption.

title: Mapbox Public Access Token
description: Detected a Mapbox Public Access Token. While meant for frontend use, unprotected keys can lead to quota theft and billing abuse.
helpText: A Mapbox Public Access Token (starting with pk.) is designed to be used in client-side applications (like mobile apps or web browsers) and only contains read-only public scopes. While an attacker cannot modify or delete your Mapbox resources with this key, an unprotected public token can be stolen and embedded in a third-party application. If the token does not have proper URL/domain restrictions configured in the Mapbox console, attackers can drain your free tier and run up your billing quota.

title: Moneywave API Key
description: Detected a Moneywave API Key. While often used as a public identifier, exposure aids attackers in targeted API abuse.
helpText: A Moneywave API Key acts as the public-facing identifier for your Flutterwave/Moneywave account. While it typically cannot process financial transactions on its own without the accompanying Secret Key, exposing it provides attackers with half the required credentials for authentication and gives them a direct target for reconnaissance and credential-stuffing attacks.

title: Moneywave Secret Key
description: Detected a Moneywave Secret Key, risking unauthorized financial transactions, access to payment data, and severe billing abuse.
helpText: A Moneywave Secret Key grants programmatic, cryptographic access to a Flutterwave/Moneywave payment processing workspace. If leaked, an attacker can bypass standard authentication to initiate unauthorized mass payouts, issue fraudulent refunds, or extract highly sensitive customer financial data (PII) from your connected bank accounts, leading to direct monetary theft and PCI-DSS compliance violations.

title: New Relic Admin Key
description: Detected a New Relic Admin Key, risking unauthorized administrative access and complete control over the New Relic account.
helpText: A New Relic Admin Key grants broad administrative control over an entire New Relic account. If leaked, an attacker can manage users, modify or delete critical data, alter alerting policies, and potentially extract sensitive performance or business data from connected applications.

title: New Relic API Service Key
description: Detected a New Relic API Service Key, risking unauthorized access to the New Relic REST API and performance data.
helpText: A New Relic API Service Key (or User API Key) allows programmatic access to view data and configure settings via the New Relic REST/NerdGraph APIs. If leaked, an attacker could access sensitive telemetry data, alter monitoring configurations, or disable alerting mechanisms to mask an ongoing attack.

title: New Relic APM License Key
description: Detected a New Relic APM License Key, risking unauthorized data ingestion and billing abuse.
helpText: A New Relic APM License Key is used by agents to report application performance data to New Relic servers. While primarily a write-only key used for data ingestion, exposing it allows an attacker to flood your monitoring platform with fake telemetry data (ruining analytics) or incur massive data ingestion charges on your billing account.

title: New Relic Insights Insert Key
description: Detected a New Relic Insights Insert Key, risking unauthorized injection of custom telemetry and event data.
helpText: A New Relic Insights Insert Key allows systems to send custom event data directly to New Relic Insights. If leaked, an attacker can pollute your dashboards by injecting false operational metrics, logs, or security events, potentially triggering false alarms or hiding malicious activity in a sea of noise.

title: New Relic Insights Query Key
description: Detected a New Relic Insights Query Key, risking unauthorized access and extraction of sensitive custom event data.
helpText: A New Relic Insights Query Key grants read-only access to query custom events and telemetry stored in New Relic Insights. If leaked, an attacker can extract proprietary business metrics, application logs, and potentially PII that has been ingested into the observability platform.

title: New Relic Synthetics Private Location Key
description: Detected a New Relic Synthetics Private Location Key, risking unauthorized submission of synthetic monitoring data.
helpText: A New Relic Synthetics Private Location Key allows private test nodes (minions) to report synthetic monitoring results back to New Relic. If leaked, an attacker can inject fraudulent uptime or performance metrics, manipulating SLAs and potentially masking real downtime or incidents from the operations team.

title: NuGet API Key
description: Detected a raw NuGet API Key, risking unauthorized package publishing and supply chain attacks.
helpText: A NuGet API key allows programmatic access to push, delete, or unlist packages on NuGet.org or a private NuGet feed. If leaked, an attacker can push malicious updates to your libraries, triggering a devastating supply chain attack for anyone downloading your automated updates.

title: Okta API Token
description: Detected an Okta API Token, risking unauthorized administrative access to an Okta identity workspace.
helpText: An Okta API Token (SSWS) grants programmatic access to an Okta organization's API. If leaked, an attacker can bypass standard authentication to manage users, modify security policies, provision rogue administrator accounts, and potentially gain access to all connected applications via identity provider hijacking.

title: Okta OAuth Client Secret
description: Detected an Okta OAuth Client Secret, risking application spoofing and unauthorized authentication flows.
helpText: An Okta Client Secret is used alongside a Client ID to authenticate an application to Okta's OAuth/OIDC provider. If leaked, an attacker can spoof your application, intercept OAuth authorization codes, and forge authentication tokens, leading to potential account takeovers for users logging into your service.

title: OpenRouter API Key
description: Detected an OpenRouter API Key, risking unauthorized access to hundreds of premium AI models and severe credit exhaustion.
helpText: An OpenRouter API Key grants programmatic access to a centralized marketplace of hundreds of LLMs. Because a single key unlocks the entire catalog, if leaked, an attacker can bypass standard authentication to route massive automated inference workloads to the most expensive premium models available, rapidly draining your prepaid credits.

title: Pinecone Legacy API Key
description: Detected a legacy Pinecone API Key (UUID), risking unauthorized access to vector databases and AI embeddings.
helpText: A Pinecone API Key (Legacy) if leaked, an attacker can query, poison, or delete your AI vector embeddings, directly compromising any RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) infrastructure relying on the database.

title: Pinecone API Key
description: Detected a modern Pinecone V2 API Key, risking unauthorized access to vector databases and AI infrastructure.
helpText: A Pinecone V2 API Key grants programmatic access to modern Pinecone serverless or pod-based indexes. If leaked, an attacker can bypass standard authentication to extract proprietary dataset embeddings, inject malicious vectors (data poisoning), or permanently drop entire indexes, causing severe outages for your AI applications.

title: Plaid Access Token
description: Detected a Plaid Access Token, risking unauthorized access to a connected bank account and transaction history.
helpText: A Plaid Access Token permanently links a specific user's bank account to your application. If leaked, an attacker can continuously pull real-time bank balances, massive transaction histories, and routing numbers.

title: Plaid Client ID
description: Detected a Plaid Client ID. While not a secret on its own, it aids attackers in targeted API abuse when combined with a secret key.
helpText: A Plaid Client ID is used as a public identifier for your Plaid environment. It cannot be used to extract data on its own, but hardcoding it is an anti-pattern. It is required alongside the Plaid Secret to make API calls.

title: Plaid Secret Key
description: Detected a Plaid Secret Key, risking complete administrative compromise of your financial service integrations.
helpText: A Plaid Secret Key acts as your master password for a specific Plaid environment (Sandbox, Development, or Production). If leaked alongside a Client ID, an attacker can generate new access tokens, provision fraudulent bank links, and extract massive amounts of financial PII.

title: PlanetScale API Token
description: Detected a PlanetScale API Token, risking unauthorized access to database management and operations.
helpText: A PlanetScale API Token grants programmatic control over a PlanetScale MySQL organization. If leaked, an attacker can delete production branches, alter database schemas, and generate new database passwords, leading to total infrastructure compromise.

title: PlanetScale OAuth Token
description: Detected a PlanetScale OAuth Token, risking unauthorized access to database resources via third-party integrations.
helpText: A PlanetScale OAuth Token is used by third-party applications to act on behalf of a PlanetScale user. Exposing this token allows attackers to hijack that delegated access, potentially compromising the specific databases the user has access to.

title: PlanetScale Database Password
description: Detected a PlanetScale Database Password, risking direct unauthorized access to production database records.
helpText: A PlanetScale Password (pscale_pw_) grants direct, immediate connection rights to a specific database branch. If leaked, an attacker can connect via any standard MySQL client to dump tables, steal user records, or permanently drop your data.

title: Sentry User Token
description: Detected a modern Sentry Auth Token, risking unauthorized access to crash reports, source code context, and environment variables.
helpText: A Sentry Auth Token allows programmatic access to an organization's error tracking platform. If leaked, attackers can pull highly sensitive crash reports. Because Sentry often captures the application state at the moment of a crash, attackers can extract hardcoded API keys, database passwords, user session tokens, and proprietary source code paths from the recorded stack traces.

title: Sentry Org Token
description: Detected a Sentry Org Token, risking administrative compromise of the entire error tracking workspace and source code integrations.
helpText: Sentry Org Token grants elevated administrative access across an entire Sentry instance. If leaked, an attacker can manage cross-project routing, delete entire project histories, alter source control integrations (like GitHub/GitLab bindings), and access highly sensitive crash reports containing hardcoded credentials and proprietary stack traces from all applications within the organization.

title: Sentry Access Token
description: Detected a legacy Sentry Auth Token, risking unauthorized access to sensitive application error logs and crash reports.
helpText: A Sentry Auth Token (Legacy) grant access to sensitive crash logs. To prevent your scanner from triggering on standard 64-character Git commit hashes (SHA-256), this regex forces the scanner to look for the word 'sentry' nearby.

title: Shippo API Token
description: Detected a Shippo API Token, risking unauthorized extraction of physical addresses, e-commerce PII, and fraudulent label purchasing.
helpText: A Shippo API Token grants programmatic control over a merchant's shipping and fulfillment operations. If leaked, an attacker can extract a massive trove of customer PII (including full names, phone numbers, and physical home addresses) or use your connected billing accounts to purchase fraudulent shipping labels.

title: SonarQube API Token
description: Detected a SonarQube API Token, risking exposure of proprietary source code and hidden security vulnerabilities.
helpText: A SonarQube API Token (Global: sqa_, Project: sqp_, User: squ_) grants access to a continuous inspection platform. If leaked, an attacker gets a complete roadmap to hacking your application. They can pull your proprietary source code and immediately view all unpatched security hotspots, zero-day vulnerabilities, and technical debt that your team has not yet fixed.

title: Sqreen API Token
description: Detected a legacy Sqreen API Token, risking unauthorized access to application security monitoring data and WAF configurations.
helpText: A Sqreen API Token grants programmatic access to the legacy Sqreen (now Datadog) application security platform. If leaked, an attacker could potentially modify Web Application Firewall (WAF) rules, disable Runtime Application Self-Protection (RASP) policies to blind your defenses, or extract sensitive security telemetry and attack logs to map out your infrastructure's vulnerabilities.

title: StackHawk API Key
description: Detected a StackHawk API Key, risking unauthorized access to DAST vulnerability reports and security pipeline configurations.
helpText: A StackHawk API Key grants programmatic access to the StackHawk Dynamic Application Security Testing (DAST) platform. If leaked, an attacker gains a literal roadmap to your application's unpatched weaknesses by reading your vulnerability reports, and they could potentially alter or disable your CI/CD security scans.

title: Stripe Webhook Secret
description: Detected a Stripe Webhook Secret, risking webhook forgery and bypassed payment verification logic.
helpText: A Stripe Webhook Secret is used to cryptographically verify that incoming events to your webhook endpoints actually originated from Stripe. If leaked, an attacker can forge malicious webhook payloads (such as fake 'checkout.session.completed' events) and send them directly to your server. This tricks your application into fulfilling orders, upgrading subscriptions, or granting digital goods without the attacker ever actually paying you.

title: SumoLogic Access ID
description: Detected a SumoLogic Access ID. While acting as a public identifier, exposure aids attackers in targeted API abuse when combined with an Access Token.
helpText: A SumoLogic Access ID acts as the username or public identifier for programmatic access to the SumoLogic API. On its own, it cannot be used to extract data, but hardcoding it is an anti-pattern. It is required alongside the Access Token to authenticate API requests.

title: SumoLogic Access Token
description: Detected a SumoLogic Access Token, risking unauthorized access to sensitive application logs, security telemetry, and SIEM data.
helpText: A SumoLogic Access Token is the master password for your log management and SIEM workspace. If leaked alongside an Access ID, an attacker can extract massive volumes of sensitive application logs. Because applications frequently log errors, stack traces, and user sessions, an attacker can often find hardcoded database passwords, PII, and session cookies buried inside the extracted telemetry.

title: Teradata VantageCloud Personal Access Token
description: Detected a Teradata VantageCloud Personal Access Token, risking unauthorized access to enterprise data warehouses and exposure of sensitive analytics data.
helpText: Teradata VantageCloud tokens grant programmatic access to enterprise data warehousing, compute clusters, and analytics services. A leaked token allows attackers to query, modify, or exfiltrate highly sensitive business data and manipulate cloud compute resources. Immediately revoke the exposed token in the Teradata VantageCloud console and provision a new one.

title: WePay Access Token
description: Detected a WePay Access Token, risking unauthorized financial transactions and exposure of merchant data.
helpText: WePay Access Token grant programmatic access to payment processing gateways. A leaked token allows attackers to initiate fraudulent transactions, issue unauthorized refunds, and view sensitive merchant financial data. Immediately revoke the exposed token in the WePay Developer Dashboard and provision a new one.

title: Code Climate Access Token
description: Detected a Code Climate token, risking unauthorized modification of test coverage metrics and exposure of code quality analytics.
helpText: Code Climate tokens grant programmatic access to submit test coverage and code quality data to a repository. A leaked token allows attackers to manipulate or poison code coverage metrics, potentially disrupting CI/CD quality gates and skewing analytics. Immediately revoke the exposed token in your Code Climate repository settings and configure a new one.

title: Docker Swarm Join Token
description: Detected a Docker Swarm join token, risking the addition of rogue nodes to the cluster and unauthorized access to container workloads.
helpText: Docker Swarm join tokens allow servers to attach to a cluster as either a worker or manager node. A leaked token enables attackers to introduce rogue nodes, intercept cluster traffic, or extract secrets. Immediately rotate the compromised join token by running `docker swarm join-token --rotate` on a manager node.

title: Docker Swarm Unlock Key
description: Identified a Docker Swarm unlock key, risking the unsealing of the cluster's Raft logs and exposure of all stored secrets.
helpText: Docker Swarm unlock keys decrypt the cluster's Raft logs, which store sensitive configuration data and secrets. If leaked, an attacker with access to the manager nodes can unseal the cluster and extract all environment variables, passwords, and certificates. Immediately rotate the unlock key by running `docker swarm unlock-key --rotate`.

title: EasyPost API Key
description: Detected an EasyPost API key, risking unauthorized shipping label purchases and exposure of customer logistics data.
helpText: EasyPost API keys grant programmatic access to shipping, tracking, and logistics services. A leaked production key allows attackers to purchase fraudulent shipping labels at your expense, access sensitive customer delivery addresses, and manipulate tracking data. Immediately revoke the compromised key in your EasyPost account dashboard and generate a replacement.

title: Microsoft Outlook/Teams Webhook
description: Detected a Microsoft Outlook/Teams incoming webhook URL, risking internal phishing campaigns and unauthorized channel messages.
helpText: Incoming webhooks allow unauthenticated HTTP POST requests to send messages directly into internal Microsoft Teams channels or Outlook groups. A leaked webhook enables attackers to masquerade as trusted internal systems, distribute malicious links, or conduct targeted social engineering (phishing) against your employees. Immediately delete the compromised webhook from the channel's Connectors configuration and generate a new one.

title: Plivo Auth Token
description: Detected a Plivo Auth Token, risking unauthorized telecom billing fraud and SMS/Voice abuse.
helpText: Plivo tokens grant programmatic control over telecom services. A leaked token allows attackers to execute premium-rate toll fraud, initiate SMS pumping schemes, and access call/message logs. Immediately revoke the exposed token in the Plivo console and rotate to a new one.

title: PubNub Secret Key
description: Detected a PubNub Secret Key, risking unauthorized real-time message interception and channel hijacking.
helpText: PubNub secret keys act as the master credential for real-time communication channels. If leaked, an attacker can bypass access controls to intercept private chat messages, inject malicious payloads, or hijack IoT device commands. Immediately revoke and regenerate the key in the PubNub Admin Portal.

title: Samsara API Key
description: Detected a Samsara API Key, risking unauthorized access to fleet management systems and exposure of telematics data.
helpText: Samsara API keys grant access to industrial IoT and fleet tracking environments. A leak exposes highly sensitive data, including real-time vehicle GPS locations, driver PII, and critical telematics. Immediately delete the compromised key in your Samsara dashboard settings.

title: Brevo (Sendinblue) API Key
description: Detected a Brevo API Key, risking mass-email abuse and exfiltration of customer contact lists.
helpText: Brevo (formerly Sendinblue) keys grant access to transactional email and marketing campaigns. A leaked key allows attackers to export sensitive customer email lists or launch phishing/spam campaigns that severely damage your domain's sender reputation. Immediately revoke the key in the Brevo 'SMTP & API' settings.

title: Stability AI API Key
description: Detected a Stability AI API key, risking unauthorized generative AI usage and rapid consumption of prepaid credits.
helpText: Stability AI keys grant access to Stable Diffusion models and generative APIs. A leaked key allows attackers to piggyback on your billing account, rapidly draining your prepaid generation credits. Immediately revoke the key in your Stability AI account portal.

title: Stream API Key
description: Detected a Stream API Key, risking unauthorized access to real-time chat infrastructure and activity feeds.
helpText: Stream API keys are used to identify your app to GetStream.io. While the secret key is required for full backend control, a leaked API key can still be abused to map your chat infrastructure. Rotate the key in your Stream Dashboard.

title: Stream API Secret
description: Detected a Stream API Secret, risking full compromise of chat channels and exposure of private user feeds.
helpText: Stream API Secrets grant full backend administrative control to GetStream.io. A leaked secret allows attackers to mint admin tokens, forge chat messages, read private channels, and hijack user activity feeds. Immediately revoke and rotate the secret in your Stream Dashboard.

title: Yahoo OAuth2 Client ID
description: Detected a Yahoo OAuth2 Client ID, risking application spoofing.
helpText: This Client ID identifies your application to Yahoo's OAuth2 servers. While not a secret on its own, it is often leaked alongside the Client Secret. Verify that the corresponding secret has not also been exposed.

title: Yahoo OAuth2 Client Secret
description: Detected a Yahoo OAuth2 Client Secret, risking authentication bypass and unauthorized access to Yahoo user accounts.
helpText: Yahoo OAuth2 Client Secrets authenticate your backend to Yahoo's API. A leaked secret allows an attacker to spoof your application, forge user authentication tokens, and potentially gain unauthorized access to users' Yahoo data. Immediately regenerate the secret in your Yahoo Developer Network console.

title: Zapier Webhook URL
description: Detected a Zapier Webhook URL, risking unauthorized automated workflow triggers and data pollution.
helpText: Zapier webhooks allow unauthenticated POST requests to trigger internal automated workflows. A leaked webhook enables attackers to spam your CRM, pollute spreadsheets, or trigger unwanted internal notifications. Delete the compromised webhook in your Zapier 'Catch Hook' trigger settings and generate a new one.

title: Zendesk API Token
description: Found a Zendesk API Token, risking unauthorized access to customer support tickets and exposure of PII.
helpText: Zendesk API tokens grant programmatic access to support infrastructure. A leaked token allows attackers to read sensitive customer communications, extract PII, and potentially hijack account recovery flows. Immediately delete the exposed token in the Zendesk Admin Center under 'Zendesk API'.

title: Zillow API Key
description: Detected a Zillow API Key (ZWSID), risking unauthorized real estate data scraping and quota exhaustion.
helpText: Zillow Web Service IDs (ZWSID) grant access to property valuation and real estate APIs. A leak primarily poses a risk of quota exhaustion and service denial. Regenerate the API key via your Zillow developer dashboard.

title: Zoom JWT Token
description: Detected a Zoom JWT Token, risking unauthorized meeting hijacking and exposure of cloud recordings.
helpText: Zoom JWT Tokens grant programmatic control over Zoom accounts. Although Zoom formally deprecated JWT apps in late 2023, active legacy tokens still allow attackers to extract recording URLs, delete scheduled meetings, or hijack calls. Revoke the token in the Zoom App Marketplace and migrate to Server-to-Server OAuth.

title: Discord Bot Token
description: Detected a Discord Bot Token, risking total server compromise, mass user bans, and channel deletion.
helpText: Discord bot tokens grant programmatic administrative control over the bot's actions. A leaked token allows attackers to hijack the bot to mass-ban users, delete channels, exfiltrate chat logs, or distribute phishing links to your community. Immediately regenerate the token in the Discord Developer Portal under the 'Bot' tab.

title: Discord Webhook URL
description: Detected a Discord Webhook URL, risking targeted phishing campaigns and unauthorized channel spam.
helpText: Discord webhooks allow unauthenticated POST requests to drop messages directly into specific channels. A leaked webhook enables attackers to masquerade as trusted server integrations to distribute malware, post malicious links, or spam the community. Delete the compromised webhook immediately in the Discord Server Settings under 'Integrations'.

title: Facebook Page Access Token
description: Detected a Facebook Page Access Token, risking unauthorized brand impersonation and page manipulation.
helpText: Facebook Page Access Tokens grant programmatic control over specific Facebook Pages and ad campaigns. A leaked token allows attackers to publish posts as your brand, read hidden page content, modify admin settings, or run fraudulent ads. Immediately revoke the token in the Meta App Dashboard and review recent page activity.

title: Facebook App Access Token
description: Detected a Facebook App Access Token, risking unauthorized app-level Graph API calls and application spoofing.
helpText: Facebook App Access Tokens consist of your App ID and App Secret. If leaked, attackers can modify app settings, impersonate your application, and access aggregated app analytics. Because this token directly exposes your App Secret, you must immediately reset the App Secret in the Meta App Dashboard under Basic Settings.

title: Facebook App Secret
description: Detected a Facebook App Secret, risking application spoofing and unauthorized access token generation.
helpText: The Facebook App Secret is the master key for your Meta App. A leaked secret allows attackers to verify forged payloads, generate rogue App Access Tokens, and completely hijack your application's Graph API integrations. Instantly reset the App Secret in the Meta App Dashboard under Basic Settings.

title: Open Exchange Rates Token
description: Detected an Open Exchange Rates Token, risking unauthorized API usage and billing quota exhaustion.
helpText: Grants programmatic access to currency conversion and exchange rate APIs. While generally low risk, a leak can lead to rapid quota exhaustion and service denial. Regenerate the App ID in your Open Exchange Rates dashboard.

title: OpenVSX Access Token
description: Detected an OpenVSX Access Token, risking unauthorized publishing or management of OpenVSX extensions.
helpText: An OpenVSX Access Token grants access to publish and manage extensions in the OpenVSX Registry. If leaked, an attacker could publish malicious extension versions, alter extension metadata, or disrupt release workflows. Revoke the token in OpenVSX and issue a replacement.

title: Picatic API Key
description: Detected a legacy Picatic API Key, risking unauthorized access to event management data.
helpText: Picatic was acquired by Eventbrite, but legacy keys may still exist. Ensure this integration is migrated to modern Eventbrite APIs.

title: Riot Games API Key
description: Identified a Riot Games API Key, risking immediate developer account suspension and API blocklisting.
helpText: Grants access to Riot Games player statistics and match data. Riot aggressively monitors GitHub for these leaks; if exposed, your key will likely be automatically revoked and your developer account flagged. Regenerate the key in the Riot Developer Portal.

title: Salesforce OAuth Consumer Key
description: Detected a Salesforce OAuth Consumer Key, which identifies a Salesforce Connected App and may expose integration details.
helpText: A Salesforce OAuth Consumer Key is a public client identifier for a Connected App. It does not grant access by itself, but if exposed alongside the matching Consumer Secret it can help an attacker impersonate the application in OAuth flows. Review the Connected App configuration and rotate the paired secret if it may also be exposed.

title: Salesforce OAuth Consumer Secret
description: Detected a Salesforce OAuth Secret, risking CRM authentication bypass.
helpText: Used to authenticate Salesforce Connected Apps. Exposing this allows application spoofing to extract CRM data.

title: Ubidots API Token
description: Found a Ubidots API Token, risking unauthorized access to IoT dashboards and sensor telemetry.
helpText: An Ubidots API Token grants read/write access to Ubidots IoT deployments. A leaked token allows attackers to extract private sensor telemetry or inject malicious data points to disrupt dashboard analytics. Revoke and rotate the token in your Ubidots API Credentials settings.

title: WeChat App Secret
description: Identified a WeChat App Secret, risking miniprogram spoofing and user authentication bypass.
helpText: Used for WeChat Official Account and Miniprogram backend integrations. Exposing this allows an attacker to hijack OAuth authentication flows, forge user sessions, and spoof your miniprogram. Instantly reset the App Secret in the WeChat Official Accounts Platform.

title: Yandex Predictor API Key
description: Detected a Yandex Predictor API Key, risking service quota exhaustion.
helpText: Grants access to Yandex prediction and text translation services. While it does not expose user PII, a leak can quickly exhaust your API limits and disrupt services relying on it. Regenerate the key in the Yandex Developer Dashboard.

title: Datadog Client Token
description: Detected a Datadog Client Token, typically used for Real User Monitoring (RUM) and browser logs.
helpText: A Datadog Client Token is designed to be embedded in front-end client code (like React or mobile apps) to send telemetry data to Datadog. While it cannot be used to query or extract sensitive data, its exposure could allow an attacker to send unauthorized or fabricated data, potentially polluting your analytics or impacting billing.

title: Datadog Personal Access Token
description: Detected a Datadog Personal Access Token granting scoped API access.
helpText: Datadog Personal Access Tokens provide specific, highly privileged access to the Datadog API. Personal Access Tokens function standalone and do not require a paired API key. A leaked token can be used immediately by an attacker to interact with the API based on the token's granted scopes.

title: Datadog API Key
description: Detected a standard 32-character Datadog API Key, used for submitting telemetry data.
helpText: Datadog API Keys are organization-wide keys primarily used by the Datadog Agent to write telemetry data (metrics, logs, traces) to your account. If leaked, an attacker could flood your environment with fake data, mask an ongoing attack by overwhelming monitoring systems, or drive up ingestion costs.

title: Datadog Application Key
description: Detected a Datadog Application Key (either the legacy 40-character hex string or the modern 'ddap_' prefixed format), which grants programmatic API management access.
helpText: Datadog Application Keys are tied to specific user accounts and give programmatic read/write access to the Datadog API when paired with an API key. Exposure can lead to an attacker reading sensitive infrastructure logs, exfiltrating PII, or deleting security alerts to completely blind operations during a breach.
